content: This is one of the best apps I've come across. It's certainly the best one for tracking workouts. You can pick pre-loaded exercises or create your own. You can create your own workout routines. It then tracks weight, reps, and/or time. The free version is essentially the same as the Pro version. The main difference is that Free allows you to create and track three or four routines, whereas for $30 a year, you can have unlimited routines. Either way, it awesome. Highly recommended.

I use the monthly paid version ($2.99 a month), and it's exactly what I needed in a workout tracking app. Plenty of pre-loaded exercises with animations and how-to descriptions if you need a reminder, plus the ability to create custom exercises. I also enjoy being able to take a workout plan from the app and edit it as needed. Support is also responsive and timely!

Really great app. The free version has literally everything I need to track my workouts. I love how easy it is to use. I can swap exercises in my program with a couple taps. I love the exercise library and pre-con programs. There's also a plate math calculator!! And you can customize it!! No ads, no bloat, just a great tool. The social aspect is an added bonus! Don't sleep on this app.

I love this app for tracking my workouts! It's so easy to use and customize. I love that it syncs with Strava, too, since the cardio tracking isn't quite as good as the weightlifting tracking. It's super fun receiving medals for progress! It provides helpful info on correct form and has pretty much every exercise you could want. If you have a lot of different routines, you'd need to upgrade, but I'm perfectly fine with the 4 free ones.

Absolutely love this app. I love that it includes animations about how to do specific workouts, and I love all the growth tracking features. I feel like I've made more progress in the last two months with this app than I have in years without it! There is also a certain "game" element that I enjoy, in that I am constantly trying to beat my own records. It also contains tons of premade exercise routines. I never pay for apps, but this one was worth it.

it is mostly easy to use; there is a comprehensive collection of exercises and building a routine is intuitive. there is even an option to create an exercise if the app doesn't contain it. however, users can only build a 4 day routine for free, any more is behind a pay wall. greed, plain and simple. it could be the best, but the devs are more concerned with profit over quality. still, it's better than most. barely.

I would like a larger variety of exercises that don't require equipment as much. I only have a bench and some adjustable weights. So I feel like my overall options are limited much like the space I have to work out. so far for having the app for a week I do find it convenient and easy to use and they don't try to price gouge you every month with barely anything to offer. 🧐👍

Great app, but needs more exercises and content: different grip handles for machines, exercise variations, rest-pause logging, maybe even logging partial reps, etc. It also needs to allow you to save past notes you submitted for a previous exercise without having to retype them (it shows a preview only)! Unfortunately I will probably have to move on to another app that has more content near the end of my subscription. Hevy is great, but it needs some love.

This app is great! it has everything you need to track a workout. logging exercises is easy, reviewing a graph of your past weight on each exercise is available to track progress. I use it for two separate routines at different gyms where the available weight machines are slightly different and I have to adjust the weight - Hevy reminds me of the previous weight I lifted on that routine which makes it easy for me. And, all that is on the free version!

I recommend to everyone I meet! This is user friendly and helps you with exercises if you don't know how to do them. I love you can add in your own exercises or equipment that is not in the app already. I barely tried the free trial and loved it, so I signed up for a year, but after that I may upgrade to lifetime! I love all the real life weight comparisons!

I love everything about this app. It gives you data about what you do, never its opinion about whether you've gone too hard or not hard enough. It tracks your lifts over time, and celebrates your PRs. Thoughtful features include rest timers, a thorough list of lifts with written and video instructions for each one, and very minimal social feed. I show this app to all my gym buddies, because it's so useful without any unnecessary bells and whistles.

Great app for tracking your workouts and inexpensive compared to other apps (You pay the same amount for lifetime access as you would for a year of other apps). Granted, this app does not create workouts for you... but, in my mind, ID rather go to a free AI product and create a workout there and pay less here than pay a stupid amount of money to have the apps AI make something up. Plus, this way I have a lot more control over the final workout. Definitely worth a download to check it out.

Great App for tracking routines and workouts especially when it comes to progressive overload. My only complaint would be content wise. Lifting the same truck gets boring. Maybe an Oak Tree, 4,000 Large Pumpkins, or 100 Sofas. That way, it's more of a game as to what you unlocked that day. Otherwise, a very solid app. I've lowered my rating by one star because I've experienced a few bugs and there's no way to catalog an entire workout program.

I like that you can copy workouts. Modification is easy. I like being able to take notes and trending. My biggest issue is that something changed in the last month so that when I change a weight it will not let me complete the rep unless I log out and log back in. It makes logging more time consuming in real time. I use the free version so I just suck it up.

Before I downloaded Hevy I found it so difficult to actually track my progress. This app organizes it so well and makes each is so simple to track your progress in individual exercises, muscle groups, etc. And building your own workouts as a beginner is also incredibly easy as it will filter exercises for your needs to help you get started. I've been using it for about 2 years now and have put on significantly more muscle since starting the app.

Absolutely love this app, it works great in helping you track your progress. The only true issue that I have with this app is that I copy my previous workouts for routine and not always do I complete each exercise due to time but keep them in for the next week.. that being said even if those exercises are not checked off per set, the app is still tracking those towards my volume lifted at the end thus not truly accurate in thw end. Would love to see this updated.

I have the payed version, I like the selection of equipment it matches my gym pretty good, the trainer work really good, rotates exercises and equipment so it doesn't get stale and also gi e you replacement options if there's something you don't like or if a piece of equipment isn't available, seeing the past logs and set ratings helps alot, only thing i don't like is it doesn't have some of the past history if I switch routines.

Love it. Setup routines ahead of time. Track reps, weights and sets as you go. Notes are visible right where you want them. The data from the previous workout is right on the screen so you can modify if needed. Rearrange or modify the routine on the fly if a station is not available or a different hit is wanted. Keep changes or discard them after. Very large exercise library as well the ability to add custom exercises. This is the perfect tool to use in the gym and when planning at home.

I absolutely love this app. It's given me a way to keep track of my lifts, build out plans, doesn't constantly ask me for money, and the free version does everything you need it to. Can take notes about my workouts, can add in modifiers, can even make your own excersizes if you know how to import the right files. Wouldn't have the progress I've made so far without it. Easy win dog.

I've used the free version of this app, for about 4yrs now. It has a large base of exercises and workouts. Options range from Free weights, machine weights, kettlebells, resistance, body weight, and cardio. All options have a "How to" for reference. It allows you to track workout time, Reps, Sets, weight, failure/warmup, supersets, has a section for notes, and you can save/view all prior workouts! I LOVE IT and I would highly recommend!!

I used to rate this app at 5 stars, but now I'm giving it a 1 because of multiple bugs or other issues. I filed a support request but just received an AI-generated email. I often can't save workouts or even log exercises. Usually the app doesn't tell me why: it just silently fails to register the button press. Other times their servers seem to be down. When I save a workout, I might see a spinner for a long time.

I've used a variety of workout tracking apps, and this is the best one I've used. I've been using this app exclusively to track my workouts for over a year now. It's worth paying for the upgrade a you can use to customize and build multiple routines. It keeps track of all of my workouts for every type of exercise and allows me to track the weight, sets, reps, and volume since I began using the app. I really like how customizable and easy it is to adjust my plans.

I absolutely recommend this app, makes tracking my daily workouts super easy, you can see your progress easily, see stats, get monthly reports,... and no ads, no horrible paywalls for premium version (there is a premium version for this app, but the standard version is very comprehensive and super easy to use that it doesn't feel like you're missing out). will keep using this app, and keep telling all my friends about it.

Had the app for 3 weeks and I'm very impressed with it. It's what I thought I would get with Zepp and the new Fitbit interface, but they failed miserably. Heavy allowed me to build my own routines for each day and then it auto fills your previous weights and reps each exercise so you aren't having to keep a log in a notebook. That allows me to push my limits and get stronger and grow my muscles. Highly recommended!

This is the best workout app by far. It's like Instagram but it doesn't rot your brain. Not only is it easy to use, accurate, and free; it also encourages you to keep going to the gym by tracking your workout streak. The app also offers short gifs showing proper workout form and the muscle groups beind worked in a given exercise. It even shows you tips on proper form, and allows you to leave notes for yourself. I personally use it to remind myself to focus on my form.

App is overall great and easy to use. being able to track my workouts and see what muscles are being targeted, helps keep me motivated. My only criticism, is that I wish they included more workout options. For example, ball ball or palates bar kit exercises. This app tends to be geared more towards those would have gym equipment or actually go to a gym. Still, it's an overall good app.

I'm a week/ 3 workouts into the free version of Hevy. The best part for me so far is the motivation of seeing other people showing up! If they can do it, I can too... it's fairly intuitive, I was able to set up my first weight lifting workout without a problem, and u can log other things like walking, yoga, skiing... I'm very happy so far. editing to add I've made it to 50 workouts and paid for a lifetime subscription! nothing but great things to say about Hevy app

This is the app I always thought should exist, but I had no idea it would be better than my imaginary app. Truly, easy to use, easy to track my workouts, easy to see my schedule, easy for me to show off to my friends. I still have the free version, but I may upgrade soon. The free version is great, but there are a few more features in the paid version. Definitely recommend.

really love the app and the wide variety of exercises to choose from when creating my workouts. the one thing that would move this to a 5 star would be the ability to have more than three workouts saved. it's not a huge deal but I'd love to be able to see older programs and know what I did so I can modify new programs. aside from that it's a great app

lots of exercises to choose from or create your own. let's you create up to 4 individual workouts for free version. being able to add notes to the exercise, set an automatic timer. and reflect at the end of your workout are great features. If you are new to workouts, use the reflect feature and be honest about your struggles, then come back to them after a few weeks of working out and compare yourself to that former person.

Honestly this was the secret sauce to seeing real progress in the gym as I use the app to record every weight and rep and am able to increase it every or every other week by 2.5-5lbs. On top of it you're also able to organize and create entire workout routines for yourself. Would definitely recommend, even better if you have other gym buddies so that you can compete a bit with each other using its social media features

I used to track all my workouts using an actual physical journal. one of the influencers that I follow on Instagram recommended hevy I made the switch and I'll never go back. the free version allows you to create four routines that you can utilize and if you pay for the subscription you can use even more it's worth every penny but the free version is great for beginners or intermediate lifters.

worth trying if you need a free app. will allow up to 4 routines and pro is unlimited. 3 months of data stored for free and unlimited for pro. tbh, simple, intuitive and allows synchronization of health connect and other apps that track health. definitely enjoying the free and most likely will purchase the lifetime option for the pro, but like I said free is pretty awesome too. btw, speaking as a beginner lifter

I use this app to track my workouts on my functional trainer with a barbell. It has access to many different exercises. It's just good to keep track of what you've done, what your personal best is, and just keep track of working out. It's a nice app, very user friendly, and has some videos that show the actual exercise, so that's pretty cool.

I really like this app, I started working out with a group of friends and this app lets us all participate and be present for each other in our workouts. It's great for finding exercises based on muscle group as well. I think this app will be a great help as I am starting to workout by myself, would recommend this to anyone in similar situations

Having a plan going into the gym is important for me. This app helps me build that plan, and review past performance, in order to adjust and make improvements. Super easy to use and helps keep me on track and focused. I would love for the free version to allow more than 4 preset routines to be built by users, but with the "open workout" option, you can change it up when you need to from your primary setup. I like seeing the data it provides and sharing functionality.

Great app that can track a full routine for free. No ads so far, and the subscription is inexpensive if you want to track more then one routine. Adding workouts to the routine is simple and they have have a good selection of routines for everything from beginner to advanced workouts. Each workout also shows a simplified gif and explanation of how to do the workout.

The best tracker app for real lifters. It's clean, ad-free design is simple and easy to use. I love the Routines feature, where you can create custom workouts with detailed sets, supersets, rest timers, and everything else. The database contains every exercise you can think of, and the insights after each workout are fun and rewarding. I recommend this app for anyone who wants to cut out the fluff in their lifting and wants to see real progress.

An absolutely amazing app! I have been using it since 2024. The free version is largely enough for most people and does not contain any ads. The pro version unlocks the possibility to have more than 4 routines and few additional metrics. Hevy is the perfect way to learn movements, craft routines, track progress, gamify workouts, stay motivated, exchange programs, share workouts, and cheer people on. Its unique social media function is excellent, but can be turned off if you only want a gym app.

I have been using the Hevy app for over a year to track my progress lifting weights. I find it very helpful with planning routines, selecting equipment and exercises, as well as daily tracking completion. It helps me remember my routines and keeps track of previous accomplishments. The reports and statistic are also useful to evaluate progress.

I am a beginner weightlifter but knowledgeable of the weights. just haven't weightlifted in a while. I had used a couple of other apps but they were designed more for Sirius bro type weightlifters. when I found Hevy, it provided me with the platform to track my workouts and easily swap out exercises. I'm using the free app and it's truly incredible, but I'm thinking about upgrading in the next month or so. from a technical perspective, no glitches, no issues with the app! Love it

I really like using this app for my weight focused workouts. It keeps track of all my stats and the little how to videos for each exercise are great for when I need a refresher. The suggestions for similar exercises is also very helpful. I like how the app tells you what your workout volume equates to in a real life object like a helicopter or elephant!

I've been using this app on my phone and watch for a few weeks. its a good app with decent user interface. were it falls short for me is: A) widgets on phone. my homescreen is 5 tiles across and their widget is 4, with no option to adjust length. B) on watch after I've started a workout, to allow you to see what you done the last two sessions on each exercise. c) to add suggested weight increases based on previous workout on each exercise, viewable on the workout itself after you've started.

I've used a lot of fitness and tracking apps, they have ranged from bad to ok. Some were missing features I needed, some had features I didn't want etc. Then I found my Goldilocks app. HEVY found a way to make the process easy. Logging is simple, you can create routines, add exercises by selecting from an extensive list, or creating your own. It keeps a complete history and is easy to use. I even got their paid subscription just because I want them to succeed. I do like the added functionality.

Great app. The free version is good enough for most people, supporting up to 3 workout routine rotations. I decided to upgrade to the paid version to have the flexibility of having more routines. I love the tracking, the timing between sets, ability to do supersets (no breaks between 2 or more workouts) and the tracking of your best overall lifts. This app helped me gain 10 lb of muscle in 8 weeks. Current total gain is 15 lbs of muscle. Goal is 30 lb 💪

Love the app! A few critiques: it's a massive battery drain on my pixel watch 3. Distance related exercises (like walking lunge), allow you to select increments of 5 yards on the phone, but increments of 10 yards on the watch, so even if I have it set to 15 yards on my phone, if I finish a set on my watch, it automatically changes it to 20 yards. Seems to completely lose connection to my watch if it enters battery saving mode. Love the integration with watches but improvements can be made!

after using the free version for a couple of months I moved on to the Pro version and updated my review from 4 to 5 stars. for a few bucks a month, it's a no brainer. the app works very well and syncs seamlessly with my watch during workouts. I track 3 90-minute sessions a week with 32 sets over 8 exercises each session, mainly all with my watch. love the stats and reports as well. haven't found anything major to complain about.

Super simple and straight forward. I just wanted a way to track my workouts like a spreadsheet but needed the spreadsheet to be on steroids, and this is exactly that. I barely found out today that the app also has a way to track not only your weight but measurements of multiple body parts and appendages, like my neck, my back, my... you get the point.. it's super dope. I bought the lifetime membership. 100% worth it cause I can have all of my routines and variations of them stored in there.

The app is really good, makes updating and tracking your progress workout to workout easy. My only complaint is that I wish their was a way to create a routine from your current workout, currently you'd have to remember your weight and rep ranges, plot them all down in the tracker, and save it. would be very convenient to just have an option added to the drop-down menu during your current workout, other than that though Hevy is great.

Super easy to use and very customizable. I'm coming back after logging my 25th workout! I really enjoy using this app! after trying a few options, this one stuck. I love the celebration whenever you beat your PR and how it breaks down what muscle groups your workout focused on. It is so nice having all my rep counts and weights automatically saved from my last workout, and the short "how-to's" at the top of workouts. Great app for beginners or anyone trying to more effectively workout.

This app is great. I was using the bodybuilding app and when they updated it last year, it completely ruined it and I was on the hunt for a better app. This app is the best one I've ever used. It tracks your progression, personal bests, best volume, literally anything. It even adds up the total weight lifted and will tell you if you have lifted a truck, dinosaur, yacht, and more. One thing I would love to see in the future is badges or trophies or anything like that.

This is exactly what I've been looking for, a good app for tracking workouts without all the videos, preplanned workouts, and fluff. I don't even know how I would improve it aside from it missing a couple exercises (but you can just input it manually). The free version is great, but it's amazing that it can be bought for a one-time fee of $75. I was planning on using the free one for a while just to check before buying it, but ended up buying it after a day or so.

I switched from Strong to Hevy at the behest of a friend who wanted to see my workouts and it's been a real upgrade! I even upgraded to the paid version when they had an end of year discount and haven't regretted it. I'm a real sucker for data visualization and it's been cool to see myself improving over time in graph form. Have only used it for a few months but so far it's been fantastic!

Really great workout app. A wide selection of exercises even if you don't have that much equipment at home, you can easily find some exercises for every muscle group. I believe the app recently improved supersets workouts. I love the scroll to the next exercise feature, especially useful with supersets,so I don't have to wonder around what is next.

I don't usually like subscription models, this one feels like it is a fair ask per month!! things like the rest timer automatically activating when I finish a set, the ability to really quickly throw up custom exercises, and the instructional videos on each exercise so that I can get a brief refresher on form. if it's been a while on an exercise, make it a really good value. value. I think I've used almost all of the main trackers, this is the best. thanks for streamlining the morning!

I really like this app. I use the free version for about 30 days before I decided I wanted to upgrade to Pro. I think the free version would be great for someone who only works out at one gym, but I use multiple gems so this allowed me to create different routines for each gym and Unlimited Custom exercises. you have to dig into the settings a little bit to get your rest timer working properly, and just to get things the way you like them to be as far as reading your effort and things like that.

It has been very useful for me. In one app I can keep track on my previous exercises, weight, and reps. Additionally, it keeps track of the muscles worked so I can focus on the ones that need more attention. I upgraded to pay account so I can create more than 3 workouts. I like that it was a one-time payment, very affordable. Forget the montjly fees.

I don't write reviews for anything, except Hevy. It's the only gym tracker I've stuck with. It's easy to use, definitely worth the one time purchase for the additional workouts and custom exercises. My favorite is at the end of a workout seeing exactly how much I've lifted and what that compares to (like a T-Rex!) Thank you for making an app for the gym that isn't bloated or gamified, just gains! ❤️

great app for tracking workouts, my strength and weights have been going up steadily from keeping track of things with it. I am a beginner, but it seems hevy has all the essential functions for beginners and experts alike. I also like the friends aspect of it, I didn't think I would care about it but now a few friends of mine use the app and it's so fun to see everyone's workouts.

As someone who has always been inconsistent in the gym, Hevy helps me keep track of what my numbers are for different exercises which makes getting back into the grind much easier. Not to mention, you can look up a machine or exercise name that may seem confusing and it'll give you an animation for how it's supposed to be performed.

I'll start by saying I really like this app and recommend it to all my friends and students. But I'm starting to get irked with the lack of integration with Garmin because I can't track my heart rate otherwise. So then I have to have 2 trackers running at once and monitor both. I'm also annoyed that exercises like Kettlebell Swings aren't accounted for in the muscle group breakdown. I feel like that's an easy fix. You give primary and secondary muscles for everything else. Why not these?

I've never liked a workout app before, but this is incredible. It keeps me focused in workouts, and gives me all the data I need to make decisions about my workouts. It is incredible that the app is so intuitive while giving me so much information. Also, I love the workout summaries that tell me "this workout was like lifting a truck".

Great app. One small problem. I love this app. It makes tracking my workouts super easy and it plays well with my other fitness apps, however, the WearOS version drains my watch so fast. I don't know if it's tracking more than the Fitbit app does, but I can track a whole hour and a half workout with the Fitbit app, and still have enough battery to last the rest of the day, but with this app tracking my workouts, I'll get down to 15% and have to charge again before I go to work.

Hevy has completely changed the way I hit the gym. It keeps detailed track of all my workouts, including sets, reps, and weight, without having to carry a pen and pad anymore. One of my favorite features is how it automatically updates when I increase weight, so my workouts stay accurate over time and I can clearly see my progress. It’s simple, clean, and helps me stay focused instead of messing around with pen and paper.

solid workout tracker. you can use an AI plug in to work out a progression routine. overall just like my old notebook and pen. except much easier to read. for just wanting to track your weights reps and volume can't beat this. I like this app so much I paid for pro Even though the free version is completely functional for all my use cases. keep up the good work!

Update 2: What happened to being able to type in the time on timed sets? I hate the scrolling timer, it's way too slow and disturbs my workout flow. Update 1: Great tracker app! Has the most subdivision of muscle groups for tracking volume, custom bar and machine weights, body weight included, progress tracking, and great organization of routines! I also like being able to share my routines with others. Devs are responsive and fixed a UI issue in a reasonable time.

I have been looking for an app that would track my workouts its 3asy modifiable and where I could track new challenges for myself. Since using this app I feel.excited for my new workout every week. I tend to change it up weekly which keeps me entertained and avoids a plateau and feeling like I'm repeating the same thing over and over. Highly recommend if you're are looking to track your workouts, come up with new things to do or simply keep track of your PRs.

A great tool for the gym. Without Hevy, I was relying on memory to track numbers of sets/reps/weight, and lacking real data for improvements. This app is easy to use and highly motivating for me, being able to track new records for volume or weights. Easy to setup workouts and super simple to track progress as I go. Love it!

the users interface is great, has tons of exercises, and the statistics it gives at the end of each workout is both fun to see and encouraging for future workouts. My only gripe is that there are no stretches that you can add to workouts, and you get a limited number of "create your own exercise" so you cant just add them all in if you want to. I like how the reps and sets carry over to the next session so you can see how you performed and improve upon it. overall great app.

I love this app. The free version has plenty of options for me (just starting out) and its been so helpful with keeping me motivated. I recommend this app to everyone I know who is going to the gym. The only issue I've had is my settings not saving for my workouts. It honestly might be user error since I'm still finding my way around the app. I was able to download and use immediately with the built in workouts and that has been instrumental for me.

I've used dozens of fitness apps over the years - and Hevy is far and away the best there is. The interface is very mobile friendly, the access to demo videos is a great feature - but my favorite is the memory that displays your last workout. I cannot imagine even one additional feature they could include to make it better.

an amazing app for workouts. provides visual examples of the workouts for proper form, and allows for some different forms of excercise like failure sets as well as notes so I can remember alterations I did in a previous week. it also connects to my watch and keeps track of my heart rate. sharing my workout with a workout group im in also helps clarify what we are working on that day! its also very good for what is offered for free!

Honestly so easy to use and intuitive. I feel excited to get back into lifting as this takes a lot of the mental effort out of it lol. Love looking at the little body map to see what muscle groups I should work next for balance. I would love if you could change the sizes of the widgets, finish workouts without entering sets/reps, and if there were pelvic floor/womens health exercises added as well as niche things like foot strength, finger strength. Overall really great!

Solid app. Free version is good it's just 3 months of workout history vice all-time. If you decide you want to upgrade to the paid version 6 months in, all of your workout history will be there, not just the previous 3 months. Large library of exercises to pick from and even the option to create an exercise. I've used Hevy for 3 years now and won't be leaving.

Love the Hevy app! It’s super easy to use, tracks your previous weights, and gives you those fun alert medals when you lift your heaviest or hit your longest hike which is super motivating. I also love that I can create workouts for friends and family to follow on their own. Makes training more fun, more social, and way easier to stay consistent. Highly recommend!

great app through and through. best for tracking progress, exercises (rep volumes etc), weights etc. it also shows / and can filter by muscle group. all exercises have a how to and shine even have a video to show. I got the lifetime subscription about 2 years ago, and it's been great since. I haven't personally experienced any bugs.

First review I've ever written for any app. This app is incredible. Well thought out. Perfect for tracking workout progress. No annoying in-app ads for free version- (which actually makes me more inclined to consider purchasing the "Pro" mode to unlock full features). Glitch-free. Very user friendly. This is exactly the app I was looking for and has become an essential tool in my workout program. Zero cons to this app. Great job to the team at Hevy. Keep up the excellent work.

Love this app for tracking my workouts. It has everything I need all in one place. Not only can I track my workouts, but I can also send them to others when planning a group workout. The sample workouts are great, as are the animated tutorials for newbies like me. I will definitely recommend this to others.

This is the best workout app I've used, and it beats my spreadsheets. I can track my workouts well with it and it gamifies it by giving me a "total weight lifted" for the workout and for the workouts that week. This just makes me feel like a rockstar, and pushes me to work harder. Wrapping up an arm day that lifted 3.5 tons makes me feel like a monster. I'm currently using the free version, and that is working well for me, but will probably go to paid when my next workout program starts.

The Hevy app is nice! It is a great app for logging and tracking your workouts. I use the free version and it's good. I would like more options for bodyweight / calisthenics / resistance bands options for tracking. If you have that feeling of needing to report or log workouts to see your progress then definitely give the Hevy app a try.

this is my go-to app for tracking all of my workouts. It keeps my rest periods consistent and I can implement progressive overload because it always shows how much I lift it the time before. for anyone. just getting started or who is currently a lifter. this is a great app to track your workouts. if only they add a circuit or tabata timer, then we'll be in business.

Really like the app, the tracking and progression monitoring has been game changing. I have recommended it to many ppl. While the exercise library is extensive, its missing some common movements. However, you can add a customs that get added to ur personal library. the cons are minimal but mainly consist around random ui errors, like closing out an edited workout session and only getting half of the option to update or keep appears on screen or a formatting limitations when adding custom img.

I love this app. I'm new to the gym and it's really helped me build a consistent routine & track the metrics that matter to me so I can see my progress every time, & adjust sets reps and exercises on the fly. I also value the small animations of each exercise in case I have form questions & the muscle heat map to show what I've worked in each set and over the course of the workout and week. The comparison of my total weight lifted to a heavy object like a truck or a yacht is awesome!! LOVE IT!

Great app for tracking your workouts, don't bother installing the companion app on your watch though because the watch version is straight trash clearly programmed by a 3 year old. Not only that, if you're trying to track your workouts using Samsung Health on your watch the Hevy app forces it to stop tracking randomly because it's (Hevy is) such a piece of junk.

I really enjoy using this app. The only downside is that you can only create so many custom exercises for free. Since I do a lot of odd workouts that I find very effective, I chose to do the yearly bill, which actually isn't expensive. It would be better if that was included in the free plan, but otherwise, I can't complain.

so much is offered on the free version it's unbelievable, I will willingly pay for it because they deserve it for being so generous. today I discovered an option to compare your stats to another member so you can start competing with them which seems awesome. I love the community in this app. it reminds me a bit of fitocracy (who remembers that?) This is definitely the best of all the logging apps I've downloaded. maybe in the future we can also use it to track nutrition?

Excellent for strength tracking, better than anything I can imagine a paid app would offer. And it even has a free, perfectly set up Android Wear app too, which makes this already convenient app even better. I'm a bit of a novice, so being able to filter and see basic form guides for exercises helps me out also, although generally you only need to use them once. None of the paid features matter to me and unfortunately it's subscription only, otherwise I'd consider paying just to say thanks!

not perfect. it doesn't have every possible exercise or every variation, but it does allow you to create exercises, and though the creation method isn't %100 comprehensive, it works with most exercises. also, it won't allow you to put more than two exercises in a single superset, which is slightly annoying, but doesn't really affect the results. overall not bad, and helps track improvement. edit-just found that I can only save 4 workouts and create 7 exercises until hitting paywall. down 1 star.

I just found this app last week but it's so handy and I love it! It's absolutely worth the $2.99 a month to be able to add whatever custom exercises I want and make however many routines I want it's so user friendly and easy to track progress on. I absolutely adore it and couldn't recommend it more!

I like the app and the variety of exercises it can record, but it has a huge problem with syncing with healthy connect. I use Samsung Health, and sometimes the workout I did on hevy will not be recorded in Samsung Health negativity affecting my energy score, even with multiple attempts to troubleshoot the issue. Hevy plz fix the sync issue. I would give it a 5 star if it didn't have this problem

I've used Heavy since day one of my fitness journey and never looked back. The core experience is completely free,log unlimited workouts forever, with automatic lift and progression tracking for the last 3 months. No paywalls on what matters most. Want more? The paid subscription unlocks full lifetime statistics and additional custom exercises and routines. Great extras but totally optional. The free version is genuinely powerful,not a crippled demo. Heavy gives you the tools to track properly.

excellent! I changed to hevy after using other apps that were so full of junk they could hardly run, and often didn't even save your progress. HEVY is simple and extremely easy to use, and has every feature I need. Some People say they wish it had more set routines available to chose from, which would be nice but that's also one of the main reasons other apps are so slow ,so I disagree. I just copied my routine from my original app, took 10 minutes as it's very simple to create your own routine.

I'm using the free version and it has no ads at all, BLESS WHOEVER MADE THIS APP!! It has been very helpful in reminding me the rest times and how many more sets i have yet to do. Plus, almost all kinds of workouts are in here! So, whether you have equipment or not, there's a high chance it's in this app, so i definitely love using thiss!

I'm two weeks into using this app, and I absolutely love it. With a bit of effort I've been able to fully upload the weight resistance program I purchased and sort all the various routines (Bro split, PPL, and full body). The ability to crop videos to reference when creating custom exercises is super convenient. The timer works great, and being able to edit the routine or add exercises mid-lift is super intuitive and easy. Love the social aspect of it too. Super impressed.

Hi I'm by no means a gym rat , however I've had great experience with this app. The pre prepared workouts are great and I found building my own routine to be very helpful. All exercises are demonstrated with graphics and text instructions. The little rewards and pats on the back help keep you motivated. This is very suitable for gym beginners and people who want to get fit and in shape. Keep it up.

This is an amazing app. I am on the free version and I can create my own routines using their library of exercises or make custom ones. I can add rest times which automatically start. I love that the app automatically tracks how much weight I've lifted and compares my performance to the previous time I did that exercise. I particularly like the how to section and the filters of equipment and muscle group to find exercises. There's also charts and stats of muscle groups, weight and measurements!

was recommended this app. im not a gym person and find alot of the machines baffling but this app shows how to use machine/weights in a easy way. its great being able to see your progress. easy to use once you've started. app tells you when you've reached new goals and keeps track of what you've done. work's off line. im 60 and if i can use it so can you.

Great way to track your progression, I always know what exercise to do next and if I'm bored of a particular one Hevy can come with recommendations for similar ones. -- I would love more examples (real life video/gif) of correct form as the gym man illustrations sometimes isn't enough. Overall I highly recommend this as it's helped me tons getting into shape!

I really love this app for so many different reasons. Aside from logging workouts, there's a great variety of stats, little ribbons for every PR that act as a little dopamine hit, and these cute little graphics comparing your lifting volume to lifting helicopters, yachts, etc. I also love the social feature, because I can stay in touch with friends long distance and compare workouts! The paid version is worth every dollar in my opinion, and at $30CAD, the annual plan is pretty affordable!

I really enjoy using this app. It's user friendly and easy to log my workouts as I go. But you better hope you don't run into any problems because the customer support is next to non-existent. I've got a subscription issue that I've been trying to get resolved for 2 weeks. Out of the 5 plus emails I've sent them they replied to 1 telling me they see what the problem is but unfortunately that thread went cold. Super frustrating dealing with generated auto replies.

Started using this for the free version while getting back into weight lifting. used it for a year and love it. so easy to use. it provides a library of exercises to do for each muscle group and has videos of how to do it and where you should feel it so you can ensure you are doing it correctly. have recently signed up for the pro version as use it almost every day and now want to add in more than my 4 free routines. I know it is money well spent. would highly recommend for the free or pro vs

Best app I've found... and yes, I pay for it! I've been lifting since 1996. Regardless, Hevy is a solid programme allowing one to use either recommended programmes or the ability to create a personal one. The ability to add exercises and sets is brilliant. One can search for an exercise by muscle group or equipment. App shows progress by time exercised and by volume - big plus! Downside: Extensive list is somewhat dated and body weight exercises are not included in volume and should be.

The app is very user friendly and has many different exercises that can be tracked easily. It also has multiple helpful features to track exercises in further detail. However, there are some improvments to be made. Providing more details on which muscle is being worked out is needed. For eg, further categorising shoulder exercises into those that work out front, side and rear deltoids. Also, there are some missing exercises that are very beneficial and should be added. Overall excellent app

I have been using Hevy for about 6 months now. Rating it as a 4 (for now) due to the limited selection of exercises. I hope there is a way to add either more, or customize the workouts (exercise names, angles, setups, etc) so far I have been using the notes portion to remind me. Which is also a nice touch. The Strava integration is also excellent. Looking forward to more helpful features!

Love Hevy! It's so easy to build and follow a training plan. I can't fault this app, I initially used the free version but as my training plan grew I upgraded, I love how it tracks everything, shows me which muscles I've worked, shows how to execute the moves, has graphs to show progress, and tells me I've lifted a yacht 🤣 I think it'd be a great addition to anyone who trains, if you're just starting out or have been training for years.

Hevy is amazing! This is exactly the app me and my friend were looking for when trying to find somewhere to log our workouts together. The app is extremely simple to use and has so many useful functions. It's almost like Instagram as a gym-social app. What I love most is that there are no ads and no pop ups asking me to pay for any extras. Purely focused on helping you track your work outs with plenty of premade routines and the ability to make your own. Absolutely 10/10!

I'm currently using the free version of the app and it's great. I love the features that we get just in the free version alone, since I'm a student who's trying to find a great workout app, this will be in the top list. App offer most things that we can usually use for workout routine, though it is limited for free. It's enough for me, so whoever need a try on workout app, try this and maybe go for the purchased version later on.. Which soon I might go for.

Just did my 150th workout and I'm really impressed with this user friendly app. The library of exercises is fairly wide and you can always add your own. Only recommendation is if when selecting which type of equipment you have (full gym, free weights, machine, etc) if you could specify any particular bars or special equipment. ie: trap bar, ez curl bar, etc. Otherwise, I recommend! Thanks guys!

was looking for an app where I could log all my workouts including strength, runs, yoga, pilates, just for my own records! this allows me to track it all, not just strength like some of the alternatives. I've had a few small glitches recently where it's hard to mark my set as complete but I think this is just a small issue that will be fixed soon 🤞🏼🫶🏼

Downloaded this in Jan to help me track my classes and exercises and even at the free tier, Hevy is amazing - I've now paid for Pro. Personally, I'd love it if I could add custom measurements so I can keep track of certain areas of my body that I'm trying to work on. I'd also love for the time/date scroll to be type-able as scrolling can be tedious. Overall, keep up the good work!!!

I’ve been using Hevy daily and it has made my workout tracking very easy. No need to remember sets, reps, or weights — everything is recorded properly. It really helps with progressive overload because I can clearly see my previous performance and try to improve each session. The app is very user-friendly and simple to navigate. Exercise info and structure are also good, which helps during workouts. Overall, a very useful app for anyone serious about gym consistency. Definitely recommended.

Loving the app and its really amazing features for a free workout app. It's fun seeing my PRs and my mates workouts. Tracking is super easy which is what I want resting during sets. Would rate 5 stars, there's just one bug that's annoying me. When using the search feature, typing at a normal speed will type multiple letters at once e.g. 'cam' turns into 'ccaccam' very quickly 😂 Otherwise great work! I have been recommending this app to all my gym mates!

I really like the app, its informative and makes training so easy the only minor things I dont like is when I upload a picture it resets the whole app and I have to try 3 - 4 times to get it to the next screen. Also when you lift it tells you all your pbs etc and what your lift equates to but it always picks a car or bus or yacht. I wish there were animals or fun quirky things because I've seen this in other apps and it's a fun feature.

great app. easy to follow and modify workouts as you go. I really like the visual prompts and simple instructions that show where you should feel the exercise. I have proprioception issues so its been good for me in my early part of the journey. Only downside is that is doesn't speak to my garmin so if I want to track properly it requires doubling up on entries. Currently not an issue as im still a novice, but once that cross over if achieved watch out this one will be unstoppable.

This app is perfect, down to the last tiny detail. I started using it about a year ago and it's completely changed the way I workout, logging feels simple and straight forward, it's so much easier to know when your progressing, and just opening up your profile and seeing all the work outs you've done in the past is such a satisfying feeling, I just wish the app had more users!!

Love this app. Helps track progressive overloads very easily. The free version is ad free and is more than enough for regular gym goes. Social section of the app is a great way to get motivated as well. Overall, very happy with the app and would not regret paying for the premium version to help the dev team build such a flawless product.

Great app! I used to do everything through my excel spreadsheets to track workouts and progress. Hevy really simplifies things and it has great integration with Health Connect allowing seamless syncing with many other fitness apps including Fitbit, Google fit, Samsung my health, myfitness pal, and many more. The free version is good and the subscription or one time payment for life is great!

Tried paid for a month. It is not intuitive, display is awkward, functions don't work. Then there's the Trainer. It spit out a poor version of push, pull, legs. The routines don't have enough per muscle group exercises to stimulate development. The sets have a very high rep range, increasing injury risk. It puts isolation work in between compound lifts, destabilizing and risking injury. It suggests exercises that don't recruit what you're working or have high risk of straining something else. 0*

an absolute golden find for someone getting into workouts. so many routines to choose from, and I have built 4 custom routines for myself as well. you can browse through exercises, see actual videos to get the form right and also track your progress. this app has really helped me get on top of my workouts. can't recommend enough!!

Edit: They used to have some pre made training splits like full body 4 day, push pull etc which I loved. Sadly, maybe in a desire to streamline or God knows what, they modified the pre made workouts and categorised them by advanced, intermediate etc and by equipment. The useful pre made splits from before are gone. I wish they would bring them back. other than that, I love how useful this app is. Even the free version.

Thankyou so much Hevy, this beginner feels much more equipped stepping into the gym now ☺️ I especially appreciate that each exercise has a looping clip to show you the correct form & the muscles worked. The range of exercises available in the library is great! Very easy to create your own workout routines & save them for future use. And so easy to keep track of your workouts at the gym in real time & not lose track of what set you're on etc cause you can check each set off! love it 😀

I've found this to be a really good app. Helping to keep track of my gym activities and found it has helped to motivate me to attend, do better sessions and improve lifts. It also has a list of activities you can use to search for inspiration when you get bored of the same old, or you just want to don't know what to do to train a set of muscles.

The app has been my constant while I workout for the last two years. It has some easy ways of tracking and also showing some wonderful graphs based on the data we share. I've reduced my fat and gained muscle. I'm a paid user. I've tracked my body measurements as well. It's quite good to see the changes in body tracked, measured and progress seen. Stay motivated, stay fit!

Really good app for tracking workouts. It's easy to setup routines, find the right exercises, and track your progress, what muscles you've used, etc. Everything is easy to navigate and use. I've been using the free version but might upgrade just to support such a great app.

the app is incredible in both free and paid plans I love how you can do a lifetime payment. I also love the attention to detail and the little things but I would appreciate adding more exercises to your library. I don't get how even a hip flexor stretch isn't already there and I had to make it myself also add a field to input instructions when creating new exercises and make instructions available for already existing ones Thank you VERY much

it's good but very VERY glitchy. I was unable to replace my exercises during the routine. I couldn't add new sets or remove either. I had to force close the app a ton and restart and that only fixed the problem half of the time. I'm a pixel 8 user.... edit: I started paying the monthly subscription fee and it seems less glitchy now. but it sucks that I couldn't get that level of functionality as a free user. I do love that they have lifetime option that's reasonably priced too... I hate subs.

best social/gym tracking app there is. Other social media sites need to take notes on this. also, thanks for preventing any NSFW. only a couple suggestions with some things I've noticed is maybe adding the option to turn off the vibration with like button, and also a way to add routines to future days on the calendar somehow. but love the app.

Great, easy to use app that keeps track of exercises, sets and weight perfectly. Also, I like at the end of your routine it tells you how much weight you did all together and the fact that I lifted as much as a T-Rex today... and shows you what muscles you worked.

love the application a lot; the only thing that would improve it would be that the premium version has RIR and things like that, to be able to control it better. It would be a super good application if you could also add training mesocycles of 4 to 8 weeks in the premium version, alright.

app is good, but doesn't improve. They know their issues but don't fix them. For example, they know about the lack of a feature to delete your personal records or heaviest weight, but still don't add it. But this feature is so important when you change gyms and switch to new equipment.

Look elsewhere for now. EXCESSIVE battery drainage, can't delete history (come on, a delete button is a basic feature), only allowed a few custom excersises or programs until you pay the lifetime or subscription price. Instead of fixing the issues, they're to busy doing AI trainer.

The best workout app. After trying at least 15 different apps I keep coming back to this one. Highly recommended. Editing this review 5 years later: I purchased the lifetime premium as this app is so good. it now connects with health connect which means I can link the workouts with other apps like Garmin and Cronometer. It just keeps getting better. Much love to the Hevy team!

I'm using the free version at the mo and rate it very highly. The only thing I'd love is being able to schedule the workouts and get reminders, but that's minor and overall the app really is fantastic. I will upgrade in a few months as the pro is great value for money.

An outstanding tracking app. Has a few bugs here and there but for the most part those don't really impact you. Solid recommendation. Has a subscription model, but I'm fine on the free tier. Also has a lifetime sub which I appreciate. I'm thinking about getting the lifetime.

App keeps its word. let's you track your own workout without forced sign up and credit card. offers paid services if you choose it. It doesn't make you spend 10 minutes filling out your stats and suddenly force you to get out your credit card to continue.

(edit: -2stars, only four routines for free tier, not mentioned upfront. Deleted) Not five because 1. Not minimal enough. Ex. Adding circuit routine is a hidden feature, we have to search. 2. We just need the name, the rep and timer when we workout. 3. No cardio, calisthenics, hiit mode. Reps sets even names are less important than the intervals or volumes. Insane to list them all. 4. Routines override on default, and the switch is under "finish" page.

I just love how much value you get from the free version. it works as a good tracker with easily accessible animated gifs of all exercises. explanations are there, muscle groups are there, you get access to 3 months of history which is plenty for most people... I dont need any of the premium stuff but I've bought the yearly sub just to support a great free offering, rly nice user friendly work.

Best workout tracking app and the crazy part is that it has everything you need in the free version. Has almost all the exercises and so many useful features. Once you start using it there's no going back. Wish I found it earlier!

very nice for personal tracking workouts, they have a lot of exercises to customize your workout but I hope for more variety of exercises (i.e band row, shadow boxing with weights and a few more that you often see around the media). Well done everyone!

I really like this app. it's fairly easy to navigate, I love the instructional videos. I wish it would allow me more custom routines though instead of just 4, even 6 would be awesome. it syncs to my watch which is great.

I've been using Hevy for around three years now (free version). I use it every time I weight train (haven't hooked it up to my watch for cardio). they allow you to make around three or four workout routines on the free version and allows you to review your progress over three months (the paid version allows for longer time periods to observe). I recommend Hevy to everyone I know - also for those ADHDers out there you can check mark each rep. I cannot recommend Hevy enough. Thank you Hevy

This app helped me so much with keeping track of my workouts, weights, routines and pretty much everything I had to do in the gym. It's genuinely a lifesaver and I love it to death! The free version does its job impeccably and I'm very glad that I found and managed to keep using it. I highly recommend it for anyone, especially those who take big breaks between gym sessions or who just want to get in the gym, turn of their brain and hit the weights on autopilot, noticing progress along the way (:

Love the app! its very easy to use and overall very user friendly. I sometimes find it hard to find the right machines in the app (especially ab machines). Id also appreciate if there was a feature where i could click on the machine in app & get instructions for how to use it & correct form. other than that it's great & id defo recommend it!!!

I was looking for something that I could create my own workouts and just keep track of my sets instead of the app formulating a workout for me based on a whole bunch of factors so this is perfect for what I was looking for. I also really enjoy all the metrics it gives you after your workout like how many pounds total and a comparison to what that would be, like lifting a helicopter for example

Great app for tracking training and gym workouts. I DON'T have pro and it's not too pushy to try and get you to subscribe. I genuinely don't feel any limitations with the free version. Would recommend!!!

I have really enjoyed using this app. It has been easy to create my own routines to quickly access while in the gym OR use the app created workouts. The reporting feauturw is fantastic. It was so worth paying for the 1 year subscription.

Great app for tracking, yearly membership is kinda cheap and you don't really need it. I'd love to see a trophy pop up when u hit a new maximum amount of reps with your highest weight as sometimes I hit a pr but it isn't a new weight or volume pr

Good app for tracking,currently using the free version which allows me to input workouts without issues and despite not allowing to save more then 4 set workout regimes, it's easily bypassed by them allowing me to do a new workout regime daily with no major downsides utilising the free option

fantastic app for tracking progress and managing lifting routines. I've tried a few others, and as a beginner/intermediate, Hevy is by far the most capable and easiest to use. The automatic stats, graphs, and metrics are very useful and beyond what the other apps offered

only certain exercise get sent to health connect. further with some exercise only some of your sets get sent. basically the sync to the health connect is broken and that severely limits the ability for this app to work with other health tracking. major bug, easily fixed. nonsense this exists

I used to have pen and paper, Goggle sheets, other apps... now it's just Hevy. Absolutely brilliant to track workouts progress, weights, sets etc. It's also integrated with Strava, so it has all needed.

This app has made a huge difference to my gym progress. It makes training progression easy, visually shows you which muscles you have trained, and areas that can be improved. The app is easy to use, and is a great motivation tool as it shows you your progress for each exercise over time. I would highly recommend the app - it was a game changer for me!

Hevy is hands down the best workout tracking app I've ever used, the interface is very clean and intuitive and everything works perfectly, absolutely would recommend for anyone looking to track their workouts.

This app is actually brilliant! I love seeing my progress, I love how easy it is to use and how it shows you how to do exercises! I love how you can tailor all the workouts to what you are doing and you can even ask for exercises on specific muscle groups! I can't recommend it enough for beginners and experts!

unbelievable app. my training was plagued for nearly a decade and as soon as I started using Hevy to progressively overload, my strength has shot up to levels not seen since college. unbelievable. if you're not using this, you're wasting your time.
Super easy to use. I think you get 3 free work outs then you need to pay but its only about 6$ a month. I tested other apps and this one was the best for price value and simplicity.

really great app to track progress and log workouts and measurements. I'm part of a fitness group on FB and already got about 8-10 people to sign up and get the premium subscription too so we can keep track of ourselves and show off our progress to each other. 10/10 would recommend to anyone that goes to the gym.

Hevy has been incredibly helpful with my fitness journey, from starting out as a beginner, hevy helped me stay consistent and stick to a routine that works for me. Through being able to track your progress and keep records of everything has been so helpful. Also the fact that it demonstrates how to perform an exercise has been amazing, especially for a newbie.

It's the best app I've tried so far. I use it to track all my workouts and apply progressive overload. The only thing I don't like is that warm-up sets are included in the statistics, even though they shouldn't be. Update: I have just found a setting to exclude warm-up sets! 5 stars without reserve!

I completed my 50th workout today, and hevy asked me to give a review... this app has made my life so much easier when it comes to tracking my workout and providing me with that little motivation when I hit new PRs. Unlike most people in the gym, I always keep track of my workouts and how much I've lifted previously and how much I should lift today, it helps so much when it comes to progressive overload. It has a wide variety of exercises and you can create custom workouts too, all for free!

This is an excellent gym tracking app and I've been using it for over 2 years now and would highly recommend it. I would just like to make a suggestion to the developers, if possible could you make a search function in the list of custom made workout groups so that I can find a workout in my list easily please? That would make things much better thank you very much.

I started out my fitness journey two years after becoming a mom and couldn't find the proper app to use. (also didn't wanna pay for one). You don't have to pay for this app to get the stuff you need! I use it to track progress as well so this is a great lil app and if you want the extras really not that expensive! Been using it for a year now and love it

I've used quite a few workout apps and this is my favorite. It's easy to adjust workouts, and pairs well with my Galaxy Ultra to track my heartbeat. I'm not particularly fond of the social aspect of the app but it's easy enough to ignore. I do use the paid version and definitely recommend it.

Easy to use and great for beginners. I liked the preloaded routines to start and create my own routines in the app now that I'm more comfortable in the gym. I like the progress tracking graphs, it motivates me to see that I've gotten stronger. A lot of great features for a free app!

I was looking for a new weightlifting log app that had more than just the basics. I love the simplicity of the interface and the ability to discover new exercises. The user interface has intuitive options to quickly record your sets and add new exercises on the fly.

Been using it for about 18 months now and on premium. I am a fan of its clean and easy to use UI. I like that it gets out of the way again quickly so. I can do my sets. I wish you could review previous months performance in the log, but only gives you the most recent month, which makes it much harder to see progress over time

Super impressed with this app. They automatically track every gym related statistic I've been too lazy to track and graph myself, it's very intuitive to make routines and use them. I've been on this app to track my workouts for 3 months now and I'm impressed by how many cool features AREN'T behind a paywall, but even the premium seems affordable and offers very sensible extras. It shows the team behind this genuinely pours love and care into the app and making money isn't the top priority

I've been using this app for free for over 3 months now and I'm very pleased they i found it. It has great featuresz allows me to track my exercises and my progress, marks any improvements, keeps good stats and track of everything I need. I could only recommend it to anyone.

Very good tracking app for workouts and PRs. Highly recommend. Allows you to add photos with each workout routine so you can see your progress over time, which I absolutely love. Told some of my gym buddies abt it, and they love it as well. Highly, highly recommend.

App keeps crashing... I paid for the lifetime subscription and it has been working perfectly for a yr and a half but in the last month it has been repeatedly crashing during use. Works for 10 mins than freezes and crashes. App is "up to date" and phone has no issues but the only app on my phone that repeatedly shuts down.

Brilliant app. Sensational for tracking progress, workouts, sessions and exercises. super easy to use with the option to preload routines and features like monthly reports are great. Highly recommend. The paid upgrade is well worth the dollars! if ya not on Hevy already, giddy up and get on it

Great for tracking your workouts, no matter your capacity and preference. there are workout plans you can follow, you can make your own or just track empty workouts. each exercise has a simple animation to help you do it right and it syncs with other health apps to help track your progress.

an actual game changer, the process I had made in the gym was completely insignificant for like a year UNTIL I installed Hevy, it's very easy to use and convenient for whatever type of training best suits you, if you're thinking about actually making good progress in the gym and you aren't using Hevy there's something wrong going on.

Pretty good app. Would be great to introduce a progressive overload prompt for past/current workouts, something more than RPE. No real benefits from the social side of the app. Galaxy Watch syncing is pretty good, but the scrolling numbers for recording weight and reps might be easier/quicker as keypad inputs.

Pretty good app, I wish they had kettlebell exercises though. That would make this 5 stars. I work out in the gym a few times a week, but use kettlebell routines when traveling or not able to get to the gym. would be great to have them listed here so I don't have to use a separate app.

I really like this app. I would like to see the addition of more exercises. Some things are wildly specific, but others such as sandbag training or static holds, are missing entirely. There is the option to add custom exercises, which partially addresses this issue

I've been using it for a while and overall it's a great app for tracking your training with tons of useful features like getting notified with PR's, being able to create your own exercises to add onto a plan, and things like that. I'd say its a really good app, especially for being mostly free

the UI/UX is pretty straightforward. I tried a few apps before finding Hevy and I think this is the best for planning your workout routine or even adding sessions on the go. the free plan allows u to add up to 7 custom exercises which is cool. Even on the free plan it's quite feature rich.

I love this app but the recent update took away your ability to see the set amount per muscle for a specific workout. Now you need to go to stats, select the muscles and look at a graph. It was much more convenient before the update. hopefully they'll change it back 🤞🏻

plain and simple, the best plain simple workout tracker. no bloat. just clarity. free version is so highly functional, but I bought the paid version just because I wanted to support the maker. thanks again!

Been using this app for 2 years now, and it's super simple and gets the job done! My only gripe is that uploading pictures after a workout is incredibly time-consuming and irritating, often taking at least 3 tries for one picture. A fix for this would make it a solid 5-star app!

Over the past 3 or so decades, I've used many different tracking apps. Free versions, and paid versions. This is by far, without a doubt, the best one! I would recommended paying so you can build as many workouts as you want.

I've used this app for almost a year, and I find it very useful to track your workouts and see your progression in real time, even with the free version! Lots of exercises are included, and it also measures your rest time Absolutely recommend!
fantastic. easy to use, no annoying ads, doesn't push for you to buy premium too much. you can get by just fine using the free version. feels like a truly honest app. cannot recommend enough

Been using this for a while now. It helps me stay on track on my workouts, keeping track of stats in detail. the free demonstration videos, 4 free custom workouts and variety of exercises keeps me on this app! Plenty of usability on the free plan. Only upgraded to premium because my exercise routine has diversified and I need to save more routines.

Generally a fantastic, easy to use app for strength training, despite some issues. It's great at tracking your progress, allows you to access a library of routines and exercises, plus the community aspect of it is great at keeping you motivated. I've had issues with the live sync with my watch, the heart rate data and calories data are lost frequently so unreliable at heart rate zone tracking. In general it's not great for cardio and also uploaded contacts cannot be deleted.

Overall good experience. Easy to create routines, record reps and weight etc. You can easily track your progress. Would be helpful if you could add notes to your workouts, sets etc. You can't move an exercise from one routine to another which is frustrating. The stats are great and helps you see where to focus your training. But it needs a weekly overview not just monthly / 30 days. Will be very interested to see if they adapt any AI int the app, that would hell take it to the next level

I've been using Hevy as a workout tracker app for almost a year now and have no complaints. I typically work out at 5 in the morning (before work) and at the beginning, on those days I didn't feel like getting up and working out, all I had to do was look at my progress. Now its a habit! I also enjoy the positive feed back after every work out, as well, the fact there's a 'how to' video for every work out you have. I would recommend this app to anyone who is wanting to start their journey!

This is my 1st week, 3rd time using the app, and I have to say. I'm loving it so far. I'm able to navigate around the app easily, it's intuitive. I'm able to add and remove sets/reps while I'm in the current set. There's a place to save my progress photos when I finish my workout so I can have a visual record too. Best part I've been able to add my PPL routines and get in and out of the gym faster. If you enter all the info correctly it will automatically prompt the next exercise or set.

The best app I found tracking workout, was the first thing that came up when I searched for workout tracking. Simple and clean UI, nothing fancy, and the best thing it has no ads. There are some exercises missing but is more of a my issue since I make up stuff when I can't find the equipment available. I have been using this app everyday and never have I found any issue whatsoever.

The best workout app I have used. Well worth the purchase. The only thing that I would like to be added is the option to add alternative exercises for workout (so I don't always have to use the "replace exercise" option and search for similar exercises while working out). With that, it would be a complete app for me. Keep up the good work!

I use the free version. It's super easy to use, helps take away the intimidation of getting into lifting weights by Showing images/videos, and great for tracking progress. I like that I can find programs to follow or insert my own (I used to have a personal trainer so they gave me a program to follow, and I uploaded that into the app). Each workout movement you select for your program shows you what muscles it targets, so you can build full body or isolate easily on your own.

Very good!! I can save up to 4 routines in the free version, which is perfect for me since I workout 4 times a week. I could also easily import my workouts from my previous app, and this app has many exercises the other didn't, especially unilateral variations. The only downside is I need people's users to follow them. I wish there was a location-based following filter. Other than that, I love it!

First time at the gym and I was looked for an easy to use, user friendly app suitable for beginners. after reading some reviews, I found Hevy and I have never looked back. the paper and pen / notes in the phone are in the bin and its Hevy every single time. Motivation with the little gold medals and the workout streaks. Very easy to use and a huge package free of charge and no ads! I actually didn't need the paid version but wanted to support the app, so purchased 12 months. Recommend it to all!

Loving it! So Helpful! I used the free version for a bit but loved it so much I opted for the premium version of the app. I love the videos that show you how to do the exercises so you can try new exercises and add them to your workouts as you go. The app comes with pre-loaded workouts if you prefer. You can add your cardio sessions including running and swimming or it syncs to strava which is great!

Great app with great features. It makes tracking my workouts and progress super easy. Since the gym isn’t my main priority, I often forget my lifting numbers — but Hevy keeps everything organized. It saves me time by showing me what weights I used for each exercise, so I can focus on lifting heavier instead of guessing. Plus, all the statistics it provides make it even more useful. Really helpful and user-friendly.”

The Best both Offline and Online Fitness Application on the market. You can still use this application offline. Once you download the program, you can see the actual video tutorial on how to do the specific work out. You can also gain more knowledge even if you did not download the video. Just go to the (How to Do) section to read the description on the specific workouts. 100 out of 10 Keep it up. I hope you didn't change this feature until the future ✨

Hevy is the perfect app for what I'm looking for: a simple place to track sets and reps and monitor progress. The medals for PRs are a nice motivational touch as well. Probably the best thing about this app is its lack of ads and although there is a payed version, it doesn't feel like it's being shoved down our throats (looking at you, Strava!). I was recommended this app by a friend and have already recommended it to three other friends.

Very nice app, all my friends and I use it to compete with our performances. 2 features that I hope you can add. 1 the ability to filter out the leader board to certain people, cos I want to follow a lot of people but also don't want them on my competing leader board. and 2 a button on people's profile maybe "See Beginning" that takes you all the way down to their first post to see what they started with, that way we don't have to scroll down if they have 600 workouts. but otherwise amazing!

Probably one of the best value apps on the market, they've thought of everything when it comes to UX/UI and the functionality it offers. Phone app, watch app, web app and API access? Absolutely unheard of. As someone who has been tracking their workouts manually since 2017 in Excel / Google Sheets, this app is a game changer. As long as the API access doesn't get depreciated, the app could stay as it is with no further updates and you'll have me as a customer for life.

The non plus ultra of gym apps. The free version offers all that you would need already, its easy to use and helped me a lot in my fitness journey for the past 3 years. Even tho, again, the free version is enough, I bought the Lifetime-Version recently as a thank you to the developer team for creating what I consider one of the best apps for tracking your bodybuilding journey.

I've been using Hevy for 6 months and its a great app to use. I've tried several workout apps to track my workouts but this is the best one I've used. It's easy and quick to fill in when you're working out so that means that you're not spending ages on your phone and breaking your rhythm. The data it collects is all useful and you see your last sets/reps for each exercise when you do it again in a new routine. It tells you when you are setting new records which is a nice perk to get a medal!

ISSUE: if you have exercises in your routine that you skipped, it still shows in the summary that you completed that exercise. Not great for tracking. No response from developers. RECOMMENDATIONS: 1. after entering one set, it would be nice for it to automatically jump to the next set and ready to enter the number of reps. just saves an extra tap. 2. ability to star exercises so that it's easier to find exercise used often.3. Give people a trial of the personalized trainers.
