It makes satisfying sounds during navigation and I as able to jump right in and figure out how it worked as I used it. One small area did opportunity to the design team. The ux on choosing exercises is a bit clunky with single hand operation. I think just being able to search and filter from lower on the screen would make this a lot better (by not having to click on the bar at the top.

***** 6th review update over 800 work outs logged. It's what I use!!!! Updated review. Still using. Extremely customizable. Easy to use, basic and effective. I use this app to track my kettlebell workouts and the 5x5 barbell work I do. I wish it had a tabata timer, and an AMRAP timer for those workouts. Since this app has no idea what a kettlebell is I've had to create work arounds for it. But it does let me add exercises and program my own routines. It works very well. i am still enjoying it.

I use this app everytime I lift. Great app. The only thing I do not like and is the reason I'm not giving it 5 stars is because the charts that you unlock when going pro do not show your weight timelines for an exercise based on what you lifted that day. The chart goes based off of the apps estimate of what your 1 rep max would be for that exer. (Which for me is very wrong, its way lower than what my 1 rep max really is). If it gave us an option to switch between the two this would be 5 stars.

Easy to add workouts, simple design. I like that it vibrates before the rest timer is over, and i like that you can see what you're doing in the banner on the top of your phone. I also like that you can see previous weight/ reps on the same screen. My only complaint is that you can't add notes to individual reps (like "next time do more" or "wow that was really hard") overall a great app, coming from someone who likes pen and paper!

The best workout tracker I've found. It actually has specific workouts instead of general ones. My request is to add easier ways to add up weights when working with barbells. Build in a calculator to add the weight of each side and bar so I don't need to do math in my head haha. You kinda of already have this in the premium where it shows weight when you put in the full, just a small addition 🙂

Great app. Love the clean design, very intuitive. One of my favorite features is the auto rest timer. It just works exactly how you would want it to. Excellent for tracking and gradually modifying workouts. Wish there were more exercises to choose from, but fairly easy to create your own if you don't care so much about putting data metrics behind them (beyond weight/reps). Really wish this would somehow sync with Garmin.

Got this app to help track my workouts and better track my progressive overload. At base, it gives you some example workouts following a basic body split, lets you track your workouts and you can create 3 custom workout routines. For a free app its not super limiting. Bought the 1year plan, which lets me see a graph of my workouts, and I can create as many as 5 (atleast that's how many I need) custom routines. After 2 weeks I'm still super happy with the app. Wish it connected to Fitbit though.

Excellent workout tracker without trying to be extra. Makes it super easy to duplicate, update, and track individual workouts. Has added features to save routines. Great exercise variety, yet to find an exercise that isn't included. There is an ability to create custom exercises if so. Only issue found was auto-rest timer is unique to the exercise and not the workout (was expecting each instance of an exercise to have its own and not share). Overall amazing workout tracker. Would recommend!

Been a long user of this app. Recently resubscribed after a break. I am unable to change my status from free to subscription. In otherwords I am paying for no features. Contacted customer support, no answer. It's a shame as the app is great, but I don't want to pay for something I can't use the features for. The one star rating for bad customer service.

Just started the subscription model. Already doesn't appear to be worth it. It says it will let you choose your plates, but the choices are REALLY limited, half my plates aren't shown and I see no way to enter them. In addition, the "automatic warmup sets" doesn't appear to work. For example 50% of 76lbs is not 45lbs. Free version is great for beginners, but I don't think I'll keep the pay version.

Switched from JEFIT to Strong. I tried a lot of the workout apps before deciding on Strong. Love that I can select the rest period timer time, add notes, etc. The only thing I haven't been able to figure out how to do is revise the order of the exercises. Fix that and I'll upgrade to 5 star!

Used it for years and came back after using one of the apps that's used often by coaches. After three months of using that one, I actually got off of the team I was on, mostly because I hated the app so much. I love how simple Strong is, and I've been a paid user for a long time just because I love the app. One thing I would actually like to see in the future is a folder system to group workouts. I think that'd be a neat feature to make it easy if you are a coach to share with your team.

Latest update deleted my account with 660+ workouts over 3+ years. I went to the profile page (where there is a "danger zone" to delete your account) and immediately a loading bar spun. I was signed out and can't get back into the account with email or google which was linked. it just makes a new account. It's gone, gee thanks.

Great app! I love the ease of use and flexibility it has with creating your own workouts and adding new exersizes that aren't on the app. I have used other fitness apps before this one and for ease of use, flexibilty, and price this one is the best. The reason why I don't give 5 stars is because there are some exersize forms demonstrated that are out of date and can cause injury like the lateral raise. Check Andrew Kwong on YouTube for proper form.

It's a great app, Sadly the free version doesn't doesn't have the amount of workout templates that I require which means I have to use another app as well which is an inconvenience but it makes sense as the developer wants to make money from this which is understandable, As a young gym goer, I can't see the value in paying for the premium version of the app

Used this app for over 200 workouts. left for a while, when I was newer to weight lifting and wanted something that would make workouts for me. But, nothing tracks as well as Strong. Easy, works perfect, looks good, once you have a program or know how to structure your own workouts this app is the GOAT.

Nearly perfect but needs another post-workout option. Currently you can assume all non-completed exercises were completed, or delete them. What if the gym was full and I didnt get to it today but want to leave it for next time? App just needs a "update weights only and dont delete non-completed exercises" option.

I've been the app for couple years now. It does what I need and is somewhat easy to use. But after one of the latest update, I no longer receive notifications on my Garmin watch. So I can't mark sets as complete and stuff. I have tried a complet reset of the watch and settings. No luck. :(

Been great but ever since the last update I no longer get notifications on my Garmin watch when my rest period is up. I've checked Garmin and it still has notifications active and hasn't been updated so seems to be a Strong issue. I often don't hear the beep over music so now often go over.

Lots of built on exercises with explainers if needed, but also the ability to add your own if they don't already exist. Helps track rest time and training progress, including PRs. Allows for tracking of body measurements. Great app.

Needs kettlebell exercises. I bought the lifetime subscription for this. I love the app. use it multiple times per week and have for years. Still don't understand why they've not yet added kettlebell exercises.

Has been the easiest app to plan and log workouts. Allows for easy updating and modifications to templates during workouts. Easy to identify supersets, and even create new exercises that are specific to a gym or a workout. Keeps your history directly on the exercises when moving from one template to another. Allows for notes as well. Would like to see the ability to leverage AI for coaching or recommendations, but there is always going to be room for improvements.

The program does not allow me to delete the real template and just edit it. if I just accidentally added an approach, then I can't change it. There is no way to fix your program in the application if there are errors.
I've been using this for years and even as a free user, it's been really great. if it was a one time purchase instead of a subscription, I'd for sure buy it.
This app is really the best app out there to track workouts. it also helps with the weight journey too! Moreover it's free, and gets updates regularly! definitely a 5/5

I like this app but I think the 1RM calculations are wrong. It says it uses Brzycki's formula, but, for example, gives a 1RM of 99 for 75 kg x 11 reps. With Brzycki, this should be 75*36/(37-1) = 104 kg. Please fix

Please implement the option to turn the 3 sec countdown vibration off (or any vibration for that matter), I have a gym where the only way to make the countdown on the phone visible is to put it on a narrow windowsill, it can easily crash this way
unable to login with Facebook and I tried to reset password with email but email never sent. so unsure how to login since I pay for a subscription.
Great app. The formula for 1RM used to be correct. Sometime around January 11th, the formula changed. Now incorrect

I've been using the free version of this app for over a year and it has been great. It recently updated and now only allows two free templates instead of three. Having three templates was enough, but having two is not, and so I will be looking for a new app because the paid version of this one is not worth it.

All the Data tracking you need in an easy to organize and follow format. Really like the customization and integration ability. I have been using Strong for a number of years now. I haven't found another app quite like this one. Try it out. You won't be disappointed.

I hate AI apps that shove non-human suggestions down my throat. This app has none of that. Simple, clean, 100% optimized and perfectly functional. All I wanted was an app which let's me track my workouts like a notepad and that's exactly what this is. The app can also be used absolutely free which I did for about a year before upgrading to the pro version. Not at all necessary, but super useful and surprisingly affordable as well.

No ads. Amazing capabilities. The free version gives you 3 templates you can make and if you want more you can get the paid version, though 3 templates are more than enough. 10/10 would definitely recommend it. Been using it for 4 months now.

I like building my own routines based on what I build in ChatGPT so I can track the weight & rep build. I'm not certain the superset feature really works and I wish I could add my own sets, something without weights, with bands for example. Overall it meets my needs.
notification sound is Messing with the system sound. only way to restore the volume is to restart Spotify. it's rather annoying to restart Spotify 10x each Work out

The app is very functional for creating workouts, checking correct exercise execution, and tracking sets and time. I rarely leave reviews for apps, but the Strong support team deserves my time. And thank you for the most immediate problem resolution I've ever experienced in the digital world.

Great App - Been using it for over a year now. User friendly, quick to setup, easy to log and use in the gym. 4 Stars only as I'd like to see a greater variety of exercises within the list (It has most but not all) 😊

I love this app. I logged my 755th work out today! Also it's pretty handy for powerlifting too with the RPEs, it's also extremely easy to use. The only downside to creating exercises in the app is there's no option for glutes - which is fine, legs it is lol.
need an option to delete a set from a template or one added by mistake. the only way in can get rid of it is to duplicate the original workout and r enter the data
when performing supersets, the notifications are incorrectly labelled with the wrong exercise.
I like the app but it has limited exercises. I can't log some exercises.

I've used this app for over three years and have been hugely impressed by the free version and the intuitive interface. Which makes the recent developments all the more disappointing. I turned on today to see a notification about the Strong 6.0 release. Some cosmetic changes, but when I came to record my workout, it was completely unusable and buggy. It wouldn't even let my type reps and weights in the correct box! So frustrating, I had to cancel my workout. Please fix soon!!

While I'm still unsure on the upgrade. I'm sure I'll get used to it. My 5 star review is purely based on the support received. Recently they had an issue where user accounts disappeared/got locked out/data gone. Even though I'm not a paying user, they were quick to respond, and assisted in getting everything back. PS I would be a paying user if the once off cost wasn't so high, I can't do "monthly" subscriptions :-)

Finally found a good free gym app, this is my first time leaving a review: This app has EXACTLY everything you need and nothing more to track your workouts and progress as a serious lifter. No fancy features that you are never going to use. Yes there is room for improvement but it is just good enough as it is. Note for devs: I'm able to add multiple excercises with the same name, seems like a bug to me, thanks.

Great app! Helps me a lot with my workouts. I had to add a lot of exercises though as there was heaps missing. It would be great if I could add an image to a custom exercise and assign multiple muscle groups to it. I find the auto rest timer on cuetom exercises quite annoying! It would also be handy to "copy" an exercise as a template to quickly create another one that is similar. Also would be good to add a permanent note to a custom exercise.

This app is great for most gym goers as it has a huge variety of exercises to choose from and track your progress. HOWEVER, it could be better as they lack a lot of exercises that could be helpful to track, like boxing related exercises and more like rear deltoid, hanging on bar, boxing with weight, etc that would make the app better. Would appreciate updates to it, thanks. Really love using it. ❤️ 💪

I have been using the app since the beginning of the year, and I like it a lot. I even think if unlocking the paid features. Though there are two things I don't like: 1) For single arm/leg exercises there isn't a way to enter weight and set per limb, so you have to enter the combined weight of, for example, bicep curls, if you want the total weight to show in the end, instead of entering the weight of the dumbbells used, and have the app multiply it by 2. 2) Bigger library of exercises.

I play provincial level football and train a lot. i have tested out many different apps and this is by far the best workout logger app ever!!! I love it. I have multiple workouts saved and pick which ones I want to do so its good for cycles. It tells me exactly how many lbs, sets and reps I did last workout. It also has a rest timer and allows me to superset. It tells me if I hit a PR. I can also CREATE my own excercises and add them in. I could not imagine a better workout app. Must Have App!!!

I've been lifting for 3 years and have tried 5-10 other tracking apps, but Strong is my number one. It is super intuitive and I can find all I need easily. Workout logging is a breeze. Only feature I miss is automatic weight increase between workouts like in StrongLifts. Otherwise 4.9/5 stars. It also has a cool feature i use for tracking body measurements.

I loved this app, but in 3+ years they haven't updated it in any significant way. - Sample Templates can't be hidden, stuffing up the UI. - No new exercises have been added, so you have to manually create loads. A scan of power users' most commonly shared manually-created exercises would fix this. - Est. 1RM doesn't account for volume, so it's just wrong. - No autofill function when adding more sets. Why do I have to enter the same info in repetitively. There's more but no space to add more...

Everything about the app is great, unfortunately the price for the pro version which is required to plan more than 3 workout routines is prohibitively expensive. At the time I wrote this review the one time payment to unlock the app is about $99 USD, completely ridiculous. If the price were reasonable I would be more than happy to pay. The free version, although good, is too limiting for me to really use when there are alternatives available.

Review is for the paid version. It's expensive, but if you want something that tracks and allows extensive history of your PBs and previous days.. Plus being able to create your own templates (great for on-season / off-season etc) then I highly recommend. My only gripe is that the exercise library needs to be more extensive. Just so I'm not marking down an exercise that I'm actually not doing 😅

Great app, love the user experience. Would love to see an option to add the starting resistance of machines with the options for bars. Eg. My leg press has a starting resistance of 136lbs and I'd love to add that to my workout so when the math isn't mathing, I can click the help button and remember how many plates I need to add. Other than that, I really enjoy this app. Thanks!

I have used this app in the past it was okay. But now when I add external workouts even when I haven't reached the limit of workouts, i cannot add them (through links) I am so frustrated and annoyed by this. I am constantly missing workouts too. I dont know about iOS but in Android it's just not working properly. I click on the link, it redirects me to the app but nothing happens. Please look into this and fix it ASAP.

When I first installed the app. It was really nice! There would be visual displays of how to do a workout. But since the update, it asked me to login and all my previous workouts were lost and my routines were lost as well. All the visuals have disappeared. You can't select the measurement to be set as metric and have to manually change the weight for each exercise. If updates are to improve the app, the updates have ruined this app. I'm considering uninstalling it and switching.

Logged me out for no reason and now will not allow me to log in or reset password. Years of training history lost Edit: now that the issue appears resolved I must edit this review to reflect it's value to me. To be brief, sometimes you don't appreciate how valuable something is until it's gone and I realised yesterday that I rely on this quite a bit. It's an excellent tool for tracking training and making steady improvements. Does everything you need and does it well. Excellent app!

I love the customisability of the workouts. I did not like the rest timer appearing automatically after completing a set initially as I try to look at my phone as little as possible during a workout, but I found out you could turn it off! The integration with HealthKit (I mostly use yhe app on my iPhone) is applaudable! On a different note, I have always wanted to know how much weight I am pulling for example during a bodyweight chin up - is there no way to estimate that from logged weight?

Pros: 1. Very easy to use 2. Provides out of the box workout plans 3. Great for beginners 4. You don't have to carry notebooks with you everywhere at the gym to track your workouts and progress Cons: For advanced lifters the free version might not cut it, since you can't track your measurements, body fat percentage, add more than 3 different workout routines or track your progress in various exercises as a student subscription based fees or spending 90$ for an application is not ideal

Very nice, but could be better. The app is very nice to use and has a good amount of exercises, while some are forgotten, it still allows you to create new exercises to adapt your routines to your personal taste better. Progression tracking is also well made. It does not work with locked screen though, making circuit training a little bit harder to work with in the app, specially since the superset options is very very clunky and counter-intuitive in the UI.

Love this app, it has everything you need for tracking your workouts and gains. However with the latest big update completing sets from the notification is buggy and sometimes won't work and the timer will be messed up when you go into the app you complete the set there. Also there's been more crashes but they have been becoming less frequent.

Really nice looking app and I'd have happily carried on using it BUT only 3 free workout slots which means I can't plan my 5 day workout without paying the subscription fee so it didn't work out for me. It would be good if there was an option to pay a small one off fee to unlock extra slots, I don't need most of the premium features so the subscription just didn't work out for me. Great app if you're a beginner working out a couple of times a week though!

This is app was a reason why it was hard to leave iOS. So glad it's on Android now. Fantastic app. Quick, efficient, intuitive, perfect. I'm removing a star because my one-time payment for premium many years ago along with a decent amount of beta testing was completely forgotten when I returned to the app. I have to repurchase on the sub basis. Seems cold.

I really love the app. It's clean, easy to use and the exercise illustrations are super helpful. The reason why i'm only giving it a 2/5 rating is because it's only possible to save three workouts if not paying for the pro version. Normally i have no problem paying anything for an app. But a monthly 5€ fee for this..? Cmon guys. Lower the monthly to 2€ or a one-time flat 7-12€ fee, and you'll end up seeing a lot more retention here.

Love this app. :) Exactly what I need. Would be great if you guys could include a timer for duration-based exercises. Example.. Skipping for 30 seconds and you have an option to click "Start Timer" or something similar to start a timer. Currently I'm using the break timer in between exercises to avoid switching outside the app to use a separate timer.

Easy to use, i use this to track multiple workouts for different goals. Custom exercises are a great feature. I have used other fitness tracking apps, this one is very good at what it does. If only we could design a set program (instead of just the workout) and to track the individual training days and give results/statistics for exercises over the whole program.

Only used once but it's exactly what I was looking for. Something simple where I could track my sets and reps to ensure I'm progressing how I want to be. No bells, no whistles, just a quick and easy way to keep track. What excercises they don't have you can just add. I'm on a push/pull/leg split so I only need the 3 workout templates, if you want more you have to pay for it.

I've been using this app for about three months (been going to the gym on and off for decades). It is an amazing way to organize and track your progress. I have been using the free version and have been able to build and follow a personalized 4 day routine (by using the three free make your own slots and altering one of the provided templates). The interface is easy to pick up and it is easy to change, add, or switch things as you go. An excellent upgrade from my worn out little black book!

This app is really good, from the design to the data analysis. However, as a professional, I find it hard to track volume on specific muscle districts (e.g. quads, hams, calves and glutes rather than just "legs"). I really wish there was an option to add new "body parts" when creating an exercise. It would really make a difference and I hope you will consider implementing this feature.

Great app. Simple, yet intuitive and packed with features; a combination not easily achieved. My one gripe is the cost of the 'pro' version. It is subscription based and costs more than my Spotify and Netflix accounts per month. I would absolutely get the pro version if it were like a one time 6$ fee, but currently id way rather just make my own Excel sheets than spend hundreds of dollars per year for someone else to do it for me!

As we get more serious about the gym sessions, keeping track of programs, sets, reps and load is important to ensure good progress. The Strong app providers an easy to use interface, quick to get into, and fairly easy to program. There are some issues I’d like to address. It is not possible, as far as I can tell, to remove the example programs. They clog up the main menu and serve no purpose when not being used. When editing the programs, the keyboard/keypad sometimes rest over the fields you are editing, thus making it impossible to see what you are typing in. This is REALLY frustrating. I have a Galaxy S20, so this might appear differently on other platforms. There is no support for “left/right” sets. If you do one-side isolation exercises, you have to make two sets and add notes for left and right, then merge them as a superset. It would be better to have support for left/right foot/hand/side/whatever directly in the sets. Added bonus of being able to track progression per side, as well. I use a template for standard program, usually compound exercises, and add ad-hoc stuff to that for each session. It should be possible in the app to highlight or visually separate what was in the template for the Workout, and what exercises was added on the fly.

After having tried so many apps and being turned off by so many because of their cluttered and confusing menus and being riddled with ads, I was incredibly relieved to have finally found an app that looks like it was made by me - for me. Clean menus, easy custom workout creation, satisfying progress tracking during workouts. It has everything you'd want. On top of that, minimalistic design and a sharp dark mode. Contemplating buying the (albeit expensive) premium version just out of respect.

This is one of the better workout trackers I have used. Very functional and easy to use interface. Would love for a few additional options to be added that would make this app even better. Would be nice to be able to group your workouts into a program. Right now I keep making new workouts but they are related to the same program, so would be nice to make it a folder or something as to not block up the main workout screen.

This app just works. It's intuitive and thoughtful. A few features I love: picking weight unit for each exercise, auto starts rest timer, doesn't crash, doesn't also try to be a nutrition tracker. Wish there were more exercises programmed in but creating custom exercises is easy enough. One suggestion. The app detects when you've changed a pre-saved routine and allows you to update the routine or keep the old one, but it would be great to be able to save it as new one.

Such a shame, I like the platform and the ability to search a workout however I would not pay for that or to save my workouts. If anything they should have made it so that you could save all your workouts but if you wanted the automatic PR goals to be saved then you upgrade to pro for better tracking of your best lifts. Too bad. It was a nice app while it lasted.

I really loved the app initially but as soon as I got to know that you can create only 3 custom workout templates, I was let down! I went on to see the prices for pro membership but they are way too high for a student like myself. They could have given atleast 6 workout templates in the free version, if not the other stuff. That would be really helpful.

Love this app. It's the easiest to use fitness app that I've ever tried. You can put it in "Halloween " mode which is like a souped up dark mode. And there is lots of flexibility with how you organize your workouts. The free version is more than enough but I'm interested in seeing some graphs 📊 to track my strength improvements, so will try the Pro version.

Amazing app! Supports custom workouts. If you know what program you're going to be using then customize it and you're on your way. Very easy to change things on the go. Only thing it needs is backups, in case I lose my phone or something like that. I'd like to be able to get all my workout logs back. Edit: July 2021, app is still awesome

Is ok but missing one key element. You can create your own workout routines but cannot group them into a program. No good if you have a number of different programs that you use for weeks at a time before changing. Can only save workouts rather than whole programs like other apps. This means you can have many workouts saved for many different programs which all end up mixed together.

Been using it for about half a year now, strongly recommend it! Also I love that you can export the workouts to a csv file! Just one thing: the weight and reps of a set can only be edited once the set is marked as finished, otherwise the functionality is disabled. It would be nice if the weight and number of reps of a set could be edited at all times.

The app itself is great, but I don't think the cost is justifiable. I've been on a monthly subscription for a few months and have just cancelled. There are similar tools that achieve the same thing for far less. In terms of the app itself, it's quite good and probably the best designed ui I've seen for strength training. The lack of integration with Garmin watches is disappointing though, and at this price point I'd really expect it to integrate with everything.

In general I like the app. It isn't such a big deal having to manually add exersises on a training day because of the 3 session templates limit. What I'd like to see is the option to add an exercise category with the name of 'progressive calisthenics' or something like that istead of having to create an exercise with the name of each progression or adding a note to the exercise with the progression you used on each set. Particularly useful if you work on things like planche or front lever.

this app has been a game changer for me. I now have consistency and am seeing results thanks to this app. it's easy to use and has everything you need for great strength sessions. it's so great I'm going for the pro version.

Was a pretty good app with restrictions in the free part. I loved the update until it signed me out, in the middle of a workout, and I couldn't get back in. I followed all the instructions, as apparently my password was no longer good, and awaited an email with a code, but it never came. Disappointed in the support for the app.

Even better with the new update. UI is great. Takes a little while to get used to using all the features available but I'm glad they're all there. A few glitches with 2.0, I found when replacing, editing exercises or adding notes sometimes it doesn't refresh until I scroll (pro version, note 8) but not really an issue. I like the additional filters when searching for exercises though would prefer 2 drop down menus than a button. I don't often bother to review apps. This one is truly worthy of 5*

Probably the best app I ve ever used for gym. It has everything you need to create your own workouts including dropsets, supersets, sets to failure and more. You can easily track your reps and sets for every exercise and you also have the dara from the previous workouts so you can compare them and improve everyday. You can also track your weight, measurements for every body part calories and more. The only thing I would like is to give some pro tools for free or drop the price. LOVE IT!!!!!!

I Love It!! I'm much more motivated for training since I've got this app. I've spent a plenty of time searching for perfect app because every app had adds (and I can't stand them). Also, the esthetic is great and you can switch to night mode. Tracking success is very important for me and it pushes me to do more so this app helped me a lot. However, it would be good to have some crossfit exercises, although users can add new workouts so that's ok. Thank you for making this application!

Really good fitness tracker. It would be nice if there were an optional note-taking feature at the end of each workout that is displayed as a small snippet in history. It would be a good feature because you can note what you may have done differently in this workout and how well the workout went because you did x.

its a very good app. the only thing is that I am not sure how to end a workout without it counting as a finished workout because I showed someone it but then I didn't know how to end it so I still have it running

Really great app, no ads and the free version works great. I payed for the premium cause it was that good and helpful. It would be lovely if they added a feature where you can log progress pictures, like you do with weight and measurements. Also the ability to clear an exercises history. I logged the wrong weight for a long time for a particular exercise so now the chart looks like it's going on a negative curve. Otherwise this is a great app, 10/10 would recommend.

It's billed as a 'free' app with 'in-app' purchases. The free version is basically useless and, before you even start a workout, you'll be purchasing the 'subscription' version. The paid version is good for the most part. However it is absolutely not worth the offensive monthly subscription. If you're a mug, go ahead and buy this app. There are free (or onetime purchase) apps available that are just as good though.

As a beginner/intermediate in the gym, (who is recovering from some injuries and a 3 year period of rarely working out) I thought this was so good. So easy to create and amend exercises/routines, and record your performance as you go. I've been using it for about 3 weeks now and I think it's helping me to stay engaged in the gym. This week I've found myself looking for any excuse to go to the gym, even for 30 mins, to try to record a new PR. Hopefully that feeling continues 🙏

I like it. Replaced my gympad. Just input your own workouts, go do them, and put in your results. Job done. Update : Actually, I love this app. Really helping me to track my workouts and see progress. I have one request... Could you please make an app for the Samsung Galaxy Watch. Maybe something that syncs with the app on the phone and shows the rest timer and information about the set you are currently doing. That would be great!

Without doubt the best app I have ever purchased. I've been using Strong for nearly a decade now; all my progress, all my eccentric workouts, all my efforts duly tracked and analysed - I rely on this so so much. Even after all these years the Devs keep adding more and more, the most recent updates with times between sets, adding in drop sets etc has been excellent. 5/5 💪🏻🥰

Good app, but not without a few drawbacks. slight annoyance with skipped sets; if you have any outstanding sets to do when you finish your routine, the sets you missed are deleted. This causes a lot of buggering about in routines where i alternate the sets weekly, as if I dont remember to check them off my list, they get deleted. Aside from this, all good

Very clear and has most of the features for strength training. A little confusing at first, but with a little playing comes good. The only feature I'd really love would be a 'next workout' feature - some sort of scheduling feature, perhaps a way you can assign certain days with certain workouts. Keep up the developing, stay jazzy.

Really good work out app and I love how intuitive everything is. However, the free version is too limited to be useful and the paid version is way too expensive.$7 per month or $40 per year(after 50% discount) is too much for a workout app. Slash the prices to half of that and I'd happily subscribe.
the app has made my life so much easier by tracking my progress and allowing me to make changes to my routine. love it!!!

Last update wiped out all my data initially, but received steady support from the support Team and after a few mails, they've managed to restore my data (3+ years of workouts). Still, would be extremely helpful if the app had a "remember password" functionality or at least sent a mail when creating an account - wasn't sure this was the right account cause I couldn't find any mails from them in my mailbox, cause after 3 years of being logged in I wouldn't remember lol
Have to login in for every thing this days. Can't even have a guest mode anymore.

- The rest timer is broken. It fails to notify when the screen us off. If you open the phone it will ding at you then. - Any unused exercises in a workout are deleted when you update the template. This means you can't add any variation without having to constantly manually re-enter exercises already used. The only other option is to enter a false input in any unsued exercises as a place holder which skews your stats history. There is no option to save new exercises without also deleting.

no horizontal mode. dear Strong designers, have you ever tried in your life holding a tablet vertically? it's damn uncomfortable. That's what your app is bc of its unability to turn into horizontal. Then, exerdcisess are not in categories but in an endless list. Then, export of data is poor: while I can get my data out, it's essentially unusable. So, practically you own my data and not me. Then, no interface to the only useful calories tracker, Cronometer. Strong = Strong price + weak quality.

This is the ultimate working out app. I purchased it time ago and it's definitely well thought. Even when I do exercises in other apps like Freeletics I still come back to this to put my results. There is only one thing to improve and it is the number of exercises. It would be great if I could submit somewhere my exercises to have them in the core app for the future and adding some pictures for new ones. I can add them manually but they are not as cool and easy to spot as default ones

Another PRO user. I'm very happy with the app. It's helping me to keep consistent with my workouts (weights, sets and reps) and progress. I've worked out my whole life but tracking it is making a difference. One suggestion, the Auto Rest Timer popup after each set is invasive (most of the time I have to close it). I'd prefer it more user friendly (maybe the +/- 30 secs and skip buttons on the top without having to hide the while screen). Apart from that, great job!

Free function is good for recording workouts and progress. A better app is Hevy, has same free functions as this one, but shows some stats for free, like areas of body on a monthly chart and you can add pics of custom exercises. This app feels good, is smooth, easy to use and is a great and viable alternative to other gym trackers. The paid version looks to be reasonable value, although I have no idea what extra features are on the paid version of this app, it gives no hints.

Love this app, can create and build workouts as well as track my progress in weight and measurements. Use it everytime I go to the gym. Has a bunch of workouts pre programmed and if it doesn't have one you know you can create them yourself which makes it even better. The only downside is it currently does not have a Samsung galaxy watch app. Hopefully they add it to it soon cause I recommend this app to people constantly and that would just be another reason I can use to get them to download it.

The best gym app I've ever seen, by far. The ability to track what numbers you had in the previous days of training has helped immensely with figuring out how to safely push boundaries. And that goes equally well with my current mentality that is to only compete with myself, which is the only fair comparison you can have. All the essential features which you need are there for free and even more features can be obtained for a small monthly fee. Recommend this app to both begginers and advanced

This app has transformed the way I work out. It has made it so much more easier to progressively overload, keep track of strength gains (and even potential deficits) as well as making it easy to keep track of rest times and total workout time. Adding this app into my gym time has made it more streamline and easier for me to get straight into the workout which has overall made the gym a more enjoyable experience.

App works great again, updated Android 9, now history tab no longer crashes app. Excellent app for tracking my workouts. Being new to the gym it keeps me on track with each workout so i dont forget what im doing. Sharing each workout makes it easier for my trainer to keep tabs on my progress. App layout and features were simple to use, allowing me to quickly learn Strong and implement it into my time at the gym without being distracing.

One thing I enjoy about the app is it's simplicity. No annoying ads or navigating around to find your workout. However, it could do with some improvements, especially if i am to pay for it. For instance, it lacks exercises in comparison to other apps. The exercise itself could be explained and demonstrated, clearly highlighting targeted muscles. Perhaps a clip of the movements for those who are more novice. Slight alterations so it is more appealing would make this a good app

I am using the app for around half an year and it is one of best apps in terms of user experience. It has almost everything what I would like in a workout tracker app. I also like new UI, but I think that it would be good to have old pop up timer as an option (although I like the new look also). And finally accurate 1RM formulas 🥳 I would also like to see something like calculator with weight-reps conversion (like 50x10 is close to 45x14, that kind of thing)

App crashed, lost all my data. I've been using this app for a couple years and have been loving it, but yesterday it suddenly crashed and logged me out and I have been unable to log in. It keeps saying my password is wrong, so I try reset the password but I'm not getting a password reset link. I've tried every different email I own and nothing works. All my history/data/records set over months and months of hard work is all gone. So sad and frustrating! 😭

great app, been using it for almost 2 years. clean interface with the option to add your own exercises which is handy if you want to use it to track physiotherapy work in addition to typical gym stuff. my only problem right now after switching from apple to android is that sets dont autofill weight/reps when you input something on one set, it makes you type the stuff out for every single one. kinda annoying but not a deal breaker i suppose.

I used to have to hand write my notes and keep referring back to it/use memory when doing my workouts. This cuts that time and effort out. Super easy to track reps and sets and has a great range of workouts. Would be great if there was an option to capture static holds e.g. when holding a chin up at the top, or hanging etc. Not a complaint but an upgrade to think about. Would also be good to have "half exercises" e.g. negative chin ups for those working on building up to doing a full chin up

Been using for over a year, it's great except for a few weird things. Example: the other day it recorded the total weight of my workout as over 11,000 kilos. No way I lifted that much! Esp since half that workout was abdominal work. Also, lately it won't let me share a completed workout via messenger. Logging some things like loaded carries is hard. Can't do both weight and duration. Considering switching, but if these could be fixed I'd keep it.

Best all-round workout tracker I've tried over several years. Really simple to use and a bunch of useful features I like such as setting drop and fail sets. The history is great and allows you to keep tabs on your progress, and the fact you can restart workouts gets around the limited number of free templates. Would be good if you could use swipe gestures to adjust values, because the numerical input is glitchy and upside down 🙃 This, and the expensive subscription stop it from getting 5 stars.

A flawless app for tracking my weights program. It has all the functionality you could need and nothing unnecessary. Some personal highlights are: - Showing weight and reps from previous workouts and easily applying it to the same exercise in the current workout - Easy-to-make dashboard with meaningful measurements to chart - Easy to build templates - Rest timers, with a handy tone at the end of the allotted time, which can easily be extended in the moment, or skipped. Top app, thanks very much!

I've used the app for about 2 years now. It being based on an account means transferring data to a new phone is simple. Graphing analytics make so progress is easy to interpret for lifts, cardio, and body metrics. Frequent updates tend to improve my experience. Easy to use. I'd like a way to start a counting timer for cardio exercises that counts up instead of down like the rest timer. Premium is worth it
Hi , great app. up until recently it crashes frequently, as of the last week. Display pictures will not upload either. Help! I pay for this too!
everything you need in a workout app without anything you don't. only thing it desperately needs is a wearOS companion app, with that it would be a 5 star app with no reservations!!
This app is right to the point . Very flexible to adjust your template as you go. easily swap exercises, add sets and reps. good logging and tracking capabilities.

Update: Removing 1 star, down to 2 stars now. Has a "Focus Metric" for total volume option but it is available only on the Apple platform as per their support docs. Don't understand why a simple total volume calculation for each exercise cannot be something I can track. This in addition to no RPE, RIR, and other features that are being added to competing apps it makes no sense for me to continue my subscription.

Great app, but imo really falls short on charts / statistics. Would love to see more there. (Workouts per month, year, improved charts for current options, ...). Also would really really appreciate watch support to add those values while working out without using my big phone. The app isn't cheap and I would expect feature updates therefore. Really hope so af leaat, or i might switch to another app with time. But so far I love the app and hope of stays my app to track workouts..

Fantastic at what it does! Unfortunately the prices for premium are absurd. I'd pay a dollar per month to be able to make more workout templates but I'm not paying 7... I love that it will sync data woth Google fit, I also like the total weight moved summary it's a great indicator of progress, even if my sets feel like I'm not progressing I can always see some improvement in the total volume and that's a huge motivator. On the down side, let me make more than 3 workout templates cheaper please

Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra I had already purchased the foever option when i downloaded the app last year. The app worked absolutely fine before this update. Now I have lost all my workouts, all my history, all the data gone. I can't access the full app to log all my workouts again or even re- purchased the forever option as I already have it. Talk about break something that didn't need fixing is an understatement. Emailed the issue and still waiting for resolution.

Consistently useful. I find the layout easy to maneuver and get familiar with. Strong is my preferred workout tracker app. However, I do have a wish list item - can the app have a version that can be downloaded on one's smart watch? It would be easier than carrying a phone around. Also - it would be great to be able to upload plans via Excel or text file.

I've been using the free version a good while now and I love it. Really nothing wrong with it. If you can't find the specific exercise you want there's an option to create it. You can select different set types, and a fun bonus is that the little tune that plays when you finish a workout somehow goes perfect with the drop of all my metal songs!

Couldn't be more happy with this app. User Interface is easy to understand and utilise; streamline with no unnecessary additions. Helps keep you accountable for each training session and tracks all forms of progressive overload. Super easy to enter data and convert mesurements from pounds to kilos. Highly recommend. Amazing app.

Using about a year now along side a bunch of others, this is the most intuitive and least clunky app I've found. No constant Temu ads (ads I'm okay with, Temu I'm not). A running total lifted during the workout would be a great addition and make the overall workout time a bit more visible. Definitely needs more exercises added.

Search function needs work. Pressing back after selecting an exercise I've searched takes you back to the original blank search screen, it should take you to the list of results for what I just searched. This means that when Im researching exercises, I'm forced to type the same thing(eg, Lat,back, etc) like 5 times just to compare workouts. Pressing back button(the apps arrow or phones back button) should bring me back to what I just searched, & a 2nd press takes you to the blank search screen

A great app, really helps tracking/planning workouts. Also really beginner friendly when trying to figure out what you're doing in the gym. You can create exercises if necessary. UI is nice to look at but not complicated to use. I wish you could delete sets from a workout Instead of having to remove the exercise and re add it. Also I would love more choices of apps to sync with. Specifically Samsung health and Lifsum. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED. The premium membership is worth it in my opinion.

Haven't used it all too much but it seems like a good app, other than the fact that you can only create 3 workout templates without subscribing to Strong Pro. I don't think this will be a problem for everyone, but I do tend to struggle with this as the pre-made templates do not have the same things as my workouts do.

I like it, I can create a few custom plans. But the full version is too pricey (124$ cnd for forever or you pay 6.50$ monthly). I might just go back to using a pen and paper. With the free version, there is a limited to the number of training plans you can store. I workout for a sport so I need to do periodization and would need 10-11 workouts each year. I could see paying 50$.
love the app but I wish there was a way to track half reps as well so it could look like 1.5 reps to track rep prs

this app must be really helpful, if only it would actually allow me to make an account!! have tried multiple times with different emails, tried using my Google account and Facebook account but no luck. it keeps throwing errors when I first go to make an account, then when I try again it says the email is already in use?? even though the account didnt successfully get made due to the errors it keeps throwing. unfortunately I had to give up and delete the app.

Perfect in every way, but you have to give an arm and a leg to have more than 3 workouts. What does this app offer that's worth $6 a month? I can Google exercise on the very device the app is on and I can buy a year's worth of notebooks and pens for $6. Having to delete and re-input makes it lose the convenience it had over physical notebooks.

I'm a PT and I literally recommend this to all my clients. It's actually so much easier to write them a plan and send it to them because it tracks, it's has explanations of each exercise, it's easy to use and no longer do people have to walk around with pen and paper to track stuff. Out of all the various apps I've tried, this is easily the best for trainers and average gym goers

This app is amazing I love how I don't have to think when doing my work out. The plate counter makes racking weights when your not in the mind set of counting easy. It's a crying shame there isn't a companion app for wear OS. I wish I could not have my phone on me at all. If any competitors do. I'm jumping on their app.

Simple, user friendly, effective. Regular gym goers want something they can continue to use that displays their stats. More complicated apps require a coach to send you programmes and once you've finished you either pay again or lose your access to the app. Templates save time so you can set up your training cycle and run it for a few weeks no issues. RPE is trackable and programmable the same as ideal weights to hit. The exercise bank is limited but you can add custom exercises.

Very good app for tracking progress. Not too difficult to set up. You can create your own workouts which is ideal when following a specific programme. The data is stored so when you revisit the workout you can add more weight or reps to better your previous workout. This is one of the better apps out there.

Absolutely fantastic app. It's really obvious that the developers work out themselves, because it has almost everything you'd want before, during and after a workout! I would like to request a feature to really enhance the app: the option to use reps-in-reserve (RIR) as an alternative to RPE. It's pretty easy to calculate 10-minus-RPE to get RIR, but for someone who only uses RIR, it would be nice to just have that instead rather than do the mental calculation every time. Thank you to the Strong team!

I do like this app, it's very good. I have only had a look at the free version and set myself some workouts. I like the variation and how it has images showing you what to do. My only criticism is not being able to add your own exercises. I wanted to add tricep kickbacks but I cannot see the option to do this anywhere. I figured I could add my own & name it, but you can't. It's a very simple feature you'd think this app would have; not sure why it doesn't have it. There are no forearm workouts!

So far so good!! I've only had this app 24hrs and have already signed up to the paid version to enable me to create extra more of my own workouts. It does EXACTLY what is says on the tin. Everytime I think 'oh it would be good if the app did this...' I realise it already does it, and more. No glitches, runs perfectly. No brainer.

Great for logging workouts. The only thing that would make this better, is if it could create a program for you such as the popular Wendler 531 strength program day by day. It's great for logging "as you go". Would love to see the ability for this app to create the popular work out programs for you as well as tracking them. You can use the Internet to calculate the program then manually input each day of the program but it takes a significant amount of time and steps.

Been using this app for a year now. Great for creating, logging and timing your progress. The best weight training app out there. Fantastic customer support too as I inadvertently recorded some PB's wrong and then made a real mess by deleting the workout. Emailed customer support and Catharine got straight back to me and resolved my issues. She also gave me a pat on the back on my progress. 5🌟 app with ease! UPDATE: This apps customer support is first class. Catharine helped me again 👍

Cleanest? Yeah, probably. It is very clean and I love a clean app. Beautiful. As for functionality, it does what you want it to do. Create your workout and track your progress over time. Edits can be made easily during a workout. Weight calculator and rest timer are very useful.

I really like this app. I've been using it for well over year now. One aspect I particularly like is being able to add your own exercises. I do regular physiotherapy and like to track that along with my exercises. Adding that I would like it if I were able attach pictures of my physiotherapy exercises when I add them.
top app, I've had it since I started working out, and even without paying its really a good app... goated

love it, can be improved. for example add drop-set or add an icon on each exercice when it shows up during the training. English is not my mother tongue, so it can help when i get a doubt of what workout i planned to perform. BTW, I purchase the app .I can try now to do more each session. I'm going in different place and i use different brand of machine, that change for the stats, pls find a way to make a difference between machines brand.Connect to myfitnesspal and garmin will be really great

Amazing! App is great all round, easy to use, flexible not at all pushy for premium version. Used free version for over a year. Premium offers a few nice features which honestly i dont care for but i felt it was only fair to pay something for an app i love. I cannot fault the app's simple & clean UI but the functionality (which is ideal for its purpose) doesn't justify the cost for me. I respect the work thats gone in and will continue to ocassionally subscribe to give something back.

Hell yeah! Everything you needed and dreamt about in a workout diary. No unnecessary fluff, simple, clear, wonderful. Suggestions to make it exceptional: - allow adding header background colour to the routines, so we can easily identify the one we are looking for - add extra view where routine boxes/blocks are much smaller, so 8-12 are visible on one screen - add history view not only for exercises, but also for routines (at least dates when performed and total time) Keep killing it 💪
Good, but could be better. There is no way to add details for custom exercises. Very annoying

I've been using strong for 3 years and always had my data exported to CSV as a backup. I was quite surprised that there is no import functionality at all. I had lost my data lately for an unknown reason and wasn't too stressed about it as I knew I had my backups. But then realized there is absolutely no way to import this data back. I have now switched to the Hevy app and most likely going to purchase it as the Hevy app allows you to import Strong data, which I was able to do.

I started using the iOS version of this app and switched to an Android phone recently. For the most part, the app looks great there as well. However, the lack of folders to organize templates in the main Workout screen is just terrible. All the workouts I created and organized in different folders in iOS are now lumped under "My templates" and I need to scroll through all of them to find a specific one. This simple feature has a tremendous benefit on UX and is missing in the Android version

Absolute best App, I'm using the PRO and very happy with it. (UPDATE) I switched to another one for some months because they had custom exercises (and Strong didn't). But, missing Strong, I checked it out again to see if they had already implemented custom exercises. AND THEY HAVE! So now I have my perfect App for life. Thanks Strong Team!

Pay the $100. It does everything. The only thing it's missing is 12 week training programs. It takes a little time setting up, and figuring things out but once you get the hang of it. It works flawlessly. For example: I'm doing reps of 12,10,8,6. After I finish a workout and press finish. I can use the keep template save values option to track and log my weights and reps so the next time I do that workout. I can see where I need to increase or decrease to meet my rep goals. Good luck.

Was my favorite gym app, clean and simple to use. The last update, while improving the look, ruined the experience! Adding excercises - instead of having a floating button available everywhere you have to scroll all the way down to find the button Finding excercises - instead of having a convenient list of filters at the top of the list, you have to tap a weird icon and go back to see the effects. There is also no star filter like the old version I hope these issues and more will be solved soon

Good app, bad price. Been using the free version for a few months now. It's very handy, makes following my routine easy and tracking gains simple. Id like to go Pro, however... the price point is ridiculous: even at 50% off it's $40/year, and a permanent license is $125. I'd seriously consider buying at half that price point.

Love the app I've bought all the add ons for it on iOS and have kept using it even on my move to andriod for almost 4 years now (over 700 logged workouts). If I had to make one suggestion, I'd like the app to have a search bar in the workout tab so I can quickly search through the workouts I've put together over the years. I've got around 40 or so different workouts saved and a bunch of non default exercises and the app is starting to chug quite a bit.

EDIT: Three years later and still Android users do not have simple straight forward folders. Seems like the team is only focusing on iOS ----------- Everything is great EXCEPT for the fact that you can't create folders for your routines. For example, it would be nice if we can create a folder called "Upper/Lower", and then within this folder, we can create our workout routines. Currently it will show ALL your routines in a giant list.

I've been lifting for around 30 years. This app does what many others do, but with a streamlined interface optimized for strength training. The ability to customize workouts is solid, the ability to prescribe sets and loads is solid, and the tracking mechanics are solid. Everything you need, and in a smooth interface. Love it!

Key feature like progress graphing are locked behind a very expensive paywall. If the paid version was a 1-time fee in the neighborhood of $10-15, then that would be excusable. But the 1-time fee is almost $100. Ridiculous. A monthly subscription is similarly ridiculously prices ($5/month) for an app that doesn't even feature a website interface or cloud functionality.

Been in gym in and out. But I do love how the app just ready for when you go back at it. I also love the compatibility of dark mode and the timer vibrate and noise that will sound over music you may be playing. Can't wait for the 6.0. Hopefully, they'll add support to some more exercises and machines.

Best way to track your lifts. I love this for me. It's giving scienctific lifter. I slayed those lifts. I just love training and tracking my lifts on this app!!! 😀 I can't believe what I did in the gym before this app. With this app I can finally have a reason to conduct thorough science and research on my body. I love to track my weights and reps and sets in this app! Very good time for my gym session and data. English not my first language.

Only had it about 3 days now but it is exactly what I wanted with no extra BS, easy to record sets, I organize my workout the night before, then at the gym I just input the weight and number of reps. At the end of the workout, it calculates the total weight moved (I never paid attention to that number before but now it's a new goal and motivation) & PR's. The system has a great library of workouts as well as sample workouts like leg day, back day, arms day or you can build your own. Good stuff!

I looked through plenty of apps before finding this. I just wanted a simple app that is quick to update in the gym without messing about. This is it. There are plenty of exercises and it's quick and easy to add a log while you're working out. So you can copy and increase your workout with only a couple of clicks.

Really good app to track all your workouts, the free version provides all the services you need, and if you want to gain more insight into your performance and the ability to manage more custom workouts in advance, then it's a great cost effective subscription. Only thing that I would like to see from this app is to create a smart watch app for android Wear OS so I can manage my workout on my watch without having to go back to my phone after each set.

Most fluid, user friendly fitness tracker. Pro user here, went from 310lbs to 235lbs with the help of this app. Switched from IPhone to ANDROID, Initially thought I would lose all my info and pro status switching over (Have logged over 300 workouts). Fortunately, the support team was able to help recover the account. While I didn't have access, I tried some of the other apps. Strong is the best one as far as I'm concerned. Looking forward to log 300+ MORE! Love the plate calculator.

extremely straightforward. quickest setup i could ever imagine (i downloaded it at the gym and started using it immediately). been using it for a couple of months for basic apartment complex gym sessions (dumbells, machines). as others have mentioned, it could use more exercise options. also, the rest timer could do a better job of remembering different rest times between reps vs sets.

Absolutely the best workout tracker I have used and I have used many. This one does not bore you with loads of useless garbage but gets straight to the point of the app: 1) help you build a workout 2) track the workouts 3) Show you how to do the exercises in case you forgot. Simple and efficient this app is worthy of five stars. Take time to learn to use it and it is as easy as pie. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!

Absolute best app for tracking workouts. You can add your own excercises, do supersets and save workouts for next time. This app has helped me with progressive overload thanks to the ability to see past weights easily. I really cannot recommend this app enough, it's the best way to keep track of excercises. There is a small pay wall but there's honestly minimal need to pay.

• You need to pay a ridiculous amount of money to get more than 3 workout templates. You can't buy them separately which is a big issue. I only want unlimited templates and I wouldn't consider paying 100€ just for them. • The app is great and I would gladly pay a reasonable price but the template limitation is ridiculous and ruins the whole app if you need more templates. I suggest either dropping this crippling limitation or adding the option to purchase the templates separately.

I paid for the full version of this app because I had used it on IOS and loved it. It's disappointing to see that some simple improvements are not available on the android version. For example the rest timer only has an option to extend or reduce the time by 30 seconds at a time rather than 10 and you can't add charts for measurements, including body weight, to the main profile. This would be a super simple fix! Would rate 5 stars otherwise.

Started out fine, 1st 3 workouts was able to log workouts, was exactly what I was looking for. I was considering going premium. On the 4th it failed to recognize when a set was completed, and therefore wouldn't log the workout. which was the purpose of the app. deleted the app. Back to manual logging.

Great App for tracking workouts, would be great if it could be accessed on a laptop also - so you could input workouts easier. Also it would be good if there was a central collection of workouts from Bodybuilders etc that you could download / pay to download if the app needs an incentive to implement these things.
only 2 templates possible to save in free version. Very limited functionality. Lots of missing excercises
i tried couple of apps and this one is best for me. Even the free version is great and pro is for very reasonable price
Great app and free no bugs lots of exercises and if it dosent have one you can make it

Strong has been awesome. The anxiety of going to the gym for the first time was handled well by this app. The auto rest timer and the ease of use are awesome. It also has tons of exercises to choose from, with animations and directions for most of them. Helps take the guess work out of it and keeps you informed of your progress.

I do enjoy the new update for the most part but do agree with Collin's review and hope that will be fixed. Additionally, it would be nice to have volume and weight tracking for high rep set/low rep sets. If I am am doing bicep curls for weight, and then the same exercise for volume for example, it skews my logs for reps, volume, weight into one jumbled mess.

Great app, easy to use, and definitely the timer comes handy. But as a subscription in order to log in more than 3 days is for me an unfair deal, especially when there are other equally good apps. A one-time purchase would've made me a regular user, now it went straight to uninstall.

Great free app. Wish some of the premium features were free but can't fault them for wanting to make money. Also wish the full version wasn't so expensive. Can't find a real fault though with the free app except it doesn't seem to easily allow for accounting for missed reps. Sorta wish I could input that the planned set had x reps but that I only achieved y at that weight. Easy to get around though

it's the best app for tracking your workouts. you can find pretty much any exercise, even multiple variations and record sets, reps, wt., look up history. the best feature by far has to be the export data feature that lets you dump it in excel and look at trends, history, etc.

good for tracking workouts! there is a bug on android (samsung galaxy s24) where if you are adding an exercise and have the search bar open with the keyboard, it doesn't suggest workouts based on what you're typing unless you back out of the search bar and open the search again

I honestly liked the old design better, but that's alright, I'll get used to it... What I won't get used to is the new jingles, how dare u guys change those one up. I am conditioned to them by now and feel incomplete without hearing the old set complete sound. #bringbackoldjingles! Otherwise great work, keep it up!

Seems like a great APP however, they don't seem to want my money. I m trying to pay for a subscription but I keep getting an error that my card is being declined. I am using multiple cards all which have enough money on them and none of them show any declined charges. I looked for a more discrete way to communicate with the makers of Strong - Workout Tracker Gym Log but, I can't find any contact information.
Your choice to ruin a good workout app should be studied.

Here's what I like: - It has a really good list of different exercises (bodyweight, dumbbell, machine, etc.) - You can sort them by the muscle group, - Each exercise has a picture/animation/text to instruct you on proper form (very helpful), - It let's you record your own custom routines (exercises, sets, reps) - It looks great and it is easy to use Here's what I don't like: ... ... ... (This is my entire review, there's literally nothing I can think of that I don't like about Strong) Great app

This is by far the best tracker I've used. Its help me manage my training program and closely watch progressive overload. 100% reccomend paid version. Docking one star Only for one thing- they can work on is the superset functionality. I have a hard time linking excercises to a superset. Other than that, 10 out of 10 for me

I really like this tracker. It's easy to use and exactly what I was looking for. I was wondering if it is possible to have additional exercise variations. I like to do dumbbell curls with an EZ-bar but there is no variation for this (but there is a variation for a hex-bar deadlift) Just a minor request, but otherwise a great app. Would recommend.
oh hey the only feature i wanted to use is for premium members only and it took me a full five minutes of onboarding to find that out! easy delete xoxo
worked perfectly for me for workout log and progressive overload. interval timer options are the hidden gem.

3 years worth of data gone. I'm not able to access my data even though I tried to log back in with the same account. Absolutely infuriating. I recommend getting the app, HEVY instead. Much better UI, better integration with smart devices/other fitness tracking apps, and more frequent updates. The premium service is also cheaper than Strong.

You can only add 3 workout templates before you hit the pay wall. So could not add my 5 days of workouts to put the app through its paces. Most of the app is locked down. It did at least synch and add the workout set I tested to Google Fit. Not worth the price in my opinion. There are much better apps with more features available for less price.

really disappointed with recent updates, you changed the way timers work and now I have to enter in timers for each exercise, I liked the other way where I put a timer and it effects everything. if I'm on chest day like I'm on today, and I went to add push-ups and now it's harder to navigate through the exercises to pick what I want to add.. the old way much better. I like the new screen look or background but that's only about it. need to give us options to customize it the way we want.

Been a long user of this app. Recently resubscribed after a break. I am unable to change my status from free to subscription. In otherwords I am paying for no features. Contacted customer support, no answer. It's a shame as the app is great, but I don't want to pay for something I can't use the features for. The one star rating for bad customer service.

Just started the subscription model. Already doesn't appear to be worth it. It says it will let you choose your plates, but the choices are REALLY limited, half my plates aren't shown and I see no way to enter them. In addition, the "automatic warmup sets" doesn't appear to work. For example 50% of 76lbs is not 45lbs. Free version is great for beginners, but I don't think I'll keep the pay version.

Latest update deleted my account with 660+ workouts over 3+ years. I went to the profile page (where there is a "danger zone" to delete your account) and immediately a loading bar spun. I was signed out and can't get back into the account with email or google which was linked. it just makes a new account. It's gone, gee thanks.

The program does not allow me to delete the real template and just edit it. if I just accidentally added an approach, then I can't change it. There is no way to fix your program in the application if there are errors.
unable to login with Facebook and I tried to reset password with email but email never sent. so unsure how to login since I pay for a subscription.
notification sound is Messing with the system sound. only way to restore the volume is to restart Spotify. it's rather annoying to restart Spotify 10x each Work out

I've used this app for over three years and have been hugely impressed by the free version and the intuitive interface. Which makes the recent developments all the more disappointing. I turned on today to see a notification about the Strong 6.0 release. Some cosmetic changes, but when I came to record my workout, it was completely unusable and buggy. It wouldn't even let my type reps and weights in the correct box! So frustrating, I had to cancel my workout. Please fix soon!!

When I first installed the app. It was really nice! There would be visual displays of how to do a workout. But since the update, it asked me to login and all my previous workouts were lost and my routines were lost as well. All the visuals have disappeared. You can't select the measurement to be set as metric and have to manually change the weight for each exercise. If updates are to improve the app, the updates have ruined this app. I'm considering uninstalling it and switching.

I really loved the app initially but as soon as I got to know that you can create only 3 custom workout templates, I was let down! I went on to see the prices for pro membership but they are way too high for a student like myself. They could have given atleast 6 workout templates in the free version, if not the other stuff. That would be really helpful.

no horizontal mode. dear Strong designers, have you ever tried in your life holding a tablet vertically? it's damn uncomfortable. That's what your app is bc of its unability to turn into horizontal. Then, exerdcisess are not in categories but in an endless list. Then, export of data is poor: while I can get my data out, it's essentially unusable. So, practically you own my data and not me. Then, no interface to the only useful calories tracker, Cronometer. Strong = Strong price + weak quality.

Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra I had already purchased the foever option when i downloaded the app last year. The app worked absolutely fine before this update. Now I have lost all my workouts, all my history, all the data gone. I can't access the full app to log all my workouts again or even re- purchased the forever option as I already have it. Talk about break something that didn't need fixing is an understatement. Emailed the issue and still waiting for resolution.

this app must be really helpful, if only it would actually allow me to make an account!! have tried multiple times with different emails, tried using my Google account and Facebook account but no luck. it keeps throwing errors when I first go to make an account, then when I try again it says the email is already in use?? even though the account didnt successfully get made due to the errors it keeps throwing. unfortunately I had to give up and delete the app.
