Don't bother if you're on Android. I've been using this app for over two years, waiting for them to add the most basic feature any programmer could throw together in two hours. GIVE ME FOLDERS TO ORGANIZE MY TEMPLATES. You've had this feature for YEARS on iPhone, I know you know how to do it. Let us Android users be able to create Template folders so I don't have to scroll through 30 different workouts every day to find what I need.

Cannot login to my account and customer service is non-existent. Signed up for this app via Google play, but apparently my email was never saved in the process, so I can't login to my account from any other device; only the original device. No reply from customer service. Saw others online have had the same issue and the devs are aware, since they replied to them, but no fix or solution has ever been offered. Awful experience, cannot recommend.

Before the new update 4.5/5, best work out tracker I even bought the pro. After update lags after every input, can't delete exercises, workouts are doubled when recorded and finished. All around terrible app until they fix all the new bugs. Literally every free workout tracker is better than this one right now.

Only allows 3 custom workouts. I run a 6 day PPL... Took my time setting up 3 workouts only to hit an ad telling me to upgrade when I tried creating the 4th workout. Ridiculous. Either allow us to create a program with multiple days or allow more workouts to be created. I've tried several fitness apps and this is the only one that limits you like this. If I could give zero stars I would.

Started using this, spending time to create my workouts, when I got to the end of the week and tried to save my last workout I found out I was blocked from doing so unless I paid for the full version. So, wasted my time wasted a week of my time, have to start again with another app. I might pay for it if I can actually try it out longer than a few days!

I will start by saying this is a wonderful app, and it seems to do most of the job I ask it too. But I have a few issues with it. One, if I have a sick day and I move less weight that week, it can really mess with my tracking because it's not easy to see what the record-before-the-last-record is. Two, if you ever want just three routines, it works well. If you end up in the middle of a pandemic and you want to start tracking home exercises be prepared to fork out a bunch of money for premium.

I started my inquiry with them on 11/29/2021 Because I had purchased their propackage on their app. It took them until 2/1/2022 to fix the problem. Half of our email correspondence was the company repeating the same troubleshooting steps for weeks. I was given the explanation that the error occurred on their side and was not offered anything anything for the 2 months of incompetence.

Can someone tell me how do I delete sets on android? I have added 5 squat sets and another 4 by mistake. Even though they are empty I can't delete them. I have OCD and I can't go in the main screen and see that I have 10 sets of squats. That is ridiculous... I only have premium for 2 days... imagine if I do the mistake and tap to add more sets... I will end up showing up 30 sets for bench press. Absolutely ridiculous. There is this function in IOS.

Was looking for a good log app, but this one needs Facebook or sign up start. EDIT: I decided to sign up and take a look, very good app in general but since the free version is limited to three routines it is useless for free users. Don't bother if you are looking for a free app, there are better options out there.

Really buggy. Even if I finish a workout the next day I go back to the app and it's still recording time. Adding rest time as a default is super annoying, have to go back and change it every time I add a new exercise. And now they try to solicit pro to me whenever I finish a workout. For a buggy app like this? I'll keep my money.

This used to be my favourite workout app; however, it now crashes whenever I try to alter an exercise (change weight, change reps, add a set, etc.) in my current workout. This renders the app pretty useless. I've a submitted bug report but I haven't heard anything to I'm putting this here hopes that the app gets fixed. If not, I'll have to find a new app to use.

A tracker that limits how many routines you can build via pay wall. Lame guys. Looked promising, but couldn't even program a full week just now. So I'm out. Charging for services on a monthly basis is cool. Though I'm not sure what, exactly, you guys are trying to sell. If it's literally just the app, there are free apps that offer excellent tracking. If this one is a step above? Cool, but it should be a one and done charge.

I really like the app but it's very elfish that you only get three templates. All I want to do is break up my workouts into six separate ones but you have to pay if you want more than three. I wouldn't mind paying $5-$10 but it is a monthly or yearly subscription. Just feels like too much.

My account was either deleted or stolen and there is zero recourse to fix it. Not even an email notification I was logged into another device, there was an attempt to change my email, or that my account was gone. When changing account details the original email should be notified for security. Lost years of workouts and a PRO subscription from lack of 2FA or basic security features.

Loved this app on ios. It worked great and I lived that I could create folders with multi day workouts. I have a bunch of different workouts with varying length in days. However recently switched to an Android and my fave feature of workout folders is gone. Just all a long listed chaos of my created templates. Now I have to sort through and get the right name for each set abs find a new app. Very frustrating.

You cannot use this app without creating an account. I'm interested in working out, not in joining yet another social platform. The developer justifies the need to log in so that you can keep your workouts synced between devices. That's nothing more than making an excuse in order to avoid change. I have used a few other workout apps that sync files between devices without requiring you to log in to their system (hint: Google Drive, iCloud Drive, Microsoft OneDrive). Excuses don't burn calories.

I originally gave this app a five-star review. I went ahead and paid the $4.99 a month, even though I only used the three workouts that come with basic membership. When I decided to take advantage of all the benefits of the $4.99 subscription, I found out that I couldn't take advantage of the membership because they never switched me over to a paid membership status. I reached out to them via email and never got a response. $4.99 isn't a total fortune, but still a waste of money.

Easily the worst tracking app for Android. Supersets are broken, missing simple exercises, can't add weights to certain exercises. I've read in places that the devs dont really care about Android, that's a definite possibility. This is what happens when tech bros pretend they're gym bros and vice versa. Leave the heavy lifting to the professionals (real and metaphorical).

Used paid/premium version for 3 years. Always used rest timers and they were great. Latest update added a linebreak showing rest timers between every set, cluttering the app/expanding out the set. They also no longer auto start when you mark a set complete, you need to mark it complete THEN start the rest timer. App was worsened considerably by an update

Limited to only 3 workouts that can be saved without spending over $100! This is absurd, and you're much better off looking elsewhere.  Doesn't have ads, but do you really want to dump $100 on an app available for free elsewhere, and ad-free much more cheaply.

This has been one of the absolute worst apps I have ever had the displeasure of using, especially after using the premium version. Workouts weren't saved, supersets were being deleted after creating them, names were constantly being changed, deleted, or altered in ways beyond my control. There is no option to create folders so you can have different styled workouts on different weeks. The number of reps and weights constantly gets added to the templates instead of the workouts. Do not use or pay

with a purpose of recording workout logs, it really shouldn't charge people to create their own workout routine. certainly unnecessary business model of monthly subscription charge in order to use its full feature of the app which is a note keeper. it's a shame. if one on one coach ing or coaching video are included, this will be a star for those who seek more than just handy notebook

Clunky app that is difficult to use, especially at the gym. You have to scroll through a ton of exercises just to find the one you want. The search function won't let you search by muscle group used. Worst of all you can't easily fill in the calendar with exercises you've done in the past.

Sorry for 1 star while everyone gives 5 star. The thing is there is only 2 type of charts available in this. Volume and rm. This comes with a price. Also if u forgot to log a day we cant log the previous days through calender. This is not a great app in my point of view. Apps like fitnotes and gymrun, they provide way more details and analysis. They have chats even for time, reps, sets, calories etc. They provide those for free.

Can't recommend it for a free user. Only being able to save 3 routines becomes an inconvenience, at which point you are better off using any of the other high quality apps out there. I can understand keeping advanced features behind a paywall, but this limitation is unfortunate. Unisntalling. 👎

I've tried really hard to like this app and even signed up for the one-month trial of the pro service. Unfortunately, the app isn't very intuitive to use and is extremely poorly documented. I've been trying for days to figure out how to use "supersets" for circuit training, and nothing I do makes it work the way I need it to. And I was expecting the cloud feature to let me sync between my phone and my tablet, so that I could use the tablet during the actual workout, but apparently completed workouts don't sync between devices.

2/7 v6.0.1 Update: latest version of app removed the ability to set different bars for exercises as well as removed the weight of the default bars so the plate load is completely incorrect for weight. EG Hip thrust default bar is barbell (set to 0lbs) where I had the ability to set it to ezcurl with the weight of 30lbs. So instead of #30 ezbar + #25 plate, it shows #0 barbell + #35 + #5

Incredibly annoyed. Seemed to be a cool app with all the features I wanted. So I spent 30+ minutes crafting 3 templates for my 4 day workout plan. But when I tried to add the fourth and final, it required me to pay $6 a month for membership. This makes the app completely useless to me. Uninstalled.

This app used to function great but suddenly one day (after 4 years of use) it signed me out, refused to accept my password or let me reset my password. Only way forward was to link my Google account, which I refused. I deleted and don't recommend downloading unless you want your account randomly pulled from you and have the app demand that you give more data. An absolute joke

Used to be really good but I been a paid subscriber for 3 years and they haven't implemented any new heavily requested features. And they've actually reduced some older features. They used to let you export your workout as a text file to any app but now it just takes a screen shot smh.

the logging is really easy and intuitive, love it , but I wish I could pay for individual features rather than a subscription. the only thing I need more than the free version is the exercise charts and the subscription price is way too much for that.

Really unintuitive. Just wanted to make lists for push, pull and leg days and there's just so much convoluted bs going around. Plus you have to make an account and after you open the app you can't log in it. even if you change the password. Absolutely useless

I've been using Strong for almost a decade. I paid for pro when it was fairly new and never looked back. It looks now I am no longer a pro member and cannot create custom workout templates. Not cool. I'm going to delete this app and move on.

CRASHED EVERY TIME I tried to add warm-up sets, there is no calendar or planner integration, no way of creating a plan that adds weight automatically, and finally, WAY TOO EXPENSIVE for what you're getting. Only being able to create three routines without paying EVERY MONTH is a joke.

The main reason was changing workout apps was because this one said I could sync my workouts and data across my devices. But after spending a long time planning and designing a new workout template, it WOULD NOT sync and then deleted all my hard work. I have canceled my yearly subscription, but in my opinion they owe me a refund for false advertising.

Causing Samsung A26 to lock up. This app worked well on my old Samsung S9+, but I recently transferred everything to a new A26 and now when I use this app the phone will lock up after locking and unlocking the screen. It seems to have consumed all the memory and after unlocking the phone while running this app the screen will jitter and become non responsive.

App is ok, but most people would probably need the features of the premium version. Unfortunately the price to upgrade is ridiculous. You can find equivalent and arguably better apps that are free or cheaper. Or hell, just get a notebook

TOO EXPENSIVE!!! with all of the other good workout apps out there, this one (even though it's nice) is way over priced. i could see maybe $20 per year max but $65 per year ($5.39x12) is rediculous. I'd rather go old school with a note pad than pay that each month

I do not see why I have to register just to use the app. I can't plan my workouts in advance, and when creating a workout it only allows me to add a set number for reps. I want to add a range for failure example 4-6 or 10-12 reps but doesn't allow me to do this.

It teases you with a very limited sample of basically your phones notes/calendar app. Just copy your routine into your calendar to track and you save yourself from another app needing a subscription for convenience features already available on your phone. How many times will you actually seek instructional videos? It's a tracking app.

Worked great for years. Recently updated. Timed me out and won't let me sign back in. Say username/password incorrect even though I have them written down. Tried resetting my password. why is it auto turning on YouTube music and turning off if I go out of the app? I don't see how to disable
would have liked to try it but it just keeps saying "an error occurred please try again later" when trying to create my account

The app USED TO work perfectly. I purchased the premium plan but for some reason I have not been able to access the premium features anymore, which is unfortunate. Update. This is ludacris. The premium plan has always been marketed as a one-time payment. I have purchased the premium plan at least 3x over.

Can't even look inside the app without registering. There are lots of moronic sheep around who give their personal info away without a thought but I'm sure not one of them. If you want my info, you have to give me something first. This app was uninstalled without going past the registration page. Poor show.

The experience was actually great. I was excited about putting down my sets and workouts and I just love the specificity. However, I was struck with ads that would slow down my phone and appear even when I'm not using the app. As a developer myself I can tell you that this was poorly managed and that they should either use a different type of ad or revise their timing for ads whilst keeping it off when the app is closed. It's a real shame because the system is set up so good.

Technical issues made this app terrible. I got a new phone and logged back in and now it kicks me out every time I go to use it. This was a great app but now my login info seems to be lost and it can't be recovered. Was great no longer works well

last update was in September. at some point in the last two months the automatic timer stopped working. and even when i manually set the timer sometimes it crashes without me knowing. rather than patch the problem theyre waiting for "the next big update" (as of me writing a month ago) but no bug fixes since september is disappointing for having paid for an app that was working great when i bought it

You have to pay £79 to add more than 3 routines, which makes it completely useless, unless you only ever workout 3 times a week and never switch up your routine. Wasted my time inputting my workouts before it told me I couldn't add a 4th one. Disappointing.
I had to pay for this twice because I forgot my login and nobody responded to my help email

I've used this app for a long time, love the tracking and format and constantly recommend it. When I logged back on all of my workout history was gone. Quite a few years of recorded workouts/prs, and the analytics and insight tracking have all been completely erased. Easy to use, but Pricey for full version. The free version is decent. 3 max for custom workout routines. All of the exercises have instructions and pictures, most have a short gif showing the motion. It is missing some exercies, but has most of the main ones (sometimes theyre named different from what I know) Full version is $99, just outrageous. Id probably pay $5. Seriously don't understand their price point.

This app looks promising. I rate it low because of the way they have chosen to charge. You can only save 3 routines with the free version. That doesn't give me much time decide if I want to pay for the full version. Limit the measuring, charting, and tracking. I'm fine with that. But if I only get to store 3 routines I'll be moving on to a different app before I get close to deciding if I like this one enough to pay the outrageous price for the pro version.

I used to have all the features they tout. But I broke my phone, got a new one and now I cannot even do a 5day workout routine. And I am not even sure of my account info as the errors I get trying to log in are not informative. The price is way up to $100 for lifetime. . .it a good app but not at that price. . .especially when you cannot import your exported workouts and routines. There needs to be easier support for users accounts. I understand you need to get paid but. . . Strongapps you have gotten a bit greedy. Open it up features a bit more to make it usable, put ads in it to get paid, add in app purchases to add certain desired features, letting us skip other and sell ad free version and then you will make money without alienating your users. Just saying it's a decent app but others do the same for less. Now I am going back to an older app.

Not enough excercises to choose from. You can create exercises that aren't on the list, but there aren't enough categories to choose from to separate body parts or types of equipment when creating your exercise. Once you create an exercise, you can't edit it. Annoying timer if creating a superset in workout. Difficult to cancel a workout that is started, and to figure out how to keep previous templates saved (if there's a way) after editing it. I should have stuck with fitnotes or another app.

I had recently posted that I loved the app. So much so that after a couple of days with it I purchased a monthly subscription. BUT NOW it's not letting me use the pro version to have more than 3 workout routines. Need help because I want to test it and if I like it will purchase the lifetime subscription. If I can't have this issue fixed I will cancel the subscription and find another app.

Before the most recent update this would have been 5 stars. The update now forces me to remove timers from every exercise every time I do them. It also now malfunctions if I try to do things too quickly, which makes it impossible to add weights or reps to the targeted exercise (instead it fills numbers in previous sets where they shouldn't be). This causes me to have to close and re open the app multiple times during a session.

I have to agree with a lot of other reviews, the price just isn't worth it. The app description promises an "ever expanding" list of exercises and routines, but when you buy Pro there are still only a few routines? Where's the value? Zero to Hero, Progression, and Jefit all give you the same for free.

Top notch app - or at least it was. The ability to customize workouts, change the number of sets, view the equipment and the movements if you were uncertain about whether the move was right for the muscle group you were working - top notch. Good library too, even allowing you to add exercises. A lot of this has been locked down in the paid version now. Can't really customize your workouts, won't recall if you add a set (but you can still do it manually) - really too expensive to bother paying. $7? Nope. For $3 I might pay.

The app is very basic which has both pros and cons obviously. Basic enough that paying for the premium was a stretch but I did it anyways. Now after not using it for a couple month it switched to SUBSCRIPTION billing! Now my premium is worthless and they have the nerve to ask for 10 bucks per month when the app is still as basic as ever? Pass.

Mediocre but not terrible. Easy fixes would elevate my review. Big new annoyance: doesn't sync across devices. WHY IS A LOGIN REQUIRED THEN?? Lots of exercises MISSING, which is normal for these type of apps. (For example, shoulder rotations.) BIGGER ISSUE is when you try to add exercises that involve Cables, you can't select Cable.

Everything about the app is great, unfortunately the price for the pro version which is required to plan more than 3 workout routines is prohibitively expensive. At the time I wrote this review the one time payment to unlock the app is about $99 USD, completely ridiculous. If the price were reasonable I would be more than happy to pay. The free version, although good, is too limiting for me to really use when there are alternatives available.

I have used this app in the past it was okay. But now when I add external workouts even when I haven't reached the limit of workouts, i cannot add them (through links) I am so frustrated and annoyed by this. I am constantly missing workouts too. I dont know about iOS but in Android it's just not working properly. I click on the link, it redirects me to the app but nothing happens. Please look into this and fix it ASAP.

I really love the app. It's clean, easy to use and the exercise illustrations are super helpful. The reason why i'm only giving it a 2/5 rating is because it's only possible to save three workouts if not paying for the pro version. Normally i have no problem paying anything for an app. But a monthly 5€ fee for this..? Cmon guys. Lower the monthly to 2€ or a one-time flat 7-12€ fee, and you'll end up seeing a lot more retention here.

Such a shame, I like the platform and the ability to search a workout however I would not pay for that or to save my workouts. If anything they should have made it so that you could save all your workouts but if you wanted the automatic PR goals to be saved then you upgrade to pro for better tracking of your best lifts. Too bad. It was a nice app while it lasted.

Is ok but missing one key element. You can create your own workout routines but cannot group them into a program. No good if you have a number of different programs that you use for weeks at a time before changing. Can only save workouts rather than whole programs like other apps. This means you can have many workouts saved for many different programs which all end up mixed together.

Was a pretty good app with restrictions in the free part. I loved the update until it signed me out, in the middle of a workout, and I couldn't get back in. I followed all the instructions, as apparently my password was no longer good, and awaited an email with a code, but it never came. Disappointed in the support for the app.

It's billed as a 'free' app with 'in-app' purchases. The free version is basically useless and, before you even start a workout, you'll be purchasing the 'subscription' version. The paid version is good for the most part. However it is absolutely not worth the offensive monthly subscription. If you're a mug, go ahead and buy this app. There are free (or onetime purchase) apps available that are just as good though.

Really good work out app and I love how intuitive everything is. However, the free version is too limited to be useful and the paid version is way too expensive.$7 per month or $40 per year(after 50% discount) is too much for a workout app. Slash the prices to half of that and I'd happily subscribe.
Have to login in for every thing this days. Can't even have a guest mode anymore.

Update: Removing 1 star, down to 2 stars now. Has a "Focus Metric" for total volume option but it is available only on the Apple platform as per their support docs. Don't understand why a simple total volume calculation for each exercise cannot be something I can track. This in addition to no RPE, RIR, and other features that are being added to competing apps it makes no sense for me to continue my subscription.

• You need to pay a ridiculous amount of money to get more than 3 workout templates. You can't buy them separately which is a big issue. I only want unlimited templates and I wouldn't consider paying 100€ just for them. • The app is great and I would gladly pay a reasonable price but the template limitation is ridiculous and ruins the whole app if you need more templates. I suggest either dropping this crippling limitation or adding the option to purchase the templates separately.
only 2 templates possible to save in free version. Very limited functionality. Lots of missing excercises

Can we get more features on this? Seems like it hasn't had improvements over the time it's been active and considering the sub price I'd like a few more features. 1) Can we get the ability to add a photo to custom exercises so we can have a better idea of what machines we've attached to the custom exercise? It would be nice to simply snap a shot of a machine and have it display on the custom exercise.

Like the app but the pricing is terrible. $100 for the full version is just not worth it. Unlees you buy the app you can only have 3 cutom workouts. If you have a 4 day routine like me this app is useless unless you pay the $100. Other apps can do the same thing without the orice gouging

Not great. Muscle groupings are mostly non-existent (the app knows nothing of things like biceps or triceps, quads and hamstrings. It knows only 'arms' and 'legs'). The library of exercises is pretty limited (no stairmaster). Creating custom exercises simply doesn't work. You also have to create an account to use the app, which is plainly unnecessary. Frankly, it lacks basic functionality. If you think you have only one muscle in your arm (your 'arm muscle') then this might just be the app for you. If you want a more detailed approach to your workout, keep looking.

Great app for free! the paid for app has very little perks! You developer could put a little more effort into the paid app. separate the the muscles to individual parts, update the various exercises as your selection is basic. based on other comments, looks like the developers don't have the courtesy to respond.

Great app but paywall is cruel. I'm not saying this app needs to be free, but nearly £100 for a lifetime access to an app... That's just a joke. I can pay £60 for a brand new video game on a console that's taken millions to pay make. I can use Jefit for free with nearly all the same features Strong locks behind a pay wall. I'd happily pay a reasonable sum, but nearly £100 is absolutely not reasonable.

Nicely designed, free version has almost but not quite everything I need, but subscription pricing is a hard pass. I have no problem paying for apps, and will buy one of the others available out there, but the pricing on this one is insane by comparison

This is a general workout tracking app. Doesn't offer truly offer anything unless you pay for it. Also stop asking me to save my work out if only to force me to buy the app. If you want to make money off this app don't offer a free version. If it weren't so annoying I might have purchased the upgrade by now.

Need better integration between other apps, notably Googled Fit. This could also give me ideas on how to switch things up, which would add tremendous value to the product.

No web GUI to design workouts on your PC. Or import template files from someone else like a trainer. Iimited workouts to choose from in the app. No option to select a custom exercise. It doesn't track and notify you of when to increase weight or push yourself. Can't believe this app is so popular for what limited features it offers.

I've been using it to track my workout for a while. The last update removed the automatic countdown between every rep. I just can't understand the reasoning behind that. I was contemon getting the perpetual subscription. But if the developers keep removing features for no reason, then I'll look elsewhere.

This app runs well enough, as long as you pay for it. The free version is basically useless as you can only track 3 custom workouts (even a push/pull routine is going to require you track 4). The "pro" version is over priced and lacking any unique features you can't find for free elsewhere.

I've used this app for a while and like it, but after the new update, I was logged out, and when I logged back in, all of my data was gone. It now shows I'm not subscribed. There is literally no way in the app to reinstate my subscription, and support is yet to respond.

It's a great fitness and workout tracking app ruined by very expensive pricing. It's also lacking a web interface to let you set up workouts on your computer. Given that it's competing with JeFit it's going to have to tune the pricing a bit.

I have used this app for a long time on Iphone and it was great, but switching to android I find that there isn't an option to keep workouts in folders, I have no idea why this was a choice, but it makes the app much worse. Surely the android version could implement folders the same as the iphone.

been using this for a while good for keeping track of exercises. but the historical charts are terrible. not as good as the other apps where they show a graphical chart display. no way to edit time if you forgot to finish the workout and accidentally leave the app running.

Love the app so far. Unfortunately the pro version price model is subscription based so I'll be stuck with the free mode until I find something similar with better pricing. I can't really justify spending monthly for something like this.

Decent app, but could use some improvements and a few bug fixes. Been subscribed for well over 2 years and now moved to an app which doesn't feel like abandonware. Used to love it until I discovered JEFIT... They don't limit the free version as much as Strong App does. I've tried 6 different apps for tracking and this came in second (will still uninstall cause 2nd ain't good enough). Wish their free version was better...

Was great before the recent update. Now there are plenty of bugs, the timer is much more annoying to use. Most importantly, the feature when adding exercises, to easily filter and only show the ones that you have used before, is gone. Please please bring it back. This app was perfect before

Lets be honest most people tracking a workout will likely track calories. Syncing with Google Fit is great but that doesn't send strength workouts to MFP, why do you no longer link directly to MFP so calories are tracked. Would be the perfect app if you did.

Good app, but it should tell you ahead of time that you're only allowed to have 3 templates on a free account. Instant uninstall for me when I saw that when trying to create a 4th. It's a well known marketing tactic... can ya'll stop doing it???

Should have spent more time fixing defects for the Android version. It's pretty frustrating to use right now. I'd be willing to rate it higher after they are fixed. Not worth the monthly fee as it stands now.

This app seemed really great and intuitive, but it should absolutely say very clearly that you only get 3 workout templates for the free version. Spent forever building the first 3 of my 5 day routines and then was a total waste of time

Seems like it takes a little longer to enter info in l into this app during workouts then others I've used. You can only create 3 workout templates before you have to pay for PR0 so if you deviate at all from the generalized templates, your screwed.

App was working fine...at some point started crashing when trying to enter weight, add a set, add exercise. Uninstalled and reinstalled and it worked, now one day later back to crashing. Please fix or I am done.
If you have a split that's more than 3 workouts then you need to spend money. Starts corrupting part of my workouts after a while and just deleted stuff, very useful for tracking progress.

On the workout menu on the exercise The new update doesn't let you change from kilograms to pounds and vice versa so now I have multiple exercises with kilograms in pounds combined and there's no way to change it.

Good app for tracking workout progress, but today I opened the app and found myself logged out, and when I logged back in, my workout history was empty. Pretty annoying that I lost all my workout data.

It is a good app for beginners, but the options of exercises is very bad. For example we got just 1 "normal" push up + band, knee and handstand push up variant l. Thats it, no diamond, close, wider or incline, decline, 1 arm versions &&&. Pls fix this thx
The update deleted my pre-existing workouts, and I can no longer see the images representing each exercise which makes finding them much more complicated. I liked it before :(

Fairly good fitness tracker but it logged me out and wouldn't let me back in even though I had paid my subscription. I was supposed to get a reset password sent to my email but it never got sent. Absolutely no way to contact customer support
App is quite basic as far as included workouts. Any of the advanced features need to be paid for (expensive). There's plenty of better free apps out there.

Downgrade since update :( Seems like a bunch of features no one asked for was added at the cost of everything that was great. The UI is now super clunky with timers taking up so much real estate, last rpe settings are no longer carried over when copying new sets, deleting a set annoyingly asks you if you're sure every single dang set... Awfully made for android I just switched from iphone to android the app worked perfectly on my 6 years old iphone but not in android version now whenever i try to see any video for any exercise to check the form the video doesn't show up This was one of my goto app for workout but now can't really use it
Been using for years but suddenly been asked to login and it doesn't work, even trying to login with Google etc. doesn't work. Really hope I haven't lost all my progress logs.
Just really weird the way the sets are setup. You can't edit the weight so you have to add the exercise each time unless you're using the same weight for every set.

Stopped integrating with Google Fit. Used to be perfect, although the ability to add workouts was pretty limited. Suggestions: - Add calisthenic timer (can use rest timer but why not) - Add AI workout creator
Simple app for monitoring progress perfect for my needs but only allows 3 custom routines before you have to pay and it's too expensive, if it was much much cheaper I'd give it 5 star.

Forget this app. You can only create 3 custom workout routines which is completely worthless. I was going to say "F" it and buy it until I saw at best you have to subscribe for a hundred dollars a year. No use keeping this app around anymore. It is a shame looked like a good app
Its about as close as you get to free without a free trial. Still can't save your own workouts without paying for it. Was convenient enough.
The UI is simple and easy to use, but the workout screen has no images, and some very basic functions (such as being able to track body measurements) are locked behind paywalls

Irritating how you can only make 3 personalized workouts. I liked the service, but i'd rather not get paywalled immediately into setting workouts. I'm not looking for this app to do anything for me, just keep track of my stats. Really? A paywall? Gotta love greedy companies.

Loved this app. Long time user. Of late I've been using split screen a lot on my phone especially while in the gym. It's a deal breaker that this app doesn't support split screen. I'll return if they implement this

Issues: (1) Sync issues prevent changes being saved, including (a) workout template/exercise renames, (b) workout/exercise note deletion, (c) changes to Fail/etc statuses. (2) In workout view, there's no indicator if a set was marked Failed/etc in a prior workout and (3) no ghost text, impeding workout planning. (4) Said statuses are also not in exports. (5) No add rep button. Issues here also affect the iOS app. Would be 1 star if I didn't have Pro 1-payment lifetime access via early adoption.
They have changed it so you have to pay to have more than 3 custom routines, so it's no longer suitable for my needs. So disappointing. Will now seek out a new work out app.

Actually I love this app. But I hate that you removed the opportunity to categorize and make a routine. It cost some money and even lesser apps do this😅 Now everything is so messy. Im actually looking for another app because of this.
Good app but the free version is pretty worthless because you only get three free templates. Can't really create a workout plan with three templates.
I have 96 workouts tracked with this app. Was in love with it. Last 2 updates have been horrible. Today everytime I added a set it crashed.
Makes finding different workouts easy but the app could be soo much more by adding a calculator for figuring ressistance for each incline setting and a notepad style file for recording progress
This is ok if you are focused on gym equipment. Bar bells etc. Not really useful if you want to use body weight exercises. E.g. no 'press up' option.
It's frustrating that advertised functionality like basic charts and having more than 3 saved workouts is behind a subscription plan.

I had the app decided id go and look else where because of the pricing structure. Cannot justify monthly premium. Rather make this a paid app.   Another thing is, I see you do not comment on negative reviews only positive reviews. Please can i ask to have a reply.

glad I used the free trial in its entirety before making my decision. the first workout was amazing. the second and third workouts were terrible. the second one was way too easy and the third had things I simply can't do. for example (though not the only issue) I am not capable of doing push ups. my wrists cannot support that movement. I tried to replace the exercise and the only options were harder versions of push ups. I stopped the workout part way through because of that. disappointing.

2nd Review: It seems they listen to their customers. They fixed/changed things from my last review and I appreciate it; however, I do want to give more feedback. If we could set the max weight we own, that would be awesome. Example: I only own up to 20 lbs in dumbells, but I cannot set that anywhere I find. It always adds extra weight and I have to set it back. Not a big deal, but it can get tedious after a bit. Last feedback: the "log all sets" button should have a warning after press.

Seems pretty good but unfortunate you have to pay for it to unlock all the benefits/full range use (which I know is common these days) but for a workout app, being 8$/ month or almost $100 annually seems too high. It might show it but I haven't seen yet where it shows your reps and sets when you log your workout which is too bad since that's a decent part about tracking workouts.

a few simple improvements to reduce the number of clicks needed would be good. When you finish a workout, go to the next one in your session. Don't make me go back to the name menu and scroll down and find the next one that doesn't have a checkbox on it and click it every step. When you have a cool down session that says to do something for EG 30 seconds, not having a timer available is ridiculous. It would be so easy to implement a timer or on those exercises.

this site is great! or it would be if I could get past the SUBSCRIPTION SOFTLOCK. I made an account not knowing about the subscription; my fault probably, but if I knew this beforehand this I'd just never had made my account. Btw you can only delete your account INSIDE Of the app. I had closed the tab and you can figure out what happened from here. I'd love to at least see if it was worth buying but not with the software override of doom.

I don't mind paying for subscriptions, let's start with that. BUT making the user set up the app + personalize the available equipment + create and account and AFTER THAT you realize that you can't even start the trial without adding payment info? You guys are crazy. Made me lose 20 minutes. Uninstall and never again... Oh, and apparently you can't even delete the account because you need to enter to the settings inside the app, which YOU CAN'T without adding the payment info. Bravo.

I want to give a good rating but the app still puts the wrong exercise in the middle of a workout. One day I told support that it was now creating half of my workouts in Spanish and they sent a "We hope your issue is resolved l" email the next day. None have been resolved and I'm still paying full price while doing half of the work by editing my own workouts.

I've been using Fitbod for years to stay consistent in my routine. Lately there has been a bug when trying to see saved workouts — the screen just stays blank. Closing the app and clearing storage doesn't fix it. The saved workouts do appear after a few minutes or sometimes not at all, it can be random. I'm hoping an update fixes the issue.

app has a lot of potential. the notifications aren't great at showing up on my phone, as in they only show up when I open the app. I wish they sent reminders to do my workout and to continue my streak. also the app should adjust the weights according to the last workout even though I am a beginner I've been trying to lift medium to heavy so it should try and push me to lift heavier than the last. I also think that the workout selection should be updated with more workouts.

2 years and running. never disappoints. One of the best if not the best. Pick your goals and stay consistent and you will see changes. I have no diet and have seen increased muscle, stamina, and body changes. Just waiting on updates of videos of workouts possibly better angles or just having alternate for when injuries have occured. Still will get the job done.

The action of logging past or recent exercises is a little cumbersome (entering workout sets as you do them doesn't save automatically, so hitting the back button will close that menu and lose your progress for that group). Other than that, a very solid way to track workouts, as well as progress or fatigue on different muscle groups.

paid for subscription amd app won't let me login totally wasted money have not even been able to see first workout. Was hesitant to buy hoping to get to see a sample workout finally decided to just pay for a month and now have no faith in any of the workout apps. after customer service helped was finally able to login. will update rating after being able to use the app after getting the login issues resolved it has been a serviceable app so far.

For the most part a decent App. However, in recent weeks leg information is not being updated. It tracks recovery and let's you input and save. However, set progress is not receiving any information. I personally submitted a trouble ticket but have yet to receive any response. Frustrating when you have a paid subscription.

I am currently using the free trial. Not sure if I will continue on with a paid membership. I recently purchased a 150 lb stack weight home gym. I find it very limiting that you do not offer this as an equipment option on your app. I have to choose some from your list that have similar functions that my home gym offers, but I sometimes get in my routine options that do not correspond to what I have on hand. Thankfully, I can sometimes use the swap out options, but it frustrating and limiting.

Great AI workout service, a bit expensive considering what you're getting, but it's the only app out there that offers this kind of workout aid. Although, I wish we could disable some features, such as strength tracking. Anytime a set drops in quantity it subtracts from the strength score, which can be discouraging. Also, not everyone can track their muscle measurements and body fat percentage, so the entire tab is kind of a waste of space that should have a toggle off button.

so far it's been great with creating workouts that push me just enough, tracks all of my reps, times me when it's appropriate, etc. However: the calorie count doesn't transfer to my Samsung Health App; the workout + time spent working out does, but it always reads as 0 cals burned. Fitbod app is able to accurately communicate calories burned with My Fitness Pal app, so I'm not sure where the disconnect is. I'm not a fan of paying for a "full" app, but I think like $3-5/mo would be fair for this

Still needs work. Marking an exercise as complete closes the menu, including the rating section that asks how much effort it was/How many more reps you could do, so it's easily skipped and forgotten which leads to the app recommending exercises and weights that you're not ready for. The app does not teach you how to recognize when you're pushing too hard or how to identify strains, injuries, or tears, and coupled with the above, its a disaster waiting to happen for anyone without experience.

This app is great in theory. It works really well with coming up with initial ideas. But as of this most recent release my app no longer allows you to automatically regenerate workouts or adjust the muscle group. Ever workout is locked in no matter how I try to automatically adjust it. I have to manually adjust it.

Absolutely love this app, been using around a year or so. Like the feature where it asks at the end of each set how many reps you would have been able to do more of outside of the suggested reps/set so it can adapt for the next time. One thing I so wish they added is the notes option where it is added to the bottom of the exercise and it keeps track of whatever you add from previous times for that particular exercise. Right now it just has a notes feature and it's a pain to get to.

I've paid for this app for a couple years now. I absolutely love it. It's probably closer to 4.5 stars but you can't give half stars. Two features I'd love but they don't have (yet). - show secondary muscles groups that are worked out for each exercise. - They should go into each major gym chain: Planet fitness, Gold's, Crunch, etc and make sure all of the equipment in these gyms are on the equipment list. I use PF and there's a bunch missing, especially ab related.

This app makes no indication that it is paid only. the app allows you to build your initial workout plan, then requests you log in to save your plan. once you log in, the app immediately opens an uncloseable splash page requiring free subscription our outright payment. you cannot pass this screen without subscribing or paying. this means that if you want to delete your account, you have to provide some form of payment information. This is deceptive.

Downloaded it, filled out the biometrics, etc only to be slapped with an enormous pay wall before I could even see my workout. They don't at all advertise that you have to pay for it before you start filling everything out. It's like one of those pop up scams you see on websites. Free trial doesn't count. you still missed advertised your product.

I've been using this for about a year. Its equivalent to Fitbod, but cheaper. I like that android is a priority; Fitbod neglected android often. I also used Fitbod for at least a year. The AI algorithms are as opaque and frustrating in this app as in Fitbod. If you're looking for smarts, it ain't in either app. I'll continue to use this until it's cost prohibitive.

Great app. It generates good workouts. I especially like the ability to swap out types of workouts (push,pull, leg) and to swap out individual exercises. 4 stars instead of 5 because sometimes when inputting exercises from a HIIT class, there are exercises that I can't find, then I have to take the time to create the exercise.

Concept is great, but execution is half-baked. Been using this for a year or so(?) They keep saying they're excited to launch the new Android version while we're still without a lot of the (apparent) iOS features and integrations... still paying for a full app though. I do like how the workouts adjust based on the calculated fatigue of each muscle group and the easy-to-use rest periods during exercise. Maybe other apps do this, but I've been too lazy until now to look into it; this does good enough, but it's not as cool as you see it advertised.

Great application, with some caveats. The Good I like the way you can select a workout by intensity, muscle group, available equipment, etc. I like the instructional videos and historical tracking The Bad You are limited to the exercises included in the database. There's a spot where you can request an exercise be added, I have done this a couple of times, but none of them have been added. No combo moves, limited variations, etc. customer service not responsive. Bummer for $80/year.

It is easy to swap out exercises, and I like the rest timer. However, it takes a very long time to show up in my Fit app after syncing. It also installs an app on my watch, but doesn't stay open or give me relevant information (like heart rate and calories burned) during the workout. That did end up showing up in the workout log, but would be good to see during the workout.

I've been using the app for years and love it. The quick ability to replace a routine is key when I'm looking to tweak the recommended workout. And I love that it pushes me over time with more weights, sets, or exercises. My biggest complaint (several years running now) is that Strava Sync still doesn't exist on Android. Given all my other apps have this, this may eventually become a deal breaker for me.

The ability to switch workouts you don't want with ones you do is a plus and I like the variety, but I've been having Health Connect issues. I can't seem to get the calories burned to show up correctly on Samsung Health with all permissions granted. I also like the Android Wear app, but a suggestion I have is when you finish a rep and click it on the watch, it should automatically go to the next one on your phone (unless I don't have it set up correctly). Overall, it is a great app

I loved the app. Paid the premium. Arm and chest workouts are awesome but the legs and hamstrings are killing me. It's all Romanian, they make no sense, not simple for beginners, and if someone has back problems you automatically work the wrong muscles because you can't push the way they want you to or you can't feel the burn at all. Put in actual squats as I can't find them. And please add real variety for legs. Well rounded workout is the expectation of the app. Not just upper body.

Great app, been using it for a few months now. I have an android and it sounds like IOS gets better features, but if I didn't check the reviews I wouldn't know any better. Great at tracking, demonstrating, and auto calculating. Only 4 stars because it glitches out rarely. (Got logged out, erased my days data) only recommendation is to add a meal and diet side to the app. Separate, but the same. I will note that the replacement option is limiting but can be manually entered in otherwise.

Excellent!! Definitely worth the price! Easy to personalize your workouts and make adjustments as needed. Bad knees? Switch out your exercise. Great for different fitness levels. Being currently out of shape, it's easy to step up the intensity as I get stronger. I also enjoy being able to see the total pounds that I lifted during any given workout.

Haven't used it for long yet, but my impressions so far, as someone who is just starting to take workouts seriously, are pretty good. They ask the important questions, but don't expect you to shell out a ton of money for extra features or gym equipment you don't own right from day one, even though I was expecting and willing to pay at least some small amount. Excited to see how it works once I can get started.

I like the app. Particularly the home gym where you can list your equipment. It doesn't seem to track my progress when I manually update my weights, as the same exercise the next week will go back down in weight even though I upped it in the previous workout. It froze up today and wouldn't let me start my workout, wouldn't let me log in to my account, etc... I had to uninstall and then reinstall to get it to work. For the cost this thing should run flawlessly. There are other apps cheaper...

I am new to lifting and this app has given me the confidence at the gym and the insight to know how much I should be lifting and the proper form at my fingertips. I really like it. It could use an option to switch between generated routines that are home based (or little/no equipment) to gym based. I wish there was an easier way to customize what you have access to. You can build your own routines so there is a work around. Overall a great app.

So far, I'm giving this 3 stars. If it works well, I may increase the rating. But, so far, it seems very finicky. After putting in all of my information initially, it gave me a workout requiring equipment I said I don't have, for muscle groups I did not pick to work on. I had to go back through, and change it all again. Not a great first impression of your app.

This app has truly been life changing for me. I was never able to stick to a workout routine. This was mostly because I really didn't know what routine to do. When I found a routine I liked, I would repeat it until I got bored with it...and then quit altogether. I have been using fitbod consistently for almost 2 years now! I feel better and look the best I have in my life! Fitbod switches up your workout every day based on the criteria you input. It is completely customizable. Best app!

I've tried a lot of workout apps, and so far this one is the best. I honestly really like this one. I've been working out for years. I have ADD and it has always been frustrating and overwhelming trying to put workouts together and ones that im happy with. This app, don't just give you one set way to work out and be like, "ok, here you go". If gives you many options to pick and choose from to taylor around what you are looking for, and also equipment that is available to you. GREAT app!

Fitbod has organised my workouts in a way I don't have to think about it too much. Once the goals are set, it is almost autopilot from that point on. Only minor changes regarding time, equipment, and workout options are needed as per user needs. As of today, I am going through my 3rd subscription, and I don't think I'll change it. Thanks for the efforts 👌🏼

Pretty good workout app. Few things I think could use work is the muscle group variations. Really like doing a PushPullLeg split but can't do that effectively through here when it recommends an unequal balance of muscle groups, ie legs only once a week. Also, some muscle groups never get recommended unless you go in and change it, ie traps, calves, forearms. Another thing that would be nice is more equipment for home gyms, like sandbags. Overall a good app.

I really like the app and saw some gains using the app. I wont give it less stars because the issues can be fixed or one can adjust on the app but that takes away from the time i already had to carve out of my day. I would like to see some of these things adjusted. I work out alone and some of the workouts require I have a workout partner. Also, some beginners workouts start at 30 lbs. No way i can start with that. Some of the weights suggestions are weird. i don't have a 7.5 lb. weight.

I've been using this app for 3 years now and love it. It's very user friendly and functional. I love that I can select specific equipment I have access to so no longer the days of a workout where I don't have half the equipment. I also really like the ability to select a step and select to do it more often, less often or never again. Update: I mentioned I'd like to be able to set gym profiles/locations and they let me know you can!!! I love this even more now. Thanks for the instructions!

I'm enjoying the app and the variations on workouts. I wish it allowed for 2 things though. #1 - it would be awesome if I could save and "merge" workouts. Specifically when I have a shorter workout related to rehabbing an injury. Being able to track and add those in would be nice. #2 - some days I want a variation of workouts before picking one. Keeping a history of generated workouts would be an awesome value add.

I've only used this app for two workouts so far and it's already better than any I've tried. Straight to the point. Simple interface. Good variety of exercises based on the equipment I own, and it seems to smartly choose exercises based on what I've recently worked out. Takes all the guesswork out of my home workout. However, there are no integrations on the android app like there are on iOS. I'm really missing Google Fit or Health Connect integration. 5 stars if Health Connect is added!

Really a fantastic app! I used to carry around a paper and pen and write my workouts in, but planning workouts was not my strong suit. This app takes out the guess work and gives you exercises with the equipment you currently have at home or have available at the gym. I've been using it for two weeks now and going with the monthly option for now. If I can stick to the schedule, I'll do a full year membership. Can't wait to see my results this time next year!

it was better before the recent update. i liked being able to add exercises NEXT, instead of LAST, in the list. at least let us choose our preference. now i have to drag each thing up into the correct order after adding. it's cumbersome. also not a fan of it asking additional questions every time i remove/change something. i understand this is to help your algo; but it's too intrusive, and it frequently doesn't even work. if i select "delete and don't recommend," it doesn't delete.

I love this app! It is exactly what I was looking for as a beginner starting to weight lift after loosing 195lbs. I was able to cancel my gym membership and buy two adjustable dumbbells, resistance bands, a medicine ball and other various equipment to fully do my workouts from home. I would give the app 5 stars if your workout information (reps and sets) transferred from one device to the other via cloud servers. Other than that it's pretty awesome 👐🏾

Very good app, but there's some kind of glitch where once a week or so it will give me a workout with insanely high reps with almost no weight (4 sets of 20 bench press at 45 pounds, 4 sets of 20 lat pull downs at 30 pounds, that kind of thing). Recalculating weight and reps doesn't work. The only way to fix it is by manually entering the amount sets, reps, and weights I want to do. The whole point of the app is so that I shouldn't have to keep track of those things. Please fix.

I've been out of shape for a very long time and never really worked out in general, even when I was young. I've been wanting to get more active for a while but gym memberships are expensive and most workout guides for at home routines are intimidating. This app is great, it asks several questions to get to know you, then builds a daily routine for you. Even as a beginner I can complete most the workouts it gives. Highly recommend for those in similar situations!

This would be a five star app, if there were parity with Apple. But there isn't, and frankly that's a little bit messed up. We're paying the same amount, but getting far less usage out of this otherwise excellent app because we're part of a different ecosystem. I know it's hard as developers to export to two different platforms, but parity has to become a major priority. Otherwise, charge us less.

Three days free, have to pay after that. Well worth it. l love the ease of use for this app. If I'm working out from home, rather than gym, I can change the settings to only incorporate body weight movements. If a machine is being used in the gym, I have options to find a comparable move that uses different equipment. This app gives me the direction I need, along with the flexibility of every day life.

I was intrigued with the machine learning aspect of this app. While I like the idea of some of the concepts, I feel that this app just fails to deliver...especially since you have to pay for it. You'll see the reviews mention the same "cons" that I noticed. There seems to be a better ios version that android version. For example, if you want to copy a workout that you did previously...apparently you can do that in ios but not android. Why would you need to do that if the app intelligently creates your workout? Well, there are a few reasons, but sometimes it creates odd superset combinations--pairing 2 completely different exercises that wouldn't fly in a normal gym like bouncing between the barbell bench and dumbbells or pairing 2 similar exercises that work the same muscle and defeat the purpose of supersetting 2 exercises that work different muscles to cut down overall rest time. Another reason to create your own workout would be if you had a specific set of exercises given by your doctor or physical therapist, but that's a challenge if not all of those exercises are available in the app because you can't add your own and you can request an exercise to be added, but I haven't seen even one of my requests added over the few months that I've been using the app. Also, one of the features that I looked forward to was calculating an increase in reps/weights, but it does some weird stuff sometimes...like increasing from 20 pound dumbbells to 22.5 pound dumbbells...I'm not sure I've seen 22.5 pound dumbbells. A nice feature is that you can edit the equipment in your gym, but it doesn't help when you can specify if your gym has "dumbbells" but not indicate that your gym doesn't carry the standard 22.5 pound dumbbell. One last issue that I would mention is that while the concept of selecting exercises for a specific muscle is nice and the idea of managing muscle fatigue by generating a workout for muscles with the most rest is nice, this app just fails at it. While it may be doing possibly more work in the background, I can give an example of what I'm talking about. Let's take the deadlift as an example since it works multiple muscles--hamstrings, glutes, traps, and abs just to name a few. The only muscle group that the deadlift is available on the app is for the hamstrings. So if you want a glute workout, it's not an option. I can only imagine that this logic is carried through by not calculating any fatigue on the glute and only for the hamstring when the deadlift is recorded. That's just one example, but there are tons of exercises that work multiple muscle groups...I just don't see this app being that intelligent. Bottom line, this app would be good if some of the logic was cleaned up, but it's not free so it's definitely not worth it. I feel compelled to use it since I paid for it, but it's really not the best app that I have for working out. A note for the App author, it would probably be better if it were free and maybe had a little advertising in it to provide the revenues, or at least some free version to help get more subscribers with more workable data for you to use.

Love the app. Very easy to use, helps provide structure to the wayward. I do not like how there isn't a free plan, or that that fact is not disclosed in the description. It does say there are in-app purchases, but it does not tell you you only get 3 workouts before being prompted to pay. I understand and respect a company needs to meet their bottom line, I just want to know how before I have given them my email address, rather than after.

First thing to say is that I really enjoy the app. It has made working out so much less boring and repetitive, for me. I really wish that it would give me the chance to give more details about my home gym though - i can't do 30lb bar work, I only have a 45lb bar. Similarly, my dumb bells go up in 5lb increments from 5-30lb. Would be great if it would let me input things like that and not recommend sets I can't do.

So far, I really like it! Since it creates workouts for me, allows me to change the weights/reps/actual workout, and it allows me to preview the workouts - I am definitely a lot more consistent and comfortable going to the gym. I wish there was a constant free version where a person could get half of the app like gets 2 free days a week and limited targeted muscle groups instead of having to purchase a membership because I only work out 1-2 days a week and only use targeted muscle groups.

This has made weightlifting so much easier and fun. It has taken all the guesswork out of workout routines. Love it! Two improvements: (1) perhaps move the timer "Done" button over to the middle and the pause symbol to the left. I've accidentally double clicked the Log Set button and the hit Done on the timer which popped up right underneath it. A little shift would solve it. (2) Is there any way to select a subset of machines to make a workout? For example, just hi lo cable workout?

* Updated because it's been 7 months and this feature has yet to be added despite them saying they were working on it. * IOS version allows you to go into more detail with weights. I'm tired of having to edit my workouts everything to fit the equipment I have in my home gym. It's constantly giving me weights that I either can't produce with my weight plates or is higher than possible with my plates. When this is finally added to android I will give 5 stars.

Paid version is great and way cheaper than a PT. Didn't know how to start lifting or what exercises to do. This app eliminates the guess work. It keeps track of how much weight I'm using per exercise and auto increases next time. It lets me set up different gym profiles based on what equipment I have available. I decided to leave a review when it became apparent that the dev team is really working hard, always improving and keeping the user up to date with the changes that are coming. Great job.

I love that it suggest new exercises and routines and tracks strength progress. It has an easy to follow routine. There are a few things gs that could improve it. 1, let me see how I have improved on individual exercises. I'd love to know what kind of gains I've made with some charts over time for individual exercises. 2, integrate with fitbit and MyFitnessPal. I use those to track calories and routines. 3, let me choose some HIIT workouts. 4, Voice search exercises. 5 stars if these are added.

Been using for a couple of weeks and like the app. One request, can you add a sled exercise to the list. I've always enjoyed pushing a weighted sled. My gym has one, but Fitbod doesn't have the exercise as an option. Thanks Also, I'm an Android user and would like the ability to plan out my weekly routine in advance. Or just get the app for android to be the same as iPhone.

Good app, but there are a few little things that annoy me almost every time I workout. 1. There is no way to identify specifically what equipment you have The issue is it would be nice to identify what weights you have in your inventory. I constantly get exercises looking for a 17.5lb dumbbell, mine are increments of 5. 2. There are a lot of exercises asking to additional weight like jack knife sit-up and pushup, it would be nice if I had an explanation of how to do this.

Updated: The app is missing very basic/ common equipment like chest fly machines. Is there a way to add equipment?still, there is no way to rate the difficulty of exercises for it to adjust future weights and reps. I thought this was supposed to learn and adjust your future workouts? I'm disappointed that the most recent updates to the app did nothing to bring the android app on par with the iPhone app.

This was a sponsored app and I'm glad it was. After trying and rejecting several weight lifting apps, it only took one free workout to sell me. I love the ability to tailor my workout while still getting suggestions from the app. The how to videos are clear. There are many exercises to choose from for each muscle group. You can choose and eliminate exercises and equipment. All for less than the price of a fancy coffee or pint a beer a month.

great overall, but recently moved to Android and I find it quite annoying that every time I edit an exercise I need to get feed back, delete, recommend this etc... sometimes I have to change the exercise because of the gym I'm at, and if you're in the middle of a workout and in a hurry, it's annoying, I get why it's probably helpful but maybe there's another way

As a personal trainer, I really don't like having to program for myself. I do a lot of programming and just don't want to do more. so I love having an app that does it all for me. I also love how it has multiple replacements for exercises, built in timer, built in cardio, and more. I also like where I can tell it what equipment I have available and set for different gyms. so I can go back and forth between my work gym and my home gym. absolute beastly app! 6 out of 5 stars!!!

Variety of workouts is good. Helpful for getting started if you don't know how to plan a workout or cycle through muscle groups. Unfortunately, difficulty doesn't feel right often. It gives workouts that are very easy even after multiple days of rest. There has also been a problem in the past few months with it including workouts with equipment that is turned off in settings. Quality has gone down since I started using this about 1.5 years ago.

The bad: I find it suspicious that I stopped getting workout notifications / overviews after paying for a yearly subscription. It worked fine during the free trial. This was incredibly useful as it helped motivate me for the workout as well as plan accordingly. I signed up for a 2 week free trial through a friend referral but it only gave me 5 days. The rest of the app is pretty great I have to admit.

I've already paid for my subscription and you want me to pay more?! I had a subscription for $60 that goes until July that I've already paid for and now the app is asking for $80 to even use it. This is garbage. Greedy fitness apps are going to kill themselves. I really did like this app and didn't mind the original subscription but the upcharge for "eLiTe" is outrageous and I can't even use the app anymore unless I pay more. So, I basically got ripped off.

Love the app. For a while I used it to suggest me workouts, which helped a newbie get acclimated to the gym. It also kept me motivated with "streaks" and rewards. Now I'm on a program and I use it mostly for tracking my progress. If the devs see this, please add Bayesian cable curls, and also improve the tracking and records for the "assisted" exercises. The weight is a negative number so the app doesn't show your records and stuff like that. Other than that though, great stuff!

Love the app. However when my account re-subscribed after 1 year the price had gone up and I have effectively been locked out from logging anything. My account auto-paid $64.99, but it is now $79.99 and it's saying I can't do anything except pay $79.99 again. I'm not paying an additional $79.99. Will gladly pay additional $15 to get to $79.99 though.

As close to having a personal trainer as you can get, without someone over your shoulder. I REALLY LIKE that I made a suggestion to the help desk and within a month, Fitbod sent me an email letting me know that my suggestion was incorporated into the app and released. I like that they ask what equipment you have, and then tailor to that. I like that you can substitute exercises if needed, or add, change weights, whatever. I like the timing function to keep your rest periods. FRIGGIN WOW!

I would give this app a 5 star review but so far for me it is telling me to do the same muscle groups that were worked again the next day. Even thought it knows I did a full push or pull day before. I really just wanted a progress tracker and the log could be a little more user friendly. You have to get used to it or you will add 10 of the same workouts or almost complete your entire workout and hit add exercise and it will crash and not save. This app has amazing potential for future updates.

Take 90 of decision making out so I can think about exercise. I wished I could see next days workout. I have a pretty nice home gym. But the app hasn't taken advantage of any machines or benches, other than bench press. It would be nice to know if it will introduce those later, or if I will have to do that one own. Which takes me back to decision making. Overall it's a nice app especially for beginners.

Great app! Paying for the subscription imo is way worth it. The ease of tracking and planning is amazing. An option to recommend new or different exercises would be great (Updated) They fixed a lot of small issues that made the app even better. Glad to see they update and work on the app. Still waiting for a simple fix for weighted walks. No way to walk with weight and log it. Nah, I'll just keep telling the app I do 4 mile farmers walks untill you fix the damn thing.

Pretty solid app. Navigating through the app is relatively easy. It's well-organized, and the workout library is simple to access. While the app is free to download, many of the more advanced workouts and features are locked behind a paywall. It would be nice to have access to more content without a subscription. The app's user interface is generally intuitive, making it easy to select and start workouts. Expanding the free content available, even if in a limited capacity, would attract and reta

Overall a pretty great app. I've started using it regularly and like that I don't really need to think much about which exercises to do for the day. I'd like to see more automation though. Once I press the timer button, keep going through rest and exercises. It stops after my rest and waits for me to hit the timer again, which I didn't, and spent more time than I should for a set. Also, I'd like to more easily see which muscle groups and exercise targets.

Such a disappointing app. I purchased a year membership after initially enjoying the free trial. I've been using the app for several months at this point. It has good intentions, but is so glitchy and isn't very user friendly. When you curate an exercise from scratch, it will change/delete the exercises chosen half way through the workout. When you try and add the exercises back, it's not just a simple swap and is time consuming. It's so frustrating and is the main reason I hate this app.

Cool concept and fine when it works, but a very buggy app. The app has a tendency to change my workout in the middle of a set if I have "custom muscles" set. This requires me to go back and manually add the workout I want back in, and then it resets again. Eventually I got so frustrated I had to abandon the workout altogether. Should not have paid money for something so buggy and untested.

Lacking compared to the apple version, when will we get the rest of the features on the Apple version? Like swapping exercises for the full list instead of the short list that pops up? Or 1rm tracking? Or sharing my results with friends? Or the other stuff, the list goes on 😀. It's weird to be getting the version that doesn't feel complete, for the same price as my wife's account that does more 🤔.

The app is alright. I do use it a lot to track my gym workouts, but I'm personally not a big fan of the UX. They try to be smart and set weights automatically, but in most cases it's wrong and I end up with more taps/clicks. I also don't like that there are a lot of exercises that are very similar instead of better grouping them, it should learn from my related exercises and suggest reasonable weights, but no... I end up needing to look up my training history manually.

Seems great at face value, but once you get to the workout, it feels completely overwhelming. I'm a starter. I can't do 5 sets of pull-ups, and I can't alter the amount. This makes it very hard ti track my progress. Plus some of the workouts are just not adequate for a beginner level. A deadlift was shown and with too much weight, it threw out my back. Not to mention the UI during the workout is confusing to navigate. Definitely could take notes from Leap Fitness. I'll go there instead

Every day I have to tell it that I'm not going to do a "Turkish Get up" a "60 second handstand" because there's no way to remove those permanently. There are exercises I can't do because of an injury but I have to manually remove them every time. I asked support about it because I know the iOS version lets you remove an exercise permanently. They said they might add it, but I don't have much confidence. Otherwise, great app. 5 stars, if they would fix that.

The perfect fitness app! I love that you can select the equipment you have available (either at home or the gym) and it will select a workout plan each day based off of that. It also shows the muscle groups used/recovered. You can add cardio options as well. You can even make up your own workout plan. The options for types of workouts are virtually endless! Really great fitness app! Worth the money, plus they offer a free trial!

Good for where it is thus far. Needs same features as iOS. Like that it builds routines for you and that you can add what equip you already have. Needs the ability to say what weights you have, but how many. would also like the ability for the program to show.me what weights I need to add to my bar so I don't have to think. Lastly, need to be able save workouts you've completed for future workouts. Update: Nearly 9 months later and still can't save past workouts. Discontinuing my membership.

It's a pretty good app, but there are too many limitations for paying $60/year. There's no Google Fit integration. There's no way to dislike certain exercises. The exercises themselves are extremely limited. For example, I can't log heel touches--LOL. Updated to one stars: you can't copy and do a past workout, save a template, nor add a superset once the workout started. Really not user friendly. I recommend a strong pass on this app.

Great app! I just wish it offered a bit more considering you have to pay for it. Things I'd like to see in the app: the ability to add notes to workouts. More stats like progression, weight loss ect. A timer for exercises like '60 sec plank' I really enjoy the instructional videos and the way you can tailor the app to equipment you have available.
