This app has a lot of potential and is listed high on a lot of fitness app review sites. I'm still relatively new to the app but so far it seems smart in what it's suggesting for exercise routines. Maybe not perfect, but you can tweak what it recommends. The main reason I'm giving just 3 stars is the complete lack of charts and graphs showing my progress. For as advanced as this app seems, this is a glaring omission. Even simple more basic (and bad) apps have a visual way to track progress.

I like using this app to build my own workouts. I like that you can build circuits and supersets, see your past weights, etc. But currently the Android version is missing the ability to save a workout, and I'm finding this really hard to deal with. I try to do the same workout a couple times a week so I can see progress and having to manually enter it every single time is a pain. If this feature is not added by the time my subscription expires, I will probably not renew.

At $10 a month or $60 a year it's overpriced. There are features missing (at least on Android), like a plate calculator. That's not considering features a premium app should have (any sort of data analysis or metrics). It isn't clear that it actually helps you progress. There isn't a place to set goals, or track progress. It's buggy. If you select "body weight only" it still gives weighted exercises. There are issues with pullups in particular (it's given me over 90 before). Nice variety though.

Good & Bad I tried the trial version and I really like the app. Selecting available equipment was really good, the workout plans (with videos for exercises I was unsure of) are really good, and I like the fact that it will pre plan your next workout (that you can adjust if you want). The BIG draw back for me is the price. If it weren't so expensive I'd stick with this app, but as of now you've priced yourself out of my market and I'm forced to keep looking

I have finished the trial and overall I am impressed with the way this app is designed and how it functions. However, I am not impressed enough to spend 59.99 on it.....let alone repay that every year. I have nothing against developers making a profit for their labors; but this is ridiculously overpriced. For that price this should be a one time purchase. I can find similar options for free or much cheaper...and even if they fall a bit short they are at least reasonably priced.

This is a great app so far but it's missing IMO a crucial feature: the ability to take notes on a particular workout. I often want to monitor resistance levels on bands or make comments on how difficult or easy a workout was - in addition I like leaving notes for myself for future workouts. There's no way to make any kind of notes on a workout in the app - which really handicaps its function as an all-in-one workout app. I'd love to get a "notes" section in future versions of the app.

I just don't have any faith that the AI knows what it's doing. It seems to select exercises at random from whatever target muscle it wishes to do at that time and throws them together in nonsensical ways to construct a workout. It gives a lot of variety, but I'd even argue too much variety at many times. Also it is deeply disappointing how far behind the android app is from the apple one, and we don't even get a price break given that we don't have so many features.

Some bugs in the beginning, like everytime I tried to log a set, it skipped to the next exercise. Would be good if you plan it out and make the workout ahead of time. I felt like all I was doing at the gym was getting on my phone. Had to always change something up due to equipment availability. Chose upper/lower splits, but still never really got a full split, had to edit a lot. May revisit in the future, but I am fed up with subscriptions.

It's great for mixing up my workout routine and tracking my weight lifted, but for an app that requires a subscription, it's lacking a lot of features that free apps have. It doesn't allow you to log and track your body weight and measurements. It doesn't allow you to leave notes on your workout sets. It doesn't chart or export data. I feel these would be easy additions given the price we pay.

Edit: Further use shows annoying bug where if you are in the second to last set of a superset/circuit, then it doesn't advance to the next exercise once clicking "log set" on the first one. It'll goto the last set of the first exercise rather than advancing. Annoying bug. Adequate program design with the freedom to edit and create your own. Additionally, outline what equipment you have available. Best workout tracker app I have encountered and worth the yearly membership.

It's solid on a basic level but the version I'm paying for really should be free. It's fine for building my own exercises and supersets/circuits (finally) but it's got new annoying glitches such as locking me out of logging a workout in supersets, as in when I click the second workout to log it, it just brings me back to my first so I gotta readd it after every superset. Honestly, it really needs the ability to save workouts, along with cardio, warm-ups, and cool-downs. Plus bug fixes.

So far so good. I've been working with the app for a week but I've logged the past two months of my workouts in the app. Quality app with what it has but could use a few more tweaks. A notes option for each exercise would be great. Also, I'm quite data driven, so a plot of weight lifted, 1 rep maxes, and volume lifted, all versus time would be a nice add on. The workouts that are determined hit the full body over the course of a week.

Useful, but missing a lot of important features. No way to save and repeat a workout, can't share a workout with a friend, no supersets, has lots of exercises in the library but missing some really common ones, can't even scroll up and down when trying to view an old workout. Update: Looks like they fixed the scrolling problem for past workouts.

The exercises are great and the video explainations are excellent. The auto generated routines are good, but usually require adjustments, which is a time consuming pain before every workout. The app is lacking a lot of essential features I would expect at this price, like being able to redo a workout from the past, being able to adjust weights after a workout to say it they were too light when you know you need to progress, the ability to add notes to the log for workouts or specific exercises.

I'm currently 68 workouts in and overall really like the app. I've noticed several improvements since I joined. The only thing I'd really like added would be drop sets. Update. I've now been using the app for over a year and the last few months it's gone strait to 🗑. The excersise, weight and reps selection don't make any sense. You have to change almost every setting every workout. It'll also either freeze, restart or change your workout entirely mid workout. I've Uninstalled this app.

I've been using this app for about a year on iOS and love it but unfortunately the android version lags behind. Fitbod has the most flexibility for how you build your sets and a pretty good library of exercises, although it could be expanded. The biggest problem for me is that the android version doesn't allow you to save and load workouts, even if you have already completed a routine and just want to load it from your log. This means I have to recreate my workout every time I go to the gym.

App on Apple is amazing. Was super excited to see it on Android so I didn't have to bring my iPad into the gym. However the app on android is bad. It doesn't have all the features and bugs out on super sets. And I made the assumption that the workouts would show up on all my devices being they are all linked to the same email but that didn't happen. I'm going to stick to the apple app as it performs as it should.

Great app. I got the app wanting to get into better shape and not think about creating workouts for myself. I love how workouts are created based on equipment you input and are tracked. I didn't expect a subscription when I downloaded it and started using it. I didn't notice anything that spoke to a subscription, though I may have overlooked it. Overall I am very satisfied. Now I'm thinking about purchasing a subscription for my partner.

There's a lot of great things about this app, with a fair amount of bugs and idiosyncracies keeping it from being the best it can be. The AI-generated workouts are great, and each exercise comes with a demonstration video if you aren't familiar with certain exercises. However, the ability to use your at-home equipment brings a few issues to the table, notably when the AI assumes you have every distinct weight so it assigns you odd weight measurements, or sometimes even ignores your equipment.

The app is great, but there are a few downfalls, especially for being a yearly subscription: 1. You should be able to select a workout to repeat it and the app adjusts for progressive overload, based off repetitions, sets, and exercises of previous sessions. 2. You should DEFINITELY be able to add a custom exercise, regardless if it has a video and description paired with it. Tracking different variations of exercises or machines than what comes default in the app is crucial.

I like that the app allows you to input what type of equipment you have access to, then generates exercises based off the equipment you have. it would be nice if there were more exercises though. Especially for things like adductors. Or it would be nice if there were a way to input our own exercise. (one that isn't listed in the app.) I like that you are able to substitute some exercises for others, or build your own supersets.

I've been using this for a few months and it's a really great app. Weights/machines were always very intimidating to me but it's so easy to use and the exercise video how tos are perfect-short and clear. My only feedback would be the ability to add your own individual exercises. I see videos on Pinterest or IG occasionally that I want to incorporate into my routine, but they aren't in the app yet and there's no way to add your own exercise. Perhaps upload my own video or link in the exercise?

At first, I really liked this app. It got me focused on strength training for the first time. But the features they tout are garbage. Every week, they excitedly send a progress report with their proprietary "muscle score" which I liked b/c I'm focused on results But it's actually meaningless - showing 35-50% strength losses in one week, even if the logs show higher weight and intensity. Their staff "trainers" seem to be bots. There are far better products out there. Ending subscription.

I was asked to fill out all my info, including my email, before being shown the pricing. That felt like a bait-and-switch. I expected to see the pricing before sharing my email—not be lured into giving it first. Then probably comes the promotional emails I never asked for. This approach is frustrating and unprofessional. It turned me off from using the app entirely.

I love this app. There are so many options if you are not able to do some exercises. I'm not able to do all their recommendations (I'm on the heavier side), so they give me the option to replace them for an easier but yet challenging exercise. Update.... I want to know if there is a change you guys can add, cardio kickboxing, or dance on the cardio section?

The app is still lacking a lot of functionality that is present in the iOS version (eg. supersets), but requires you to pay the full price for the subscription. It also has some issues (eg. secondary muscle groups are usually ignored in excercise generation even when explicity selecting them). This is more of a beta at this point rather than a full port of the original iOS app. It has the potential, but for now, use the iOS version if you can. (Also TTS tracking of current workout would be nice)

Really like the app. Helps me plan out my workouts and stay more consistent with the gym and tracking my progress. The only reason for 3 stars and not 5 is because on IOS you can rate the difficulty of your weight, set and reps, which I believed changed the preset weight and reps for your next workout using that exercise. However, switching to android I cannot find this option. Also missing plate calculator. Would like for these to be added. Otherwise the app is great!

It's a decent app. It's good for adding some variety to your workouts if you're tired of doing the same few exercises all the time. My only real issue is that it's missing some exercises. To name a few: paused deadlift, tempo variations for SBD, pin press bench squat, but squat. Maybe an option to add a "custom exercise" for movements that aren't already on the app.

It's a good app. You can pick what you need to work out and what equipment is available but it's not free with in app purchases. You can only work out 3 times before being forced to pay to use. It should just come with a price tag right off the bat. I wanted an app that focuses on weights more than general exercise but FitOn is completely free to use unless you want extra things like meals or whatever. Then you'd pay their sub.

Great variety of workouts, poor implementation so far. when you sign up it asks questions about equipment you have available, but it still assumes a ton of equipment it doesn't ask about. you can then go into the settings and remove all equipment you don't have, but it doesn't stick. I've had to multiple times go back into the settings and again remove equipment so I don't get a suggested workout with half the lifts requiring equipment I don't have. please get better at this folks.

Downgrading from 3 to 2. The workout splits that were set in my gym are never followed anymore. I keep getting the same recommendations. Something seems to be off. Add that there have been no improvements since I've had the app. They seem to be moving backwards with functionality while others are moving forward. Used to recommend this app to everyone, but now I'm going back on that.

I like the app, specifically the progressive load algorithm. however, I find that it increases weight much too fast. possibly because I have it on hypertrophy advanced. increasing the weigh every week doesn't seem realistic, at least for me. It's not a huge deal as I can just adjust the weight. just looking for a tad more personalization. perhaps an AI prompt

Only had it for 1 wk & I have been pretty consistent for the 1st time in a long time. I like how the app tracks my exercises, picks them for me (which was my biggest issue) and allows me to replace them if needed. I am currently struggling w/shoulder pain & had to replace shoulder workouts w/lighter alternatives. That would be cool to have a feature where it could set up workouts with modifications for injuries instead of me having to choose alt. exercises manually. Overall I like it!

It's not as good on Android as it is on iphone. I used this app for two years on iphone and loved it. I'm not considering moving to an alternative since I changed to an Android phone. The Android version is missing key features such as saving custom workouts, viewing volume during a session, and it can't seem to keep my gym profile saved.

The app is easy to use, and provides copious workout regimens that can easily be altered to match your personal needs based on exercise comfort level and equipment access. Best geared towards people with a little experience(you should consult a personal trainer if you don't have experience anyways). My only complaint would be that timed exercises like planks can't currently use the same in app timer that is utilized for rest between sets, or at least I haven't figured that one out yet.

I've been using Fitbod for over 3 years, but the iOS version of this App is lightyears ahead and it's just frustrating. The Android version is missing equipment options for workouts (like resistance bands) and the ability to create multiple gym profiles, which REALLY came in handy on the iOS version. Another thing I've noticed is that rest timers don't always start automatically and sometimes won't move at all between sets; the time just pops up and stays there and I haven't found any rhyme or reason as to why. I didn't notice that back when I used the iOS version either. If they could just replicate the iOS features and functionality here on Android, it could be perfect.

Beautiful app with intuitive interface and lots of options to customize. Best features are the ability to select which equipment you have (please add battle ropes) + replace exercises on demand. The recent inclusion of cardio is a great improvement (treadmill, bike, etc). Please add the ability to ”block" certain workouts if they aren't compatible with injury etc. Subscription required - the free trial is 3 workouts but a full week would be a better testing period.

This app has given me the motivation to workout. Without a training partner to help push you during your workouts, I sometimes get very lazy with my routines. This app somehow allows me to mentally focus on finishing the routines it generates for me. It becomes addicting & fun while entering your sets/reps. I also enjoy how each workout has a short video showing you correct forms so you don't have to Google & waste time. I highly recommend this app for people with garage/home gyms.

I did the free trial, but I won't pay for this app. The workout creator is a good idea, but it keeps suggesting exercises I say to recommend less often. I tried putting in a workout, but that's so cumbersome. Just navigating the app is hard to figure out. I also wish I could switch some exercises from reps to time. And I don't like that it doesn't track PRs.

Support is non-existent. No phone number to call and talk to someone. You can only contact via email and it's been 6 days with no response. Also, I signed up for a free 1 month trial, put in my credit card info and they charged me immediately. If they ever get back to me and resolve my issues then I might edit my review. The app itself is pretty good. Is just ashame they have no support, but they will take your money.

I enjoy this app very much. The workouts are diverse, and the instructions are clear. One feature I would suggest to the developers is a PR diagnostic workout so that all muscle groups could have the perfect weight for the workouts. I have found that the weight called for in my workouts can sometimes be too light or too heavy. I work out every morning, so that may be contributing to it. I can't wait to see what other feature you have planned for the future.

Finally, a smart workout app. I love how it keeps track of what you've exercised and gives you exercises for your refreshed body parts. very flexible and lets you specify all the equipment that you have and will only recommend exercises that you can do with your equipment. I love not having to think too much about what I'm going to do in my next workout. it helps me keep things fresh.

My favorite workout app but I'll admit it can be pricey for what you get. I absolutely love that it gives me great recommendations and figuresout suggested weights for me. That being said, if I say I don't have equipment, it may still recommend a cardio bike for example. There will sometimes be repeat exercises listed with different names which can get frustrating. And there are also times when the images/videos don't match the description which can be confusing for those new to exercises.

Great app, excellent exercises to choose from. I love how for each exercise there is a video as well as a description of each exercise. I dinged it a star because it would be great to add notes for each exercise. It would also be beneficial if it showed a way to modify each exercise. During the questionnaire they could ask if you have any injuries to accommodate exercises that way. I find I need to replace or modify a lot of exercises because I have a permanently injured right arm.

Love this app. there are some shortcomings but if you are consistent it will help. Here is the reply for further feedback. The app creates workouts but, it fails to accommodate. If I have not worked out in months due to some reason it won't check how long has it been since the last work out. It doesn't recommend me either weights or bands to start again. If I'm new I can't lift 35lbs to do plate twist, it should start of with 5-10lbs max. Maybe ask"how would you like to start back?"

I've used fitbod for a few months now. at first, it was good. not anymore. here are the main issues: 1. rather than adding weight to an exercise, it will seemingly limitlessly increase reps instead. this has led to workouts like 5 sets of 36 reps of air squats, for example. 2. a few weeks ago, barbell exercises broke completely. the app no longer recommends anything over 45 lbs despite me having an extensive history of lifting more weight. I have to manually adjust every workout.

Dropped to one star after learning about the lack of parity between the Android version and the iOS version. I like the app, but I paid for premium and I don't even get warm ups, cool downs, cardio, specifying which specific weights you have access to, i mean the list goes on. I'll gladly switch back to 5 stars once this is fixed. I'm in for a year subscription already so I'm stuck with this app.

Awesome app! I was getting tired of my lifting routine and remembered hearing about this app from the boys over at The Official Podcast numerous times, so I gave it a shot and I wouldn't go back. The AI-generated workouts are awesome, and it's nice to know that my workouts every other day are maximizing my gains. Pro tip: go to fitbod.me/OFFICIAL to get 25% off a yearly subscription after your free trial, it's a great deal for everything you're getting with this app. 👍

My workouts were getting stale after years of the push/pull/legs splits and I had plateaud a while ago. This app gave me totally new splits and exercises that had never occured to me before. I'm now stronger and look better than I have ever been and I'm in my early 40s. It pushes me each workout and allows each muscle group the right amount of time to recover. Only small complaint is there is not way to export the raw data. I would love to be able to plug a CVS file in Excel. Great app 👍

It's an amazing app for all types of people and it's very user friendly, you can work on certain muscle groups and can modify every one of them to your liking or even build your own workouts. You have to ability to tell the app which equipment you have or not, and the programs really work with you. The only problem I have is that they make it really difficult to use the app without buying a membership, which really discourages low income people (like me) from continuing to use the app.

Started using this app about two years ago and I was 120lbs. I am now 195 and feel like a Greek god. The trick to making this app effective is to get your own split, click the three dots in the corner, click create workout from scratch, and then click pick target muscle groups. You can then choose which ones to use according to your split. I recommend setting the time to 1hr 15mins for maximum results. It worked for me and I believe it can work for anybody if you stick with it.

Only get this app for Apple products. My friend with an iPhone has so many more features that I don't have, he also doesn't pay for it whereas I do. You get cardio exercises and other equipment, you can hold an exercise and the video will pop up, and it just seems to run quicker and more seamless on his phone.

Once I figured out what to click, it was super easy to use during exercise. I love being able to input available equipment, duration of workout desired, rest length desired between sets, and the type of workout I was going for. Being able to swap out like exercises when certain equipment was in use at my gym was very helpful in getting me done on time.

The app works well, though was a bit buggy as I encountered an issue where an exercise did not start losing until I went back to my main workout and clicked on it again. What is a showstopper for me is the lack of integrations with Google Fit and/or Strava. I will revisit the app once the integrations are implemented. Update 01/06/2022 -- More than one year later, new UI, yet still no Strava integration. This is needed so I can sync my workout across other services, using Strava as a bridge.

I think the idea for this app is really good. I love the video examples and the massive exercise library. My main problem is not being able to save your custom workouts. That is a huge deal breaker for me and, I think, a fatal flaw in the system. I would have considered paying the membership if it weren't for this lack of a feature. Such a cool idea that forgot the basics.

Terrible app experience I really wanted to try this app, but seems to not be working adequately for android. Making an account was easy but if you happened to log out, its not possible to log back in, it gives you like an error saying that your account was made on a different platform, and then thats it, I cant log in again. And customer support is nonexistent so far. This is an app that will charge 12.99 per month or around 80 bucks per year. Unless this issue is fix I cant reconmed this.

Just want to say again. CROOKS!!! When trying to cancel, its set up so when you click change subscription it tells you you subscribed for a year, then one month, no verification, just charge, no answer to customer service. Cancel is small button at the bottom but they still charged me 13 months of service in 15 seconds with a cancel.

Better in ios (I guess). My friend referred this app to me as a simple and effective workout tracker that syncs to Strava that I use for jogging. Well, the android version doesn't sync to strava, and I'm not even able to save my custom routine for some reason. I build it out and log it, but it won't save and I'll have to do that again next time which is a bummer. Might return when it gets closer to ios, uninstalling for now

This app is life changing! I've been more consistent with it than any other app or workout plan. If you don't feel up to the challenge, then just do less than is recommended, but always use the log to track your progress. It's better to keep going, even if it's light, vs pushing yourself to the point of dreading it and quitting 🙃.

I love this app. It has helped me break through a plateau, and I am seeing progress again in both strength and body composition. I also love that you can create multiple gym profiles. This is great for me because I travel constantly for work. My workouts on the road are generated based only on the equipment listed in the alternate profile, eliminating my need to swap out exercises.

This app is pretty amazing! It's completely customizable as far as what equipment you have, how many reps, how many sets you want to do, the amount of time you want to work out, what time you want to work out, the days you want to work out etc. I haven't been this excited to work out in a long time and it's because of this app!!! I wasted quite a bit of money trying out apps and each one of them add some sort of issue or something in them that didn't work for me. this ones perfect so far!!

I've been using FitBod for a while now, but after over a year, it's become clear their "roadmap" for the Android version is just something they say whenever people ask why the iOS version has more features and updates, yet it's the Android version is same price. There's no real ETA on when Android will be on par with iOS from what I've seen, so I can no longer recommend this app.

I want to love this app. The exercise variety is awesome, especially for a beginner like me. I also like that I can choose the equipment that my gym offers. I would give 5 stars if the Android version worked as well as the iPhone version. I really wish I could look at the logged workouts and see the entire list and be able to scroll through it. It would also be nice to track cardio too eventually.

It's a great app for both beginners and aficionados! You can choose all of your available equipment, tailor your workouts for whatever your goal may be, add or remove exercises based on your experience or what you want to work on. you can even choose body weight only exercises if need be. And the app shows what muscle groups you most recently worked and will give you sets on the next work out that targets different muscle groups.

feeling great! 👍 the app selects the exercises with plenty of variety and customizable everything and records it all. I used to write my workouts in books 📚 I still have them somewhere. returning to exercise feels 💪 after a break. The app takes much of the stress out of hitting the muscles groups, using the bars and weights and bands etc. I told it I have. I'm feeling much stronger and confident and looking forward to the next workout!

Coming from an iPhone, I support this app because it is such a great workout planner. However, there are a lot of features not added in yet, just so you know. On iPhone, there is a plate calculator, achievement tracker, and the ability to "show more" when replacing an exercise. Not a huge loss, but I cannot wait for this app to be at par to the iPhone one. Just know that you are not getting all of the features when you pay your subscription. I understand this is a beta app.

A suggestion/ad caused me to download and purchase this app by mistake, thinking that it was something else. But it turned it to be pretty good. It suggests a healthy mix of exercises that keep each muscle group working and recovering. I like the timer and how to videos. The one thing that it really needs is support for negative weight values on bodyweight exercises where someone may need assistance to do dips, pull-ups, or similar movements.

Brilliantly designed app. Been using about two weeks, and I can already feel and see some results. You can adjust almost everything about your workout, and the app does a great job of providing some necessary guidance. The demonstration videos for every exercise are also simple and informative. I wish the workout timer were visible at all times, as I often forget to restart it, and I don't understand why it's impossible to add certain accessory groups like forearms. But overall, super helpful.

Absolutely pathetic how this is a barebones version of the same iOS app. I bought a month of it (and regret it!) thinking it was the same thing as my wife's app. The selling point for me was the exertion rating, i.e the app learns how much weight you can lift and automatically adjusts your next workout depending on whether or not your workout was easy or hard. THIS DOES NOT EXIST ON ANDROID. Lots of other iOS features are also non-existent. Useless app now and a waste of money.

This app is great! I've been using it for over a year and the results have been amazing! The UI/UX are really well thought out and designed, everything is customizable, reps, sets, supersets, rest duration etc. It keeps track on your muscles how exhausted they are, and it'll plan your workouts accordingly. There's a lot more and the price is definitely worth it!

Pros: can create GYMs with different equipment for dynamic workouts. Easy to swap an exercise during the workout if it's not available. This introduces muscle confusion that I wasn't getting prior and I am seeing plateaus broken. Reports mailed and over time in settings. One of best UIs. Cons: No integrations in or out. No ability to add things like drop sets, pyramids, rest pause.. Good app to mix it up, not really AI though, if it could use generative AI it would be a beast of an app.

Extremely handy for a beginner with no idea what to do! It's nice to be able to swap workouts on the fly and change muscle groups. My only complaint would be the supersets. The supersets keeps suggesting two exercises that target the same muscle, like dumbbell curl to hammer curl. I usually just supplement with an exercise that targets a different muscle myself and log it so It's not a major issue. I still highly recommend this app above any the others though, it's awesome!

I love the app, recommends you weight amount to lift (I wish I could adjust them because I'm not that strong yet) logging work outs and rest periods are easy. The only issue is the $80.00 for a whole year. Gym memberships are already expensive to start with now 80 for the whole year. I just started working out and do not have that kind of money. $40.00 for the year I could do or $50.00 to buy the app no problem. You only get 3 workouts before it requires a membership, you can even get a full 5

Pretty good. Love that it pre-selects entire weight training routines for you depending on your goals and how you prefer to train (e.g. push-pull days, upper/lower body split, full body workout). Super user-friendly and clean, clear UI. Decent selection of exercises, though missing quite a few common ones. Would love to see the ability to add drop sets and pyramid sets to your workout sets. Seems a little pricey at $10/month ($60/year) but would be worth it if it had a bigger exercise database.

This app has transformed my workout experience. It has helped me explore new exercises to work my muscles in different ways, and it makes it very easy to track my sets and progress. I also like the easy access to instructional videos for very exercise, in case I want to check my form. I do wish the integration with my Samsung Watch 4 was stronger; it seems like a bit of an afterthought right now.

The lack of adequate loop band exercise support makes this app difficult to use. I don't always have access to free weights. And I don't want to just rely on body weight exercises as I have invested in bands during the pandemic. There's a setting to adjust band level, but barely any band exercises. I don't understand... Am I missing something here?

App is great, prices are a scam. Was using the free trial and was excited to see that it had a very good price which was 60/year. I decided to wait for black Friday and possibly buy it with discount when I noticed that the price went up to 70$/y and with 20% discount which totalled around 55$. I couldn't believe that they increased the price to offer that "discount". Due to those circumstances I decided to give this app a 2nd try but nope. The price is now at 80$. Becareful of what you buy.

It's been a great app overall. The only suggestion I could possibly make is that the rest periods don't track on the watch, so I have to have my phone out. There's also some equipment at my gym that I can't add in the app. That said, it creates great workouts and does a good job rotating muscle groups and building up.

Android app still doesn't have the same fictionally as the iOS app. Need the ability to put in specific weights of kettlebells and dumbbells so I don't have to constantly change the weights. Need the ability to permanently remove exercises that I don't want to do our physically can't perform instead of constantly deleting. It's very annoying.

The idea and overall concept of the app is cool. The numbers are nowhere near proportional to what I actually put up in the gym. For example, I tried creating an upper body bench for my body weight at 159lbs and the app said to do 115lbs. In my school's weight room I did 185lbs for 7 reps and 175lbs for 11 reps. The app needs to have a function to where the user can manually edit the numbers to adjust accordingly to the numbers which they are actually doing.

It's not a bad app, other than the complaints from others that the app doesn't seem to pair with other ecosystems, and the fact that it's apparently missing major features from its iOS version, I don't mind it. However, what I do mind is not being able to adjust the weight you can do for an exercise, I just started my fitness journey, I can't do 60lbs yet.

Whoa, this app works really well for me. It's the best of them I've used. Easy to switch exercises, you can choose body weight only, add cardio, a lot of options. I turned on the addition of warm up sets and turned off supersets (personal pref) and it's doing great. It tracked your weights and sets you up for progressive overload. I'm really enjoying this so far, nice job!!

Honestly, I was a bit skeptical, but it has a good system for me to get back in shape with, as well as what fitness level you're currently at. Its relatively intuitive about what you worked out to prepare you for the next workout. Its helped me stick to working out in general so I'd give it a try. It might not be for some depending what you prefer to do, but from what I see it should suit everyone, and their needs for choosing a fitness app. Good luck.

Hmmm... really liked the app, especially the circuit workouts and overall premade plans.. but I also have JeFit. With only being able to "try" this for 3!!! Days... I honestly can't tell, at this point, how it would be worth the fee?? I get most all of this, more exercise (mainly machines) choices i think, from JeFit FREE version???? I'd recommend longer trials if you have more to offer!

I have been super impressed with this app so far. So much so that I didn't even wait until the trial was done to go ahead and purch a full 1 yr sub. It is one of the very few that I have used that allows you to tell it exactly what equip you have at home. Give it your ht/wt, your level of experience, your goals and type of workout and it does the rest. Also very unobtrusive to use during a workout. However, it is in dire need of integrations with, fitbit, etc.

Really need to update to make closer to apple. I work out with a friend who is iOS and we don't have all the same workout selections. It would be really nice if we could share between our workouts too. So far it seems apple has the superior app. Please catch Android up quickly. Differences between them are frustrating.

This app has the promise of making things intuitive and personalized. But I cannot fathom why it is so glitchy. You change an unrelated setting (like the rest timer) in the middle of an ongoing workout, and the app will change the entire workout. It doesn't even remember your equipment settings. Every day it spits out a workout that's entirely based on body weights. And you have to unselect body weight only setting.

The best fitness app I've ever used, its the first one to get me to the gym regularly and confidently. The ability to quickly replace exercises for alternatives if an area or peice of equipment isn't free is absolutely the best if you have a crowded gym or are not super comfortable talking to other people while working out. It seems to escalate my work outs based on what I did before. You can set it up so a certain exercise is included every time, great for including physical therapy HW.

I have used this app on both iPhone & Android for the past 2 or 3 years. Switching from the iPhone last year was a disappointment as many features are missing on the Android side. Their customer service states the iPhone team had a two-year head start, but in the intervening time, the Android app has not progressed. I reached out to support 3 times in the past week via email, their website, and through the app - I haven't received a response as of yet. Save your money. Get an app with support. EDIT (1/31/22): Well folks. All I can say is I tried. I began trying to contact them 10 days ago because my subscription was about to expire and I needed some help. Here we are... 10 days later with no response. I assume they are out of business.

No android integration even though it's been promised for years, even strava would be nice. Starting weights don't make sense, as if the app was designed by people who don't lift. Example, 160 lbs for bench press. (160-45)/2=57.5 per side. Not really normal split. 55 per side would be more reasonable. It would help if you could do math in the weight field to calculate this. Barbell exercises should separate bar weight and weight per side, it would greatly simplify each exercise.

Love this app. I use it almost everyday. I like that they keep updating it with new features like the ability to specify custom dumbbell weights so the app will only recommend sets with your available weights. I just wish this was available to be added to the Google Play Family Library so that my spouse could also use the app if they wanted without having to pay a separate subscription. We can share a peloton subscription with different profiles, but not a Fitbod subscription.

I searched the play store for a protein tracker and this was the sponsored result. After signing up and configuring my app, I discovered there's no nutrition tracker in this app. I'm further disappointed there are no cardio options. *edit- I was able to find cardio options after the developer helped me. Maybe that should be more intuitive to access (like when selecting workout equipment).

I love this app it does everything I need it too. I'd love an option to add notes to an exercise or even just a selection after (how'd this feel? With Easy moderate difficult selections) I love the auto recommend of weight/sets but I find it doesn't match up with what I want (5 sets 5 reps always, variable weight as I get stronger) so I have to manually adjust which is fine, but being able to track difficulty of a exercise on that day would help with me knowing when I should push to a new weight

After using it for a couple months there are several missing features that make it difficult to justify the cost. First, they dont disclose that you can't move back and forth between Android and iOS (don't link me the parity update. It is 2021, this should have never been developed as isolated systems.) No saved workouts, inability to exclude certain exercises (rage at being suggested combinations of supermans every workout), and ability to edit past workouts are all missing.

This has been a great app for me. I've been using it for about 5 months. I started with the free trial then opted to buy it for a year. I've seen great results and I'm stoked about the progress I have made. It makes setting up exercises easy. I typically do upper/lower split and I add more exercises than what's listed, but the app is simple, effective, and if you put in the work; you'll see results. My only complaint is I wish it could track running for calorie counting all in one place.

It's been great to help me build workouts while I'm recovering from injuries. It's easy to switch out exercises if there's something that I'm not capable of doing right now. I wish they had a nutritional aspect to this as well; such as a food diary/calorie intake/macro intake tracker as well as AI recommendations for filling gaps in nutritional necessities. I'm overall happy with it so far, though.

Classic bait-and-switch. Looks good, gets you hyped, makes one workout then slaps you with "unlock your next workout with a free trial!" insinuating it's a paid only app, but only after you give them all your information, then make it impossible to delete you account and remove info from their servers. This app wants nothing more than to sell your data and workouts that almost exactly match what a planet fitness membership gives you. You replied. how about put a button to delete in the app.

I've used the app a few times for complete work outs and I love it! Everything is customizable and tailored to your needs or wants. There is a slight learning curve with figuring some things out but you can literally click on everything and anything to change it. I've already recommended it to 5 people. I can't wait to see my results! The only issue that some people may have is that expert folks in the gym may want more variety in workout choices and I wish there were a few more modern exercises

Oh man this app is so close to being perfect, but my only qualm is how basic the search algorithm is and how it's only able to handle terms that appear in the right order. I.e if I'm looking for exercises that contain the works "side" and "raise", unless it's ".. side raise..", the pattern matching will yield no results like "side weighted raise". This makes searching useless unless you know the exact wording order.

I was hoping for a simple weight lifting app. I thought this was the one, but certain issues keep it from being the solution I'm looking for. The program doesn't do as I put in the settings. For instance I list all the equipment I have, but the app creates plans using things I excluded. I have to manually change plans daily which is frustrating. I also wanted to program upper body day and lower body the next, but the app doesn't seem to do that either. Fix these and it would be a quality app.

Not worth the fee right now on Android. I think they should make sure people know that. What would be smart is if they let the Android version run for free for now until they actually add the features to make a payment worth it. Or at least discount it from the full price. I liked the 3 day trial (so generous...) Maybe will come back and look at this again when they actually take care of the Android users.

As others have mentioned if you originally used this app on ios and now you're using the android version you're going to be disappointed (a little bit). The biggest gripe is a fitness app that doesn't integrate with fitness apps such as Strava, Samsung Health or Google Fit. Also, no achievements, limited selection of exercises when substituting, etc.. With that being said it is still a FANTASTIC app that I use almost daily. I look forward to future updates!

This app solved my workout inconsistency problem. I'm approaching 150 workouts completed. It's the best fitness habit I've adopted since playing organized high school sports. For the Fitbod team... I just need the android Fitbit integration. Not a deal breaker, just being needy. I'd still recommend this app to anyone looking for a confident way to navigate any gym with a clear workout plan. 6/5 stars!

This app is great. Awesome for someone who works out alone and not paying for a trainer. It pushes me to do exercises I wouldn't normally do or think about doing. Which in turn has me feeling the changes in new ways. My wife has noticed the difference also. For the developer. Please add the estimated 1 rep max back to the android app. That was huge for my aspiring goals mindset. Anything that can hype me up to push harder is welcome. Thanks for the great work. Don't remove features.

Hi! I didn't give the app 5 stars because it doesn't interface with Google Fit or MyFitnessPal. It would also be killer if you could input goals (bench press weight, muscle ups, etc.) and the algorithm would work you to your goal. Other than that the app has a lot of cool features and I use it several times a week.

There are lot of positives in the app. The database is pretty good. The profile feature allows me to select workouts based on home profile or gym profile depending on equipmemt selected for each profile. I just don't feel that the workout generation is effective. There is no "progression" or "plan" that I can sense from the automated workout generation. Given the price tag, I would expect the app also help me set and reach goals.

I really love this app, though for the loop bands it would be great if we could set the tension for them because all bands are different tension from different companies, I have rubberbanditz and they are all different, also, stepping on the full loop band for biceps would be a great workout add. edit. I mean the tension of that bands color example, black is 20-50 pounds, orange 5-15 pounds.

You can enter what equipment you have and the app will target a muscle group or groups with the given equipment. it's perfect for since I just do random things when I'm at the gym. Helpful to have a routine made for me. I can always replace exercise too, in case it's too hard for now. Plus it tracks your sets and has timers for those planks and other timed workouts. It's perfect

I really like the workout plans. I have been using it for a little over a year and I think it's incredibly worth it. If the developers read this, this is the best workout app I have used! Recommended to tons of friends. Edit 7/6/2025 Three years later and still using the app! Nearly 500 workouts and still going, instrumental in my weight loss! Thank you! Stop messing with the UI 😂

The positive: you can create different gyms like big fitness gym with all the gear and home gym with almost nothing. It's also easy to swap exercises. The bad: the Android version is a totally different experience from the iPhone, I was going to rate 3 stars but they get 2 because of that. The promises to update Android to be equivalent go back 2 years, so I'm calling bs. You get 3 workouts before paying, that's not enough. I'm on week 6, has it confused me with someone else?

OK, so I love this app. I went from 160lbs to 180lbs in about 3 months of using it, and i am progressing quickly in what I can lift. It does a good job of providing workouts for your day and is customizable for your desired results. This would be 5 stars if there were a way to replace an exercise with any exercise of your choosing not just the ones the app let's you replace, mabey there is, but then it should be easier to do so. Also I wish there a web page version of the app

I've been testing this app out lately, and it really does a great job at many things, but does struggle in a few key areas that could make it a true powerhouse. 1) You cannot add equipment or exercises into the main list. 2) Very little integration with other apps, weight scales, or fitness trackers. Needs support (android) for more and ability to sync in the app or... 3) No web based page to make adjustments or see progress online, other than the app (which is very basic). Very good so far though, B rating.

The paid subscription is honestly the way to go if you dont have a trainer and need a plan. I love that I can punch in most of my equipment and it has a routine that is easily editable from reps, to weights, to sets, to even if I dont like that exercise. You can switch on the fly. The how to videos are great and also the ability to display what muscle groups are being worked/targeted. So, if I feel something is being used that isn't targeted, I can make adjustment. 5/5

Great workout app. Gives me tons of workouts that can be auto-generated or i can edit to specific muscle groups etc. You can create super sets and watch the "how to" video on each exercise. Only thing i would knock on is the ability to plan ahead of time flr multiple workouts. I want to create 4 workouts for the week and plan but i am unable. Also, during workouts, starting a set and ending a set does not automatically transition you to the next exercise.

Great app so far. I really like the workouts and I've been letting the app generate my whole workout routine for the past 3 months and have actually seen results. However I'm not giving full 5 stars because there are a LOT of features missing in the android app compared to the iOS version. Mostly I really would like to be able to connect to Google Fit.

This is a really good app for creating different work outs based on the equipment you have. It does a good job of varying exercises each day based on fitness goals. My wife has the iPhone app though and I wish the Android app had all the same features. The iPhone app recommends supersets and also estimates 1 rep maxes, which would be nice. Some more tracking of weight lifted would be nice too. That would be nice to see progress for volume lifted over time for a specific exercise.

I love the app but I do think that the heavy bag should be added, possibly boxing and kickboxing separately and the ability to pause the workout (I do my warmups at home and it's a 10-15 minute drive to the gym but I can't pause in between). More workouts would be cool too but that's my only gripe. Very easy and user friendly. Takes the guesswork out of everything. A perfect app for those that love to workout but either get bored with their workouts or get to the gym and can't decide what to do.

Good concept, LOTS of room to improve. I enjoy using it to keep me on pace to get through workouts quickly, but there are a lot of minor inconveniences that I assume will be fixed sooner or later since this app seems relatively new. The auto-generated workouts are often times not feasible or optimal so you have to go and adjust it manually which is also a pain to navigate at first, and there are several exercises that don't exist in their library. Also there isnt a timer for planks/cardio

Unless you're going to pull the trigger on the subscription, don't waste your time. This looks like it could be a good app. With a trial period of three workouts, though, it is hard to tell. The subscription price of $12.99 a month is absurd for an app though. I have a subscription to Photoshop. A program so versatile and complex no one knows how to use all the features and it cost only 10.99 a month. This is dumb pricing. At half the cost they would get more than twice the takers. A third better yet. If you want $12.99 a month, you're going to have to get me addicted and three workouts isn't even a taste.

This app is a lie. It states free workouts but then, it wants to charge you. I've used it for ONLY 1 week. It suddenly stated that my trail is over, if I wish to continue, pay monthly or annually. I NEVER pressed anything to start a trail. I understand a limited workout based app which is completely fine by me. However, if you are going to create and app that that says free, mean it! I really enjoyed it and expected more out of it. To bad I'll be having to remove it

The app is purely "okay". Expect more from an app that requires a subscription compared to some free ones. Some wants: - Independent rest timer from the exercises - A rest timers be completely custom and not minus/plus ten only from the base time - Custom exercises -Custom gym equipment - Notes for exercises custom or included. Would be nice to have a note for Banded pushups where I could include "1.5 reps, eccentric" or w/e - Be able to make any exercise time based

Missing a lot of features that I was expecting but looks like for some reason they started from scratch with android and it doesn't even have the same backend as the iOS app. Plate calculator is missing, it almost never increases weight, and you can't exclude excersises so I constantly have to find a sub for cleans I don't want to do. Definitely not complete enough to be worth 60 a year.

so far so good with this app. pro's - you see a guide with every workout so you're doing proper form. Tons of exercises on here. layout is also nice and easy to use Cons - wish there were a way you can add how much weight you can already lift so the exercises can be more tailored to your strength gain, I'm constantly having to bump up the weight because I can do more than what's given ( if there's already a setting there please tell me) otherwise I'm enjoying this app!

Great workout app that can tailor workouts to the gym, home and a mixture in between with equipment you have available. I love that the app shows you how to perform the various exercises using video and a typed explanation. Also like that you can substitute exercises with those you may be more comfortable or familiar with. Had to bump it down one star since the Android version doesn't have some equipment that the iOS version has (resistance bands) and that i can't log into my profile across OSs.

Great for generating workouts and that's about it. No way to log individual exercises, or when a set is complete, so tracking is awful unless you're doing a full workout. The equipment tracking doesn't work correctly and recommends exercises that aren't viable. No way to indicate that they prefer low-impact exercises when injured so I have to manually reject every exercise, but can't easily add them back when I feel up to it. Massive number of improvements need to be made tbh.

Can't get ahold of customer service. I've sent two contact requests in the last week to assist me in logging in to the account I created when I redeemed a promo code for a free month trial and they took my credit card information to do that free month trial so that way it converts to a paid subscription after the trial. I can't login to that account tied to that credit card. I am very frustrated that on the Android side of it there seems to be a complete lack of customer service to fix problems.

Amazing app! It is so flexible, and the subscription is totally worth it. It let's you cycle through different sets of workouts, and keeps track of what muscle groups to hit in your next workout. It lets you choose how much fitness equipment you have so it can curate a good workout, even if you have none!

Seriously worth the money. This app is exactly what I have been looking for. You're able to put in your goals, what equipment (if any) is available to you and it creates a workout specifically for your goals and equipment. You're also able to replace the exercise with others with the same type of workout and there are videos very every one.

I may keep w/this, but gave 3stars because I don't like downloading "free" app to learn only 3 are free & feel deceived. I tried this on friend recommendation & he was right in terms of helpful & efficient. I saw in another review there is supposed to be sound & experienced no sound on mine-which would be very helpful. I would suggest more than 1 equipment profile for those who alternate workouts in gym with ALL equipment with home/park/court/track spots.

The app is great and love the fact that it automatically generates your workout plan. You can also manually adjust it if needed. I'm only having one issue. The only issue I'm having is when the rest timer goes off it lowers my music and stays that way. I need to disable and re-enable my Bluetooth and close my music app. Then the music will go back to the regular sound level. It's annoying and hopefully there is a fix soon.

I've been using this app for about 3 years now for all my strength training workouts. Phenomenal app, and excellent content is shared via email whether it's a workout report, new podcast announcement or fitness tip. Definitely worth the yearly subscription price, and I'd recommend it to anyone who's serious about their workouts.

I really like the idea, but the weight/rep suggestions are way off sometimes. I'd say they're good the majority of the time, but then it'll suggest a weight VERY far outside of your training level for some lifts. I've only been using the app for a few weeks, so I'm hoping the AI is still in a learning phase or something. I'll update if/when I notice a change in accuracy, but so far the AI (the main appeal of this particular app) isn't meeting my expectations.

The pros: organization is great and I love that it keeps track of muscle recovery. It tracks your workout and makes you rest which is super helpful. It was a big upgrade from my paper/pen. The cons: it thinks an intermediate level female can do 4 different kind of pull-ups and gives me the same workout as my husband, resulting in me having to replace exercises for over 50% of my work out. i wish I didn't pay for it but I mean if you're capable of doing all the options it might work out.

Love the app! The only thing I wish could be added is that when editing the weight amount I wish the app would adjust the number of reps based on the weight change. I don't always have access to certain dumbells they suggest to use so find myself having to adjust the weight to match what I have and then guess how to balance that out with the number of reps. Wish this could be an automated adjustment. Other than that, awesome app still 5 stars!

It'd be cool if we could add our own custom exercises while we wait for it to be added to the app in updates. This way we can log everything in this app instead of using another and not miss anything we did. This would make our workout history data more accurate since as of now we can't add exercises that dont exist on the app.

I initially liked this app and was paying for unlimited workouts month to month. However, today I went to workout and it told me i needed to subscribe to gain access to unlimited workouts. The app recognized I pay month to month but still would not let me continue. I have requested a refund and will no longer use this to track my progress.
