I was intrigued with the machine learning aspect of this app. While I like the idea of some of the concepts, I feel that this app just fails to deliver...especially since you have to pay for it. You'll see the reviews mention the same "cons" that I noticed. There seems to be a better ios version that android version. For example, if you want to copy a workout that you did previously...apparently you can do that in ios but not android. Why would you need to do that if the app intelligently creates your workout? Well, there are a few reasons, but sometimes it creates odd superset combinations--pairing 2 completely different exercises that wouldn't fly in a normal gym like bouncing between the barbell bench and dumbbells or pairing 2 similar exercises that work the same muscle and defeat the purpose of supersetting 2 exercises that work different muscles to cut down overall rest time. Another reason to create your own workout would be if you had a specific set of exercises given by your doctor or physical therapist, but that's a challenge if not all of those exercises are available in the app because you can't add your own and you can request an exercise to be added, but I haven't seen even one of my requests added over the few months that I've been using the app. Also, one of the features that I looked forward to was calculating an increase in reps/weights, but it does some weird stuff sometimes...like increasing from 20 pound dumbbells to 22.5 pound dumbbells...I'm not sure I've seen 22.5 pound dumbbells. A nice feature is that you can edit the equipment in your gym, but it doesn't help when you can specify if your gym has "dumbbells" but not indicate that your gym doesn't carry the standard 22.5 pound dumbbell. One last issue that I would mention is that while the concept of selecting exercises for a specific muscle is nice and the idea of managing muscle fatigue by generating a workout for muscles with the most rest is nice, this app just fails at it. While it may be doing possibly more work in the background, I can give an example of what I'm talking about. Let's take the deadlift as an example since it works multiple muscles--hamstrings, glutes, traps, and abs just to name a few. The only muscle group that the deadlift is available on the app is for the hamstrings. So if you want a glute workout, it's not an option. I can only imagine that this logic is carried through by not calculating any fatigue on the glute and only for the hamstring when the deadlift is recorded. That's just one example, but there are tons of exercises that work multiple muscle groups...I just don't see this app being that intelligent. Bottom line, this app would be good if some of the logic was cleaned up, but it's not free so it's definitely not worth it. I feel compelled to use it since I paid for it, but it's really not the best app that I have for working out. A note for the App author, it would probably be better if it were free and maybe had a little advertising in it to provide the revenues, or at least some free version to help get more subscribers with more workable data for you to use.

* Updated because it's been 7 months and this feature has yet to be added despite them saying they were working on it. * IOS version allows you to go into more detail with weights. I'm tired of having to edit my workouts everything to fit the equipment I have in my home gym. It's constantly giving me weights that I either can't produce with my weight plates or is higher than possible with my plates. When this is finally added to android I will give 5 stars.

It's a pretty good app, but there are too many limitations for paying $60/year. There's no Google Fit integration. There's no way to dislike certain exercises. The exercises themselves are extremely limited. For example, I can't log heel touches--LOL. Updated to one stars: you can't copy and do a past workout, save a template, nor add a superset once the workout started. Really not user friendly. I recommend a strong pass on this app.

App on Apple is amazing. Was super excited to see it on Android so I didn't have to bring my iPad into the gym. However the app on android is bad. It doesn't have all the features and bugs out on super sets. And I made the assumption that the workouts would show up on all my devices being they are all linked to the same email but that didn't happen. I'm going to stick to the apple app as it performs as it should.

The app is great, but there are a few downfalls, especially for being a yearly subscription: 1. You should be able to select a workout to repeat it and the app adjusts for progressive overload, based off repetitions, sets, and exercises of previous sessions. 2. You should DEFINITELY be able to add a custom exercise, regardless if it has a video and description paired with it. Tracking different variations of exercises or machines than what comes default in the app is crucial.

At first, I really liked this app. It got me focused on strength training for the first time. But the features they tout are garbage. Every week, they excitedly send a progress report with their proprietary "muscle score" which I liked b/c I'm focused on results But it's actually meaningless - showing 35-50% strength losses in one week, even if the logs show higher weight and intensity. Their staff "trainers" seem to be bots. There are far better products out there. Ending subscription.

I was asked to fill out all my info, including my email, before being shown the pricing. That felt like a bait-and-switch. I expected to see the pricing before sharing my email—not be lured into giving it first. Then probably comes the promotional emails I never asked for. This approach is frustrating and unprofessional. It turned me off from using the app entirely.

It's not as good on Android as it is on iphone. I used this app for two years on iphone and loved it. I'm not considering moving to an alternative since I changed to an Android phone. The Android version is missing key features such as saving custom workouts, viewing volume during a session, and it can't seem to keep my gym profile saved.

Support is non-existent. No phone number to call and talk to someone. You can only contact via email and it's been 6 days with no response. Also, I signed up for a free 1 month trial, put in my credit card info and they charged me immediately. If they ever get back to me and resolve my issues then I might edit my review. The app itself is pretty good. Is just ashame they have no support, but they will take your money.

Dropped to one star after learning about the lack of parity between the Android version and the iOS version. I like the app, but I paid for premium and I don't even get warm ups, cool downs, cardio, specifying which specific weights you have access to, i mean the list goes on. I'll gladly switch back to 5 stars once this is fixed. I'm in for a year subscription already so I'm stuck with this app.

Only get this app for Apple products. My friend with an iPhone has so many more features that I don't have, he also doesn't pay for it whereas I do. You get cardio exercises and other equipment, you can hold an exercise and the video will pop up, and it just seems to run quicker and more seamless on his phone.

Just want to say again. CROOKS!!! When trying to cancel, its set up so when you click change subscription it tells you you subscribed for a year, then one month, no verification, just charge, no answer to customer service. Cancel is small button at the bottom but they still charged me 13 months of service in 15 seconds with a cancel.

I've been using FitBod for a while now, but after over a year, it's become clear their "roadmap" for the Android version is just something they say whenever people ask why the iOS version has more features and updates, yet it's the Android version is same price. There's no real ETA on when Android will be on par with iOS from what I've seen, so I can no longer recommend this app.

Absolutely pathetic how this is a barebones version of the same iOS app. I bought a month of it (and regret it!) thinking it was the same thing as my wife's app. The selling point for me was the exertion rating, i.e the app learns how much weight you can lift and automatically adjusts your next workout depending on whether or not your workout was easy or hard. THIS DOES NOT EXIST ON ANDROID. Lots of other iOS features are also non-existent. Useless app now and a waste of money.

I love the app, recommends you weight amount to lift (I wish I could adjust them because I'm not that strong yet) logging work outs and rest periods are easy. The only issue is the $80.00 for a whole year. Gym memberships are already expensive to start with now 80 for the whole year. I just started working out and do not have that kind of money. $40.00 for the year I could do or $50.00 to buy the app no problem. You only get 3 workouts before it requires a membership, you can even get a full 5

App is great, prices are a scam. Was using the free trial and was excited to see that it had a very good price which was 60/year. I decided to wait for black Friday and possibly buy it with discount when I noticed that the price went up to 70$/y and with 20% discount which totalled around 55$. I couldn't believe that they increased the price to offer that "discount". Due to those circumstances I decided to give this app a 2nd try but nope. The price is now at 80$. Becareful of what you buy.

I have used this app on both iPhone & Android for the past 2 or 3 years. Switching from the iPhone last year was a disappointment as many features are missing on the Android side. Their customer service states the iPhone team had a two-year head start, but in the intervening time, the Android app has not progressed. I reached out to support 3 times in the past week via email, their website, and through the app - I haven't received a response as of yet. Save your money. Get an app with support. EDIT (1/31/22): Well folks. All I can say is I tried. I began trying to contact them 10 days ago because my subscription was about to expire and I needed some help. Here we are... 10 days later with no response. I assume they are out of business.

Classic bait-and-switch. Looks good, gets you hyped, makes one workout then slaps you with "unlock your next workout with a free trial!" insinuating it's a paid only app, but only after you give them all your information, then make it impossible to delete you account and remove info from their servers. This app wants nothing more than to sell your data and workouts that almost exactly match what a planet fitness membership gives you. You replied. how about put a button to delete in the app.

Missing a lot of features that I was expecting but looks like for some reason they started from scratch with android and it doesn't even have the same backend as the iOS app. Plate calculator is missing, it almost never increases weight, and you can't exclude excersises so I constantly have to find a sub for cleans I don't want to do. Definitely not complete enough to be worth 60 a year.

Can't get ahold of customer service. I've sent two contact requests in the last week to assist me in logging in to the account I created when I redeemed a promo code for a free month trial and they took my credit card information to do that free month trial so that way it converts to a paid subscription after the trial. I can't login to that account tied to that credit card. I am very frustrated that on the Android side of it there seems to be a complete lack of customer service to fix problems.

I initially liked this app and was paying for unlimited workouts month to month. However, today I went to workout and it told me i needed to subscribe to gain access to unlimited workouts. The app recognized I pay month to month but still would not let me continue. I have requested a refund and will no longer use this to track my progress.

I have completed 12 workouts at this point, and honestly I'm disappointed. The workouts seem completely random, and I get some variation of pull up almost every single night. I'm not able to see any progress because I'm always having to learn some new exercise. Then I'm not pushing myself as hard because I have no idea what weight is appropriate because the app always way under suggests for me.

Paid for the full year subscription after I was sold on the app by looking at the iOS version. This one isn't even close, and is honestly incomprehensible for someone new to working out, and based on what I've seen on iOS, it literally isn't an issue on that platform. This is missing critical functionality and there really is no indication of when there might be parity. Don't get this app until they post dates for features to be added.

This app is competent at doing two things: 1. An archive of exercises, most of which will match your equipment selections, but not all. If you tell it you have dumbells it will assume you have an incline bench and plates for some reason. 2. A way to track workouts and to some extent muscle use. Anything else this app tries to do it fails at spectacularly. Support (whom I have contacted) is incredibly unhelpful and not familiar with their own broken app. Looking forward to trying something else.

Lots of weird choices that neglect ease if use and quality of life. Also, the app disregards your current capabilities and doesn't seem to adjust around your current fitness levels. It is always underestimating my recover, weight lifting capabilities and the amount of work I could/should be doing. It would be one thing if it prefaced the workout as a deload routine, but it simply never factors what I have put in when customizing my weights and ability. Edit: It's even worse than I thought.

Creates workouts for you from a library of exercises, pretty okay, until they ask for your money. It would be fine if, when you paid, it worked. Instead it doesn't work anymore, asks for subscription again and has a long wait time for support (makes sense because probably everyone who was dumb enough, like me, to pay probably needs support.) Works for 3 workouts then don't bother, you're flushing your money down the toilet. Trying to get a refund.

I was told on a subreddit that this app was free and a good way to support exercise and just trying to set it up the first day has proven to be a pain. Immediately I'm shown that there is a free trial time and you will be billed right away after it ends and "you can cancel anytime," but none of the ways to get in to cancel are working and the website is even telling me my password is incorrect even after verifying email and resetting a password. Highly sus.

I was tricked into signing up and giving credit card info. To address your response; a free trial doesn't negate the fact that you were dishonest in how you tricked me into signing up. I was glad that once I realized I had been tricked into installing your app instead of the one I wanted, I was able to cancel the subscription. I first just uninstalled your app, the later realized I had to go into my account and remove the subscription also

The fact that there is no way to edit your profile or manage your subscription through the app is very deceptive practices. I was charged for a year's subscription when I didn't want it. I had to go through a browser to cancel my subscription, but it was already too late.

Unresponsive Support Team. I issued a ticket through my membership-linked email a week ago on the app asking them about the unsent weekly workout reports and my progress to which they never replied. I believe it is my right as a paying subscriber to have access to all available features. That was terribly disrespectful.

can't use the free trial without submitting payment info. wasted my time with setting up account and all my gym stuff to find out I can't check out the app first. I was under the impression that it was a free model with limited features and subscribe if you wanted more features.

Does that scam thing where it asks for all your information and gets you hyped up only to slap you in the face with a pay wall at the very end. A free trial doesn't make that better, it's just trying to get you to forget about it and accidentally give them money. Any health app trying to trick you into paying can't be trusted with your body. Even if this got an ad free version I'd not return, they've already lost all my trust.

I've been using this app for over 2 yrs and could not understand why I wasn't lifting heavier weights. Turns out it's because of you don't enter a specific number of RiR the app stagnates you at the weight previously lifted. It doesn't indicate AT ALL what number of RiR you need to enter when your ready to lift heavier. All I have done is lose strength and definition since using this app. I will not be renewing.

Unfortunately, the workouts I perform do not sync with Samsung Health - they do not show up as completed or logged workouts unless I manually add it OR if I just start a circuit workout on the Galaxy Ultra Watch itself. I've tried EVERY troubleshooting suggestion the staff has offered and nothing is working. never had these issues using an iPhone and apple watch but ever since switching to Samsung on 3/24/26, it does not work...

I've been using this app for two years and have recommended it to friends and family and my physio, all of whom have from time to time paid for a subscription. Deleted this app today for locking me out of content without my credit card information. Textbook enshittification.

why can't I see my workout log? I've been using this for years to record workouts and now I can't see my log. because a popup immediately comes down asking for payment that wasn't required a day ago, I have years of progress I can no longer see.

Used app (free version for 18 months) I am now greeted with a 7 day free trial upon launch, then billed yearly or monthly. There is no way to close or by pass this. Super predatory - Loved the app up till this point
Used this app for a long time, but now when I open it I am immediately met with a payment screen. I cant even see my previous workouts or progress. Nothing!
Be aware—after the trial ends, it automatically charges for a full annual subscription, not monthly, and there’s no clear warning. That’s a pretty shady way to handle billing.

Didn't get to use it. during Set up got to a screen which REQUIRED me to agree to a Monthly or Yearly plan with 7 day trial. Remind me to Cancel was even OFF by default. No free option. this is a typical bait and switch. If there's a free option, you should give me the option to at least try that out.

subscription only app 🥀 (after your response) thank you for responding to my review, but, I have zero money i am a literal minor, don't care about no damn free trial, I'll use a different app without a subscription, biggest issue was that I had to email to delete my account, without paying for subscription or getting free trial, I couldn't even get in the app to delete it.

looks like a good app but doesn't tell you the price until you've installed and signed up. For those who see this review before installing (as of April 2026) 7 day free trial then £92.99 a year or £15.49 a month
Not affordable for widows with kids. $95 a year ($7.99 a month) or $15.99 a month. It's DOUBLE PRICE if you can't throw money around? I still have to feed my kids.
SERIOUSLY!!! I come back to a new app and with it is gone are my saved workouts! I am so mad! Thinking it is time to unsubscribe, uninstall, and look for an app that doesn't disappoint!
Do not like these apps where I cannot try without a subscription or without putting my card details straight away. I do not know your product yet and you did not win my trust yet. I will uninstall.
The app has gotten almost unusable with all the AI. Features keep getting removed and the work out suggestions aren't as good as they used to be.
I thought this would be a actually "free" app where everything is free, but instead you have to pay monthly or yearly just to use this app, don't get.

really love the phone app on my Galaxy s26+ but the watch app on the wear OS system is terrible... on the Apple watch system you can adjust reps and weight and the rest timer is built in but that is not available on wear OS...?!

I didn't get to see the app It's blocked by a pay wall! I don't care how good your app is I'm not giving you my credit card number unless I see what i'm being offered, and I can verify you're not a scam. play protect doesn't check for everything. and it's suspicious that you immediately ask for a credit card. Before consumers can see what is being offered.

you can't look at anything without getting a subscription. yeah there's a 7 day free trial but if that's the case then just show me the app BEFORE I get a subscription that I could or could not forget about. Just makes it seem like the company wants you to forget it.

Been using this for awhile. Its super helpful to start with however lacks alot of bodyweight exercises or non-gym workouts. Also started tracking calories this year but this app doesn't sync calories to Health Connect nor Samsung Health. That one is very disappointing. probably wont renew unless fixed.

accidentally purchased a Fitbod subscription and realized the mistake almost immediately. I emailed their support right away explaining the situation, but they flatly refused to issue a refund, even though the subscription had not been used. This felt very unfair and customer-unfriendly. Mistakes happen, and good companies show flexibility—especially when contacted immediately. Instead, Fitbod hid behind rigid policies with no consideration for genuine cases. Overall, a very poor experience and

Can't stand when apps force you to give email and info before even telling you the app isn't free. Can't wait for all the spam I get after they sell my email address ugh. *edit* You're lying in your reply. If you didn't sell email addresses, you wouldn't ask for that BEFORE saying it's not free. Forcing a sign-up before telling cost is predatory.
Cancel subscription! This doesn't work for me and it's not giving me a clear notification for my cancellation!
there is supposed to be Ai where is it ? it have to be more fixable app more options for warm up and cool down more explaining for things

Not worth the money. I have been using this app for 8 months now. Started because I wanted variety and to keep my workouts fresh. I have tried a few different workout types and I feel like it is always the same work out, just a different sequence. You can uses Chatgpt or Gemini to get better workouts. Update; I tried the more variety as suggested. Once in a while I get a good workout with variety. I am still not impressed. cookie cutter workouts. I did what was asked, no real help.

Description says "in-app purchases" but I couldn't find a way to start using it without adding a credit card (with promise of 7 day free trial). Am I missing something? Shouldn't the description be "subscription required"?

Fantastic app for home gym goers. I was so tired of looking for apps that allow customized programs based on your available equipment. UPDATE: after months of using the app I found the algorithm got wonky and was hard to correct/use. I often had to manually build my own to correct the app each workout. Reached out to customer service to cancel my annual membership only to find out their 'cancel anytime' marketing does not provide for refund. They bury it in their terms of service. What a scam!

Deceptively not a free app. 3 workout logs before mandatory subscription required. This information and 'trial' limitation is not made clear upfront, I only found out through subtext on another page. In-app purchases should be supplementary add on payments, otherwise it's just a way to hide costs. I should not need to put in personal details prior to learning the real cost of your service. Remedy? Either make it paid upfront, or clearly identify the trial period to customers and the limitations.

Deceitful to paid members. I paid to get the most benefit of this apps but Android users basically second class to Fitbod. We don't have the same features as iPhone users for example weekly workout plan and save workout. Also Android users can't edit past log. Everytime I asked their customers service, the answer will be the same: just wait. It's been almost a year and there's nothing. Such a waste of money. Edit 05/29: it won't record the workout you choose (i.e. trap). Money grab apps!!

really disappointing that it makes you go through the act of setting the app up, including the time of day to send you a reminder, just to tell you you have to pay to use it. even if it has a free trial, i dont find that worth it.

The smart watch app is not good, that's why I gave you 1 star. I don't want to have to reach for my phone if I do more or less reps or do more or less weights. There should be a way I can change my reps or weight on my watch. I already paid for one month so I'm a use it but that's all y'all getting from me. I'm a try another app.

so far I havent even been able using it due to it trying to force me to pay for the subscription right off the bat. it wont allow me to click off and pops up the second I open it so my whole experience has just been that other than set up/making my account

Predatory billing practices. After downloading the free one week trial, the subscription automatically renewed into a yearly membership, resulting in a ~$130 charge. The charge wasn’t discovered until two weeks into the membership, the app was never used, and Google declined to issue a refund.

Subbed for a year. Found it has a good looking, w/ friendly UI. But, I did NOT renew. Algorithm unreliable. Often would drop entire muscle groups from PPL. The recommendations got weird and I kept having to change exercises and progress my own weights and reps. I found it most useful as a gym log. So I've now downloaded one of those instead. Much better... I manually xfered weights to my new log, has no export function in FB. Android parity never happened. Feel deceived...
my account was suddenly locked behind a paywall, i now cannot access workout plans without a subscription. this is just pure greed from fitbod

I don't recommend this app.. don't do the same mistake as I did, do you still have time for it. it's not accurate, you pay for year because you can't get refund after 10 days... so you need to keep it, if it was good/honest, they wouldn't need to do that, one time sign up only, that's why they charge that much in advance. You can't create your own workout yourself in advance since you can't see your training in advance. Untrusted weight/reps recommended by app.

Don't use the app. It charges a high amount without permission from an account connected to Google. It charged me 109 euros. Don't install it without my request to buy a subscription. I still could not get my money back to my account

Overall, it's a great app for appfreak who does not like to note my progress in notepad and like to have diverse trainings. My issues are: - No timer between exercises - I don't know why, but my automatically generated workout never contains secondary muscle group exercises, I have to add them manually. Update: There are a lot of small errors now I can not subtract equipment

glad I used the free trial in its entirety before making my decision. the first workout was amazing. the second and third workouts were terrible. the second one was way too easy and the third had things I simply can't do. for example (though not the only issue) I am not capable of doing push ups. my wrists cannot support that movement. I tried to replace the exercise and the only options were harder versions of push ups. I stopped the workout part way through because of that. disappointing.

For the most part a decent App. However, in recent weeks leg information is not being updated. It tracks recovery and let's you input and save. However, set progress is not receiving any information. I personally submitted a trouble ticket but have yet to receive any response. Frustrating when you have a paid subscription.

Updated: The app is missing very basic/ common equipment like chest fly machines. Is there a way to add equipment?still, there is no way to rate the difficulty of exercises for it to adjust future weights and reps. I thought this was supposed to learn and adjust your future workouts? I'm disappointed that the most recent updates to the app did nothing to bring the android app on par with the iPhone app.

Variety of workouts is good. Helpful for getting started if you don't know how to plan a workout or cycle through muscle groups. Unfortunately, difficulty doesn't feel right often. It gives workouts that are very easy even after multiple days of rest. There has also been a problem in the past few months with it including workouts with equipment that is turned off in settings. Quality has gone down since I started using this about 1.5 years ago.

I've already paid for my subscription and you want me to pay more?! I had a subscription for $60 that goes until July that I've already paid for and now the app is asking for $80 to even use it. This is garbage. Greedy fitness apps are going to kill themselves. I really did like this app and didn't mind the original subscription but the upcharge for "eLiTe" is outrageous and I can't even use the app anymore unless I pay more. So, I basically got ripped off.

Such a disappointing app. I purchased a year membership after initially enjoying the free trial. I've been using the app for several months at this point. It has good intentions, but is so glitchy and isn't very user friendly. When you curate an exercise from scratch, it will change/delete the exercises chosen half way through the workout. When you try and add the exercises back, it's not just a simple swap and is time consuming. It's so frustrating and is the main reason I hate this app.

Cool concept and fine when it works, but a very buggy app. The app has a tendency to change my workout in the middle of a set if I have "custom muscles" set. This requires me to go back and manually add the workout I want back in, and then it resets again. Eventually I got so frustrated I had to abandon the workout altogether. Should not have paid money for something so buggy and untested.

The app is alright. I do use it a lot to track my gym workouts, but I'm personally not a big fan of the UX. They try to be smart and set weights automatically, but in most cases it's wrong and I end up with more taps/clicks. I also don't like that there are a lot of exercises that are very similar instead of better grouping them, it should learn from my related exercises and suggest reasonable weights, but no... I end up needing to look up my training history manually.

Seems great at face value, but once you get to the workout, it feels completely overwhelming. I'm a starter. I can't do 5 sets of pull-ups, and I can't alter the amount. This makes it very hard ti track my progress. Plus some of the workouts are just not adequate for a beginner level. A deadlift was shown and with too much weight, it threw out my back. Not to mention the UI during the workout is confusing to navigate. Definitely could take notes from Leap Fitness. I'll go there instead

Good for where it is thus far. Needs same features as iOS. Like that it builds routines for you and that you can add what equip you already have. Needs the ability to say what weights you have, but how many. would also like the ability for the program to show.me what weights I need to add to my bar so I don't have to think. Lastly, need to be able save workouts you've completed for future workouts. Update: Nearly 9 months later and still can't save past workouts. Discontinuing my membership.

At $10 a month or $60 a year it's overpriced. There are features missing (at least on Android), like a plate calculator. That's not considering features a premium app should have (any sort of data analysis or metrics). It isn't clear that it actually helps you progress. There isn't a place to set goals, or track progress. It's buggy. If you select "body weight only" it still gives weighted exercises. There are issues with pullups in particular (it's given me over 90 before). Nice variety though.

I just don't have any faith that the AI knows what it's doing. It seems to select exercises at random from whatever target muscle it wishes to do at that time and throws them together in nonsensical ways to construct a workout. It gives a lot of variety, but I'd even argue too much variety at many times. Also it is deeply disappointing how far behind the android app is from the apple one, and we don't even get a price break given that we don't have so many features.

It's solid on a basic level but the version I'm paying for really should be free. It's fine for building my own exercises and supersets/circuits (finally) but it's got new annoying glitches such as locking me out of logging a workout in supersets, as in when I click the second workout to log it, it just brings me back to my first so I gotta readd it after every superset. Honestly, it really needs the ability to save workouts, along with cardio, warm-ups, and cool-downs. Plus bug fixes.

I'm currently 68 workouts in and overall really like the app. I've noticed several improvements since I joined. The only thing I'd really like added would be drop sets. Update. I've now been using the app for over a year and the last few months it's gone strait to 🗑. The excersise, weight and reps selection don't make any sense. You have to change almost every setting every workout. It'll also either freeze, restart or change your workout entirely mid workout. I've Uninstalled this app.

It's a good app. You can pick what you need to work out and what equipment is available but it's not free with in app purchases. You can only work out 3 times before being forced to pay to use. It should just come with a price tag right off the bat. I wanted an app that focuses on weights more than general exercise but FitOn is completely free to use unless you want extra things like meals or whatever. Then you'd pay their sub.

Downgrading from 3 to 2. The workout splits that were set in my gym are never followed anymore. I keep getting the same recommendations. Something seems to be off. Add that there have been no improvements since I've had the app. They seem to be moving backwards with functionality while others are moving forward. Used to recommend this app to everyone, but now I'm going back on that.

I've used fitbod for a few months now. at first, it was good. not anymore. here are the main issues: 1. rather than adding weight to an exercise, it will seemingly limitlessly increase reps instead. this has led to workouts like 5 sets of 36 reps of air squats, for example. 2. a few weeks ago, barbell exercises broke completely. the app no longer recommends anything over 45 lbs despite me having an extensive history of lifting more weight. I have to manually adjust every workout.

The app works well, though was a bit buggy as I encountered an issue where an exercise did not start losing until I went back to my main workout and clicked on it again. What is a showstopper for me is the lack of integrations with Google Fit and/or Strava. I will revisit the app once the integrations are implemented. Update 01/06/2022 -- More than one year later, new UI, yet still no Strava integration. This is needed so I can sync my workout across other services, using Strava as a bridge.

I think the idea for this app is really good. I love the video examples and the massive exercise library. My main problem is not being able to save your custom workouts. That is a huge deal breaker for me and, I think, a fatal flaw in the system. I would have considered paying the membership if it weren't for this lack of a feature. Such a cool idea that forgot the basics.

The lack of adequate loop band exercise support makes this app difficult to use. I don't always have access to free weights. And I don't want to just rely on body weight exercises as I have invested in bands during the pandemic. There's a setting to adjust band level, but barely any band exercises. I don't understand... Am I missing something here?

Hmmm... really liked the app, especially the circuit workouts and overall premade plans.. but I also have JeFit. With only being able to "try" this for 3!!! Days... I honestly can't tell, at this point, how it would be worth the fee?? I get most all of this, more exercise (mainly machines) choices i think, from JeFit FREE version???? I'd recommend longer trials if you have more to offer!

This app has the promise of making things intuitive and personalized. But I cannot fathom why it is so glitchy. You change an unrelated setting (like the rest timer) in the middle of an ongoing workout, and the app will change the entire workout. It doesn't even remember your equipment settings. Every day it spits out a workout that's entirely based on body weights. And you have to unselect body weight only setting.

No android integration even though it's been promised for years, even strava would be nice. Starting weights don't make sense, as if the app was designed by people who don't lift. Example, 160 lbs for bench press. (160-45)/2=57.5 per side. Not really normal split. 55 per side would be more reasonable. It would help if you could do math in the weight field to calculate this. Barbell exercises should separate bar weight and weight per side, it would greatly simplify each exercise.

After using it for a couple months there are several missing features that make it difficult to justify the cost. First, they dont disclose that you can't move back and forth between Android and iOS (don't link me the parity update. It is 2021, this should have never been developed as isolated systems.) No saved workouts, inability to exclude certain exercises (rage at being suggested combinations of supermans every workout), and ability to edit past workouts are all missing.

The positive: you can create different gyms like big fitness gym with all the gear and home gym with almost nothing. It's also easy to swap exercises. The bad: the Android version is a totally different experience from the iPhone, I was going to rate 3 stars but they get 2 because of that. The promises to update Android to be equivalent go back 2 years, so I'm calling bs. You get 3 workouts before paying, that's not enough. I'm on week 6, has it confused me with someone else?

Unless you're going to pull the trigger on the subscription, don't waste your time. This looks like it could be a good app. With a trial period of three workouts, though, it is hard to tell. The subscription price of $12.99 a month is absurd for an app though. I have a subscription to Photoshop. A program so versatile and complex no one knows how to use all the features and it cost only 10.99 a month. This is dumb pricing. At half the cost they would get more than twice the takers. A third better yet. If you want $12.99 a month, you're going to have to get me addicted and three workouts isn't even a taste.

Great for generating workouts and that's about it. No way to log individual exercises, or when a set is complete, so tracking is awful unless you're doing a full workout. The equipment tracking doesn't work correctly and recommends exercises that aren't viable. No way to indicate that they prefer low-impact exercises when injured so I have to manually reject every exercise, but can't easily add them back when I feel up to it. Massive number of improvements need to be made tbh.

The pros: organization is great and I love that it keeps track of muscle recovery. It tracks your workout and makes you rest which is super helpful. It was a big upgrade from my paper/pen. The cons: it thinks an intermediate level female can do 4 different kind of pull-ups and gives me the same workout as my husband, resulting in me having to replace exercises for over 50% of my work out. i wish I didn't pay for it but I mean if you're capable of doing all the options it might work out.

This is a great product for working out. However, they have very poor customer service. I was double charged for their app one being for $64.99 and another for $86.99. I still do not have access to the workouts. They do not have a working phone number you can call to reach support, so I sent in a support ticket. Here I am three days later with no resolution to my issue and almost $200 poorer in the bank.

Mediocre workout tracker, poor workout planner. I previously used a free workout tracker, which I note had greater flexibility than this app (such as the ability to add missing exercises). The recommended workouts are unbalanced, sometimes neglecting one of the scheduled muscle groups and picking multiple exercise variations which all hit the same muscle in the same way. Eg all front delt, no side or rear delt, or all biceps and no brachialis, etc. The constant emails are a bit much also. 👎

Bought the subscription based on the recommendation from a friend with an apple device just to find out that android doesnt have most of those features (strava, google fit, multiple gym profiles, warm up and cool down exercises, etc). My annual plan says $79.99 whereas apple is getting more features for only $59.99 a month. Would rate higher if these features were added. Still not happy im getting charged more for far fewer services

This app has faulty algorithms. It combines supersets that are not. Example. It'll give you a superset with 3 workouts but only two are actually the superset and the one is just by itself. It will really mess up your workout. Plus the reps/weights are erratic. It wanted me to do 10 chin ups and then 26 pull ups in a superset for 4 sets. For the price to pay it should be a lot better but they get you with the first workouts because they are ok.

This app is great on iOS but is lacking almost all the functionality that makes it worth paying for. The app will give you a custom workout routine (no cardio or warmup) but that's about it. You can't have multiple gym locations. Doesn't work with smart watches. It doesn't even have a built-in timer for exercises that are timed. I don't know how they can charge the same as the iOS app but with maybe half the functionality. I'll update this once an update happens but right now I'm disappointed.

Comparing the iOS and Android versions, the Android version is inferior in many significant ways. The android version ignores my selected muscle groups(it'll give me exercises with muscle groups I deselected), it recommends workouts with equipment I don't have selected, the UI is basic and has less features/customizations. For example, there's no way to select which dumbbell weights you have on android, so you'll be suggested odd weights like 17.5 pounds. Please fix so I can stop using my iPad!

Great idea but poor execution and way overpriced. The App looks great and is easy to setup. Being able to pick all workouts for each session is fantastic but once you begin your actual workout the App loses all of it's charm. It essentially becomes a glitch heavy digital log book. The App does nothing during the workouts, you just manually log each set. Nice idea but I did not find it useful at all. I'm not interested in spending more time interacting with the App than working out.

the first week was good I got suckered into buy the year long subscription, don't do it. I get the apps in beta but the type of workout doesn't change. it doesn't think about how after doing the third exercise on that muscle that day you might be fatigued. it's version of toning is 8 reps at your Max weight for every exercise. I've tried talking to customer support and I got ignored that told the ticket has been resolved. I've had duplicated exercises in one day. it's projected max is off

Great workout app and easy to use for iOS ONLY. Recently switched to an android phone and the app suffers tremendously. Range of workout replacements and excercises are limited with no search engine. Picking or removing muscle groups to work on in a workout adds or removes some picked choices. The app worked great on iOS. It's subpar on Android. I like to think they'll fix these issues soon or I'll have to resort to another app.

The fact you can't create a custom workout by selecting different body parts is extremely annoying. Try to select biceps, triceps, and forearms and it gives me suggestions for back, legs, biceps and triceps saying it can't give exercises based on my original selection. Won't be renewing this when my subscription is over.

Your subscription gets you HALF the functionality available for iOS users, at the exact same price. Great app at first, but once you start needing to manually edit all your workouts because it doesn't know what weights you own, you'll start to realize you're doing all the planning work yourself and the app becomes a glorified tracker. If you're looking for a fitness app to help you in the long term, look elsewhere until they get their act together with their Android offering.

This app had so much potential when I first started using it. I switched over from the iOS app to the Android app a couple years back - and the Android version is severely lacking. I can't even choose the severity of workouts (easy, difficult, etc.) like I could on iOS - but 2-3 years later and still missing on Android. I have to assume this is playing a part in the inconsistency in weight recommendations. Needless to say, I will be cancelling once my subscription is up and looking elsewhere.

I've been paying for this app, and now I'm thinking about switching. There are a lot of ab machines at my local planet fitness that are not listed in the app! I've submitted online feedback forms to have them added and did not get a response. At this point, it's affecting my workouts. If I do not hear back, this will be my last month before canceling.

I like the idea of the app and the interface is clean, but the programming isn’t very smart, especially when you set it to strength. After doing a heavy compound like bench press, it immediately throws in tricep pushdowns and then supersets them with push-ups. That’s just smashing the exact same muscles again with no recovery. It tanks performance and doesn’t really line up with how strength training should be structured. you're supposed to not think while using this app but you have to think.

seems to be a good app but the pay wall block is a huge issue. £15 a month just to get started after inputting all your details is just wrong. ok you get a 7 day free trial but it's rather expensive after that for an app you can get free elsewhere

The app has potential, but far too many features simply don’t work as advertised. A major issue is the workout duration limit: anything longer than 1 hour 30 minutes is ignored, and the app automatically caps the session at 90 minutes. It’s frustrating because it pretends to let you customise the workout length, but the number is basically fake. For anyone who trains longer sessions, this makes the app feel incomplete and poorly thought out.

phone app is great but the watch app... not so much. I'd love to be able to change reps and weights on the watch, and also see the rest timer. defeats the purpose of a watch app if you have to have your phone with you.

I used a trial I was sent. now that it's over I want to compare it to other apps but I can't because the app lacks the ability to see your past training data, or anything about anything unless you pay money first. all of my information is blocked behind a paywall now.
too many bad AI bot apps. asking for money before showing what it can do is not worth the risk. I've been burned by too many fake apps to spend up front.

its alright but its missing common machines and it doesnt sync calories to health connect. if you add a custom workout the app wont automatically add it into your workout rotation. im probably not going to renew since its basically just a list of exercises you can get off other apps or websites.
not even able to experience the app due to me not being able to close the free 7 day plan, i literally can not get into the app bc of thst, please fix

I think it's a cool concept but needs a lot of work. Especially when it comes to workout timing. I would have hoped that an AI app adapts to the time it takes for an exercise. I tried selecting 40 minutes, the workouts were 25-30. So I tried selecting 1 hour and my workout was 30 minutes. The point of an "AI" app is that it plans for me, but since the timing is always off, I need to go through and add exercises to make the timing work.

I used to use this app on iOS & would consider it 5 stars on an iPhone. However since moving to an Android I'm so disappointed in the app. On Android I'm: 1. Unable to put the level of effort required in an exercise. 2. Unable to build a circuit or superset during a workout 3. Cannot see the streak of how many consistent weeks I've been working out. 4. Can't add/sync with Samsung Health / Google fit 5. Cannot share the workout as a screenshot Would add more but don't have enough characters

It seemed like a really good app at the beginning, but after a few months of use I'd say it's not worth the price they're asking. The exercises become repetitive and some of the instructions unclear (eg. the other day it told me to somehow add 8kg to bycicles crunches). Plus all of the missing features that so many others are commenting on. I regret subscribing for a full year, likely won't renew.

Good app for generating workouts. However it's not managing the weight over time. I'm having to go through my history and take a guess at what weight I should be lifting, or loading up the exercise until it feels like a workout. I understand this may be due to functionality missing from the Apple version. If this can be fixed I'll update to 5*.
won't let me increase length of workout, and support only said I could manually add exercises. if I wanted to do that I wouldn't need the app. and it no longer connects to myfitnesspal

It seemed good at the start but after months of use I am disappointed. The algorithm seems terrible and wants me to do certain exercises every time and telling it to recommend it less (or more) doesn't seem to do anything. It also seems like the more you do a particular exercise the more reps it expects so now they are completely unbalanced with a workout wanting me to do 24 reps of leg raises but 2000 calf raises. I can change it all manually of course but then what am I paying the app to do?
To expensive. There are several other options out there for a quarter of the cost or free.

I've had this app for 6 months or so. Honestly it was amazing. The last 3-4 weeks it has been super glitchy - exercises are changed either before I get to the gym or mid-workout with exercises either deleted or swapped out for something completely different. It is SO FRUSTRATING. I contacted support, they pretty much said 'we know there's a problem, nothing we can do right now'. That is poor customer service. Updates haven't helped. I've cancelled my membership until this is sorted out.

I am a paying customer. Reason is, this an excellent app. But it does have one serious bug. And if you guys don't fix it, I am going to give you one star. Anyway, if I manually set how sore my muscles are, fitbod dose not update recovery figure image, and what's more troubling is that your algorithm does not take that into account when it calculates next workout.

The android version is not worth the subscription, it's second class compared to iOS version. Simple basic stuff like having access to your exercise data is non-existent, you can't use health connect nor even the most rudimentary csv export. The support team always promise a few weeks away with no clear answer. For your reply: 1. notice I mentioned export, it's my data I should be able to get it out. 2. I'll edit my review when the health connect is out this summer 2024 as promised.
I don't like how it only give me one work out A DAY and it was only 15 push ups and I then switched the difficulty to advance and not its only 21 push ups

Edit: was halfway in a workout, added a machine to "equipment" and it bugged out the whole workout. Nothing logged, and a new workout showed up with different exercises. Ruined my whole workout. Terrible. Edit2: also no seeming way to remove sets after accidentally adding one. Maybe add more gym equipment? Also, the replacement exercises seem kinda off sometimes. Maybe some readjustment needed? Also more workout presets would be nice, like PPL etc.

Has the potential to be a 5 star. Concept is really good and if it had the features the iPhone app had it would be 4/5 straight away. The price is a joke considering the app isn't upto date with its apple counterpart, and there's no set dates on when some of the features will actually be completed by. Customer service aim to get back to you in 3 days... haven't heard back in over a week. Conclusion- paying for a app that isn't anywhere close to complete with no set dates for completion.

Paywall on the third workout (not even day three). Don't even get a week to trial if this suits me. UI is unclear: no clear distinction between preview and workout screens; buttons are small and navigation is difficult with sweaty hands; many icons with no clear meaning; no voice guidance; no clear indication on how long to work or rest. I'm sure it does it's thing well with the AI but there are far better, more beautiful, clearer apps out there for exercise. 2.5 if I was allowed half stars.

I'd love to give a 5 star rating to the Android version of Fitbod Elite but I'm giving it 2 for now. I like the interface, the exercise library, and the list of equipment available, although I would love to see resistance bands and cardio added to it. It would be a fantastic app if workouts could be planned, scheduled, looked at, or generated beyond the current day. I paid $14.99 CDN to test it out until September 20; I will not renew if the workout planning issue is not addressed before then.

I'm changing my review, as previously 5 stars. While it's easy to use has great features...it appears they deleted the option to save your workout to repeat at a later time?!?! Why? It takes so much time to load your created workout to not be able to save what you created?!? For a paying $10/month, this feature should be basic. Highly disappointed this feature was taken away. I might as well use the paper my trainer gave me. Considering canceling.

Edit: They've been saying "The iOS app has a 2 year head start, we plan to match features" since June 2020. I can't believe they charge the same amount for such a worse app. Biggest feature request that effects my every workout: no exclusion of exercise. The replacement I want isn't always in the replacement section so it usually involves deleting the exercise and having to search for a replacement in the (usually buggy) search and dragging it to the right place in the workout.

I switched back to android after an iPhone and was beyond disappointed when I downloaded this app. I had been using Fitbod for 2 yrs and the droid version is missing so many basic features that were available on Apple when I first downloaded the app in 2019 (plate calculator, and a search function when replacing a workout). Plus, none of my progress shifted over so that's two years out the door. I won't be paying again until this app is actually working.

Look the app looks great, functions well and has plenty of options. I would highly recommend it to a novice or slightly experienced person in the gym. For anyone who has been serious about working out for over 3 years, this is not for you. The super sets rarely make sense, pairing the same muscle group for a super set when the focus is strength training doesn't make sense. The Android app is far behind what was advertised to me on the iOS. I payed for the year but am not greatly regretting it.

Ok but missing some basics. I'm using for bodyweight only routines. Not much variety but can't fully blame the app for my lack of equipment. The most annoying issues seem simple. Exercises that are timed and not reps don't have a timer. Just says "do this for 30 seconds" but doesn't give a timer. When swapping out an exercise in a routine, it doesn't offer up new ones, just has repeats of other stuff already in the routine. Wish I hadn't gotten a year subscription so I could try other apps.

Completed 2 workouts, and both times the app didn't log anything. I had to edit the workout because my gym didn't have the equipment the app recommended, and it reset the whole workout. Now it says I only have a few workouts left before I need to pay. The app looks nice and seems to have great features, but if the basic logging feature doesn't work reliably and now there's a mandatory fee that was never disclosed until after 2 uses, I'm not interested

I love the app. I just want to add a few things to it. If there is an option to never recommend an exercise, then there should be an option to always recommend an exercise. Second, I sometimes accidentally delete a set, there should be an option to undo that, same thing when replacing an exercise. Lastly, why can I access previous workout while I'm in the middle of a workout?

Works great, but functionality on the iPhone is greater like being able to rate your sets, share a workout and have it logged. Also disappointed it won't generate other exercises if the exact piece of equipment isn't listed in the gym as it could be modified. Also, why can I not select a former workout I've completed to copy?? Android functions aren't worth a repeat subscription. ✌️ ***UPDATE - Most recent update does have the multiple gym option so removed from review.

It's a great way to stay motivated and in shape however if there was an automated way where you just start the exercise and it would make the reps for you with audio guidance, but instead I'd have to look into my phone after every single workout. Moreover, being able to sync with Samsung Health would make it the best! Edit: I have tried this app for two months and I can say it's not worth it. I have also tried wearOS and it doesn't make any difference, I keep going back to the phone version.

For experienced people, who don't need the app. Sprained my ankle trying too advanced moves. I got this to go back to the gym where I'm familiar with most workouts but I'm not overly strong. I am a woman and the weights and reps they recommended were for people much more advanced and I sprained my ankle trying something that it did not give me proper form on. Would only recommend to pros but they likely don't need the app lol

I am so disappointed that all of the content that makes this app amazing is locked behind an extremely high paywall. The only reason I'm leaving 2 stars instead of 1 is because of the detailed workouts that are provided. Be aware that there is nothing prior to signing up that tells you it is only a paid version of the app. Don't get too comfortable, because they will lock you out of everything.
